papers.title: A Neural Latent Dynamics Approach for Solving Inverse Problems in Cardiac Electrophysiology
papers.summary: Solving inverse problems in cardiac electrophysiology consists in the recovery of physiological parameters from surface electrocardiogram (ECG) measurements, a task which is often computationally unfeasible due to the severe ill-posedness and the prohibitive computational complexity of PDE-constrained optimization. In this work, we introduce a data-driven framework leveraging Latent Dynamics Networks (LDNets) to construct efficient surrogate models of the forward operator. By mapping low-dimensional parameters, representing ectopic activation sites or ischemic region descriptors, to the ECG signals via latent dynamics governed by neural ordinary differential equations, our approach circumvents the computational burden of evaluating high-fidelity cardiac models during iterative parameter estimation. The surrogate is trained offline on high-fidelity data, enabling rapid and robust inversion. We validate the proposed framework through rigorous numerical experiments with synthetic data across both 2d and 3d geometries. Results show that the LDNet-based surrogate achieves precise reconstruction of cardiac parameters while drastically reducing computational overhead, thereby enabling near real-time clinical applications.

ai.tldr: 提出基于潜变量动力学网络（LDNets）的代理模型，用于高效求解心脏电生理逆问题，从体表心电图恢复生理参数。
ai.motivation: 心脏电生理逆问题严重病态且计算复杂，传统PDE约束优化难以实时应用。
ai.method: 利用LDNets构建正向算子的代理模型，通过神经ODE在潜变量空间映射低维参数到ECG信号，离线训练后实现快速逆推。
ai.result: 在2D和3D合成数据实验中，LDNet代理模型精确重建心脏参数，大幅降低计算开销，接近实时临床可行性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种数据驱动的逆问题求解框架，可加速心脏参数估计，适用于临床实时诊断。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理信息（如心脏电生理方程）约束潜变量动力学，可能提升外推能力和数据效率。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型依赖训练数据分布，对未见病理或几何变异的泛化能力需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将神经ODE与潜变量建模结合，为复杂物理系统提供可微分、可逆的代理模型，启发其他科学计算领域。

en.tldr: A data-driven framework using Latent Dynamics Networks (LDNets) to build efficient surrogate models for solving inverse problems in cardiac electrophysiology, recovering physiological parameters from ECG signals.
en.motivation: Inverse problems in cardiac electrophysiology are severely ill-posed and computationally prohibitive for PDE-constrained optimization, hindering real-time clinical use.
en.method: LDNets map low-dimensional parameters (e.g., ectopic activation sites, ischemic region descriptors) to ECG signals via latent dynamics governed by neural ODEs, trained offline on high-fidelity data.
en.result: Numerical experiments on 2D and 3D synthetic data show precise reconstruction of cardiac parameters with drastically reduced computational cost, enabling near real-time applications.
en.help_to_user: Provides a fast, data-driven inversion framework for cardiac parameter estimation, potentially enabling real-time clinical diagnosis.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporating physical constraints (e.g., cardiac electrophysiology equations) into latent dynamics may improve extrapolation and data efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Surrogate performance depends on training data distribution; generalization to unseen pathologies or geometries needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining neural ODEs with latent variable modeling offers a differentiable, invertible surrogate for complex physical systems, inspiring other scientific computing domains.

papers.title: Composition-Weighted Symbolic Regression for General-Purpose Property Prediction
papers.summary: We introduce a composition-weighted symbolic regression framework for interpretable prediction of materials properties directly from chemical composition. The method jointly learns analytical functional forms and task-dependent elemental weightings without predefined descriptors. By incorporating max/min operators, it naturally enforces constraints such as non-negative band gaps and bounded classification probabilities, unifying regression and classification tasks. Efficient search is achieved through a hybrid Monte Carlo tree search--genetic programming algorithm with gradient-based refinement and parallel computation. Benchmarks on MatBench tasks show competitive accuracy relative to state-of-the-art black-box models while yielding explicit analytical expressions. Applied to III--V semiconductor alloys, the model produces smooth composition-dependent trends and learned elemental weights with chemically meaningful periodic behavior. This framework provides a scalable and interpretable route for materials discovery and property screening.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于成分加权的符号回归框架，可直接从化学组成预测材料性质，同时保持可解释性。
ai.motivation: 现有黑箱模型预测准确但缺乏可解释性，而传统符号回归难以处理成分依赖关系。
ai.method: 联合学习解析函数形式和任务相关的元素权重，通过最大/最小算子自然约束物理条件，采用混合蒙特卡洛树搜索-遗传编程算法高效搜索。
ai.result: 在MatBench任务上达到与黑箱模型相当的精度，并给出显式解析表达式；在III-V半导体合金上展示出平滑的成分依赖趋势和化学上有意义的元素权重。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种可解释的材料性质预测方法，帮助用户理解成分-性质关系并加速材料筛选。

idea_spark.idea: 将成分加权符号回归与主动学习结合，自动选择最具信息量的成分进行实验验证。
idea_spark.risk: 对于高维成分空间，搜索复杂度可能急剧增加，需要更高效的搜索策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受材料基因组计划启发，将可解释机器学习融入材料发现流程，平衡精度与可解释性。

en.tldr: A composition-weighted symbolic regression framework is introduced for interpretable materials property prediction directly from chemical composition.
en.motivation: Black-box models offer high accuracy but lack interpretability, while traditional symbolic regression struggles with composition-dependent relationships.
en.method: Jointly learns analytical functional forms and task-dependent elemental weights, enforces physical constraints via max/min operators, and uses a hybrid Monte Carlo tree search-genetic programming algorithm for efficient search.
en.result: Achieves competitive accuracy on MatBench tasks compared to black-box models while yielding explicit analytical expressions; shows smooth composition-dependent trends and chemically meaningful elemental weights for III-V semiconductor alloys.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interpretable route for materials property prediction, aiding understanding of composition-property relationships and accelerating materials screening.

idea_spark.idea: Combine composition-weighted symbolic regression with active learning to automatically select the most informative compositions for experimental validation.
idea_spark.risk: Search complexity may increase dramatically for high-dimensional composition spaces, requiring more efficient search strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the Materials Genome Initiative, integrating interpretable machine learning into materials discovery to balance accuracy and interpretability.

papers.title: Variational Matrix-Learning Fourier Networks for Parametric Multiphysics Surrogates
papers.summary: Multiphysics simulation is critical for system-technology co-optimization (STCO) in chiplet-based design, but repeated finite-element solutions of PDE-governed problems are computationally expensive in parametric design exploration. This paper proposes a variational matrix-learning Fourier network (VMLFN) for efficient parametric multiphysics surrogate modeling. VMLFN constructs a log-space sine neural representation with randomly sampled spectral frequencies, frequency-dependent decay regulation, and embedded Dirichlet boundary conditions. With fixed hidden-layer parameters, the output-layer weights are determined by reformulating the governing PDEs into variational weak forms and enforcing the stationarity condition of the resulting energy functional. This converts physics-informed training into a linear matrix-solving problem, requiring only first-order derivatives and avoiding both high-order automatic differentiation and penalty-coefficient tuning. A heuristic frequency-scanning algorithm is further introduced to select a problem-adaptive maximum frequency that covers the dominant spectral range of the target problem. The proposed method is validated on heat conduction, solid mechanics, and Helmholtz wave propagation problems. Results from five benchmark cases demonstrate that VMLFN delivers accurate full-field predictions with substantial speedup over conventional physics-informed neural networks and repeated finite-element simulations.

ai.tldr: 提出变分矩阵学习傅里叶网络，用于高效参数化多物理场代理建模。
ai.motivation: 芯片设计中的多物理场仿真计算成本高，传统有限元方法在参数探索中效率低。
ai.method: 构建对数空间正弦神经表示，结合随机谱频率、频率衰减正则和狄利克雷边界条件；通过变分弱形式将物理信息训练转化为线性矩阵求解问题。
ai.result: 在热传导、固体力学和亥姆霍兹波传播五个基准案例中，实现准确全场预测，速度显著优于传统物理信息神经网络和有限元仿真。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速进行参数化多物理场代理建模，大幅降低计算成本。

idea_spark.idea: 利用频率扫描算法自适应选择最大频率，提升模型对问题谱范围的覆盖能力。
idea_spark.risk: 对于非线性或强耦合多物理场问题，线性矩阵求解假设可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理信息训练转化为线性问题，避免高阶自动微分和惩罚系数调优，启发更高效的物理信息学习方法。

en.tldr: Proposes a variational matrix-learning Fourier network for efficient parametric multiphysics surrogate modeling.
en.motivation: Multiphysics simulation in chiplet design is computationally expensive; traditional finite-element methods are inefficient for parametric exploration.
en.method: Constructs a log-space sine neural representation with random spectral frequencies, frequency-dependent decay, and embedded Dirichlet boundary conditions; converts physics-informed training into a linear matrix-solving problem via variational weak forms.
en.result: On five benchmarks (heat conduction, solid mechanics, Helmholtz wave propagation), achieves accurate full-field predictions with substantial speedup over conventional PINNs and repeated FEM.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast parametric multiphysics surrogate modeling, drastically reducing computational cost.

idea_spark.idea: A heuristic frequency-scanning algorithm adaptively selects the maximum frequency to cover the dominant spectral range of the problem.
idea_spark.risk: For nonlinear or strongly coupled multiphysics problems, the linear matrix-solving assumption may break down.
idea_spark.inspiration: Converting physics-informed training into a linear problem avoids high-order automatic differentiation and penalty tuning, inspiring more efficient physics-informed learning methods.

papers.title: Deep Variational Inference Symbolic Regression
papers.summary: Symbolic regression discovers explicit, interpretable equations without assuming a functional form in advance. A Bayesian approach strengthens this through probability distributions over candidate expressions, thus quantifying uncertainty in the presence of noisy and limited data. Deep Symbolic Regression (DSR) uses a neural network to generate symbolic expressions, but it is designed to identify a single best-fitting expression rather than infer a posterior distribution over models. We introduce Deep Variational Inference Symbolic Regression (DVISR), a variational Bayesian extension of DSR. DVISR replaces the original reward with the integrand of the evidence lower bound. It also extends the network architecture to output distributions over constants within expressions, enabling posterior inference over both expression trees and their associated constants. We show that DVISR can recover the true posterior in simple settings, both with and without constant tokens, and we examine how its performance changes as the size of the expression space increases. These results position DVISR as a step toward scalable Bayesian symbolic regression with uncertainty over full symbolic models.

ai.tldr: 提出深度变分推断符号回归（DVISR），将贝叶斯变分推断引入深度符号回归，实现对表达式树和常数的后验推断。
ai.motivation: 现有深度符号回归（DSR）只能找到单个最佳表达式，无法提供模型不确定性估计，在噪声和有限数据下可靠性不足。
ai.method: 用证据下界（ELBO）的积分替换原始奖励，并扩展网络架构以输出表达式内常数的分布，从而同时推断表达式树和常数的后验。
ai.result: 在简单设置下能恢复真实后验，并分析了表达式空间增大时性能的变化，向可扩展的贝叶斯符号回归迈进一步。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从数据中发现可解释的数学公式，同时量化模型不确定性，适用于科学发现和数据分析。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图神经网络与变分自编码器，对表达式树进行更灵活的隐空间建模。
idea_spark.risk: 变分后验可能过于简单，无法捕捉多模态分布；大规模表达式空间下计算成本高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将ELBO积分作为奖励的设计启发我们重新思考强化学习中的目标函数设计。

zh.motivation: 现有深度符号回归（DSR）只能找到单个最佳表达式，无法提供模型不确定性估计，在噪声和有限数据下可靠性不足。

idea_spark.risk: 变分后验可能过于简单，无法捕捉多模态分布；大规模表达式空间下计算成本高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将ELBO积分作为奖励的设计启发我们重新思考强化学习中的目标函数设计。

en.tldr: Proposes Deep Variational Inference Symbolic Regression (DVISR), a variational Bayesian extension of DSR that enables posterior inference over both expression trees and constants.
en.motivation: DSR finds only a single best expression without uncertainty quantification, which is unreliable under noisy and limited data.
en.method: Replaces the original reward with the integrand of the ELBO and extends the network to output distributions over constants, enabling joint posterior inference.
en.result: Recovers the true posterior in simple settings and analyzes performance as expression space grows, advancing scalable Bayesian symbolic regression.
en.help_to_user: Helps users discover interpretable equations from data while quantifying model uncertainty, useful for scientific discovery and data analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Combine graph neural networks with variational autoencoders for more flexible latent space modeling of expression trees.
idea_spark.risk: Variational posteriors may be too simplistic to capture multimodal distributions; high computational cost in large expression spaces.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using the ELBO integrand as reward inspires rethinking objective design in reinforcement learning.

papers.title: ORBIT: Learning Gene Program Co-Activation Structure for Cell-Type-Stratified Pathway Rewiring Analysis in Single-Cell Transcriptomics
papers.summary: Gene programs co-activate within cells, but existing single-cell methods either treat programs independently or require experimental perturbation data to model their interactions. We introduce ORBIT, a self-supervised transformer that learns asymmetric dependencies among gene programs from observational single-cell RNA-sequencing data alone, quantifying how strongly each program influences every other program. The key mechanism is an intervention-consistent training objective: the model learns each program's directional influence on every other program by predicting how the others change when that program is removed, yielding attention weights that reflect asymmetric influence rather than symmetric co-occurrence. Applied to 191,890 prefrontal cortex nuclei across three pathway vocabularies, ORBIT recovers co-activation structure consistent with established Alzheimer's disease vulnerability signatures, identifies cell-type-specific rewiring invisible to differential expression, and achieves 0.984 macro F1 on cell-type classification from 220 pathway scores, which is within 0.3 points of a state-of-the-art classifier using all 22,088 genes.

ai.tldr: 提出ORBIT，一种自监督Transformer，仅从观测的单细胞RNA测序数据中学习基因程序之间的非对称依赖关系，无需实验扰动数据。
ai.motivation: 现有单细胞方法要么独立处理基因程序，要么需要实验扰动数据来建模程序间相互作用，缺乏从观测数据中学习程序间定向影响的方法。
ai.method: 采用干预一致训练目标，通过预测移除一个程序后其他程序的变化，学习每个程序对其他程序的定向影响，注意力权重反映非对称影响而非对称共现。
ai.result: 在191,890个前额叶皮层细胞核上，ORBIT恢复了与阿尔茨海默病易感性一致的结构，识别了差异表达无法发现的细胞类型特异性重连，在220个通路评分上的细胞类型分类宏F1达0.984，仅比使用全部22,088个基因的最先进分类器低0.3个百分点。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员从单细胞转录组数据中推断基因程序间的定向调控关系，发现细胞类型特异性的通路重连，无需额外实验。

idea_spark.idea: 将ORBIT应用于疾病进展纵向单细胞数据，学习基因程序动态重连模式，预测疾病阶段特异性调控变化。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖高质量单细胞数据，对批次效应和稀疏性敏感；非对称影响可能混淆因果与相关关系。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴因果推断中的干预思想，将观测数据中的特征移除模拟为干预，为无扰动数据下的因果结构学习提供新范式。

idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖高质量单细胞数据，对批次效应和稀疏性敏感；非对称影响可能混淆因果与相关关系。

en.tldr: ORBIT is a self-supervised transformer that learns asymmetric dependencies among gene programs from observational single-cell RNA-seq data without requiring experimental perturbations.
en.motivation: Existing single-cell methods either treat gene programs independently or require perturbation data to model interactions, lacking a way to learn directional influences from observational data alone.
en.method: An intervention-consistent training objective predicts how other programs change when one program is removed, yielding attention weights that reflect asymmetric influence rather than symmetric co-occurrence.
en.result: On 191,890 prefrontal cortex nuclei, ORBIT recovers co-activation structure consistent with Alzheimer's vulnerability, identifies cell-type-specific rewiring invisible to differential expression, and achieves 0.984 macro F1 on cell-type classification from 220 pathway scores, within 0.3 points of a state-of-the-art classifier using all 22,088 genes.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers to infer directional regulatory relationships among gene programs from single-cell transcriptomics and discover cell-type-specific pathway rewiring without additional experiments.

idea_spark.idea: Apply ORBIT to longitudinal single-cell data of disease progression to learn dynamic rewiring patterns of gene programs and predict stage-specific regulatory changes.
idea_spark.risk: Model relies on high-quality single-cell data and is sensitive to batch effects and sparsity; asymmetric influences may confound causal and correlational relationships.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing the concept of intervention from causal inference, simulating feature removal in observational data as intervention provides a new paradigm for causal structure learning without perturbations.

papers.title: ZNO: Stable Rational Neural Operators in the Z-Domain for Discrete-Time Dynamic
papers.summary: We introduce the Z-Domain Neural Operator (ZNO), a causal neural operator whose layers are stable low-rank multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) rational filters parameterized directly in the $z$-plane. ZNO addresses a limitation of existing operator learning methods, many of which are primarily tailored for continuous-time problems, while a large class of system-identification problems is intrinsically discrete-time. The $z$-domain form expresses stability as a unit-disk pole constraint and makes learned discrete-time poles directly readable. The model combines low-rank channel mixing, smooth stable pole reparameterization, causal recurrence, and an optional short finite impulse response (FIR) branch in a single $z$-domain rational recurrent layer. Across controlled discrete system-identification experiments, ZNO's advantage is most evident when the target dynamics are stable rational systems with lightly damped poles near the unit circle. Under matched parameter budgets, ZNO is not uniformly dominant; however, with validation-selected configurations, the same architecture can achieve the lowest mean error across the controlled tasks. A five-bin difficulty sweep over near-unit-circle / long-memory dynamics shows that ZNO has the lowest mean error across memory regimes, from short (approximately 10 steps) to long (approximately 100-200 steps). On five public nonlinear system-identification benchmarks, ZNO is competitive with neural operator and state-space baselines, achieving the lowest mean error on benchmarks whose dynamics align with stable rational discrete-time filters, while classical or state-space baselines remain preferable on some systems. These results position ZNO as a strong model for stable rational discrete-time dynamics, especially in near-unit-circle and long-memory regimes, but not as a universal replacement for specialized system-identification methods.

ai.tldr: 提出Z域神经算子(ZNO)，通过直接在z平面参数化稳定低秩MIMO有理滤波器，解决离散时间动态系统辨识问题。
ai.motivation: 现有算子学习方法主要针对连续时间问题，而大量系统辨识问题本质上是离散时间的，缺乏专门针对离散时间稳定有理动态的算子模型。
ai.method: 在z域中设计因果神经算子层，包含低秩通道混合、平滑稳定极点重参数化、因果递归和可选短FIR分支，通过单位圆极点约束保证稳定性。
ai.result: 在控制实验中，ZNO在近单位圆/长记忆动态上平均误差最低；在五个公开非线性基准上，对符合稳定有理离散时间滤波器的任务取得最低平均误差，但并非通用替代方案。
ai.help_to_user: 若您处理离散时间系统辨识问题，特别是目标动态具有近单位圆极点或长记忆特性，ZNO可提供比现有连续时间算子方法更优的建模精度。

idea_spark.idea: 结合ZNO的稳定极点约束与物理信息神经网络，对已知物理规律的离散时间系统进行数据驱动建模。
idea_spark.risk: ZNO在非稳定有理动态或短记忆任务上可能不如经典状态空间方法，需验证任务匹配度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信号处理z变换获得灵感，将经典数字滤波器设计原则融入现代算子学习框架。

en.tldr: Introduces Z-Domain Neural Operator (ZNO), a causal neural operator with stable low-rank MIMO rational filters parameterized in the z-plane, targeting discrete-time system identification.
en.motivation: Existing operator learning methods are mostly designed for continuous-time problems, while many system-identification tasks are inherently discrete-time, lacking dedicated models for stable rational discrete-time dynamics.
en.method: Designs a causal neural operator layer in the z-domain, combining low-rank channel mixing, smooth stable pole reparameterization, causal recurrence, and an optional short FIR branch, with stability enforced via unit-disk pole constraint.
en.result: In controlled experiments, ZNO achieves the lowest mean error on near-unit-circle/long-memory dynamics; on five public nonlinear benchmarks, it performs best on tasks aligned with stable rational discrete-time filters, but is not a universal replacement.
en.help_to_user: If you work on discrete-time system identification, especially with lightly damped poles near the unit circle or long memory, ZNO can offer better modeling accuracy than continuous-time operator methods.

idea_spark.idea: Combine ZNO's stable pole constraints with physics-informed neural networks for data-driven modeling of discrete-time systems with known physical laws.
idea_spark.risk: ZNO may underperform on non-stable rational dynamics or short-memory tasks; task alignment must be verified.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from z-transform in signal processing, integrating classical digital filter design principles into modern operator learning frameworks.

papers.title: KANs need curvature: penalties for compositional smoothness
papers.summary: Kolmogorov-Arnold networks (KANs) offer a potent combination of accuracy and interpretability, thanks to their compositions of learnable univariate activation functions. However, the activations of well-fitting KANs tend to exhibit pathologically high-curvature oscillations, making them difficult to interpret, and standard regularization penalties do not prevent this. Here we derive a basis-agnostic curvature penalty and show that penalized models can maintain accuracy while achieving substantially smoother activations. Accounting for how function composition shapes curvature, we prove an upper bound on the full model's curvature relative to the curvature penalty, and use this to motivate richer forms of penalties. Scientific machine learning is increasingly bottlenecked by the trade-off between accuracy and interpretability. Results such as ours that improve interpretability without sacrificing accuracy will further strengthen KANs as a practical tool for both prediction and insight.

ai.tldr: 提出一种与基无关的曲率惩罚项，使KAN在保持精度的同时获得更平滑的激活函数，提升可解释性。
ai.motivation: KAN的激活函数常出现高曲率振荡，标准正则化无法抑制，损害可解释性。
ai.method: 推导与基无关的曲率惩罚项，并利用函数复合对曲率的影响证明全模型曲率的上界，进而设计更丰富的惩罚形式。
ai.result: 惩罚后的模型在保持精度的前提下，激活函数显著更平滑。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户训练更平滑、更易解释的KAN模型，缓解精度与可解释性之间的权衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将曲率惩罚与自适应正则化强度结合，根据局部曲率动态调整惩罚权重。
idea_spark.risk: 过度惩罚可能抑制模型表达能力，需谨慎调节惩罚强度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受函数复合曲率上界启发，可探索更精细的层次化正则化策略。

zh.tldr: 提出一种与基无关的曲率惩罚项，使KAN在保持精度的同时获得更平滑的激活函数，提升可解释性。

en.tldr: Proposes a basis-agnostic curvature penalty that enables KANs to maintain accuracy while achieving substantially smoother activations, improving interpretability.
en.motivation: KAN activations often exhibit high-curvature oscillations that standard regularization cannot prevent, harming interpretability.
en.method: Derives a basis-agnostic curvature penalty and proves an upper bound on the full model's curvature relative to the penalty, motivating richer penalty forms.
en.result: Penalized models maintain accuracy while achieving significantly smoother activations.
en.help_to_user: Helps users train smoother, more interpretable KAN models, alleviating the accuracy-interpretability trade-off.

idea_spark.idea: Combine curvature penalty with adaptive regularization strength that adjusts based on local curvature.
idea_spark.risk: Over-penalization may suppress model expressiveness; careful tuning of penalty strength is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the curvature upper bound from function composition, finer-grained hierarchical regularization strategies can be explored.

papers.title: Divergence is Uncertainty: A Closed-Form Posterior Covariance for Flow Matching
papers.summary: Flow matching has become a leading framework for generative modeling, but quantifying the uncertainty of its samples remains an open problem. Existing approaches retrain the model with auxiliary variance heads, maintain costly ensembles, or propagate approximate covariance through many integration steps, trading off training cost, inference cost, or accuracy. We show that none of these trade-offs is necessary. We prove that, for any pre-trained flow matching velocity field, the trace of the posterior covariance over the clean data given the current state equals, in closed form, the divergence of the velocity field, up to a known time-dependent prefactor and an additive constant. We call this the \emph{divergence-uncertainty identity} for flow matching. The matrix-level form of the identity is similarly closed-form, depending solely on the velocity Jacobian. Because the identity is exact and post-hoc, it is computable on any pre-trained flow matching model, with no retraining and no architectural modification. For one-step generators such as MeanFlow, the same identity yields the exact end-to-end generation uncertainty in a single forward pass, eliminating the multi-step variance propagation required by all prior methods. Experiments on MNIST confirm that the resulting per-pixel uncertainty maps are semantically meaningful, concentrating on digit boundaries where inter-sample variation is highest, and that the scalar uncertainty score tracks actual prediction error, all at roughly 10,000$\times$ less total compute than ensembling or Monte Carlo dropout.

papers.title: Isotropic Fourier Neural Operators
papers.summary: Fourier Neural Operators are deep learning models that learn mappings between function spaces and can be used to learn and solve partial differential equations (PDEs), in some cases significantly faster than traditional PDE solvers. Within the model are Fourier layers, which apply linear transformations directly to the Fourier modes, with parameters depending on the wave numbers. However, most physical systems are isotropic, with the results being independent of the coordinate system chosen, but the linear transformations do not necessarily respect these symmetries. We propose a modification to the linear transformations that ensures spatial symmetries are respected, called the Isotropic Fourier Neural Operator, which both improves model performance and reduces the number of parameters by up to a factor of 16 in 2D and 96 in 3D.

ai.tldr: 提出各向同性傅里叶神经算子，通过修改线性变换确保空间对称性，提升性能并大幅减少参数。
ai.motivation: 标准傅里叶神经算子的线性变换不保证各向同性，而多数物理系统是各向同性的，导致对称性被破坏。
ai.method: 在傅里叶层中引入各向同性约束的线性变换，使模型对坐标旋转等空间对称操作保持不变性。
ai.result: 模型性能提升，参数数量在2D中减少最多16倍，在3D中减少最多96倍。
ai.help_to_user: 若您需要求解各向同性偏微分方程，该算子可提供更高效且对称性保持的深度学习方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将各向同性约束与图神经网络结合，用于分子动力学中旋转不变势能面的学习。
idea_spark.risk: 严格对称性约束可能限制模型对非对称或边界条件复杂系统的表达能力。

en.tldr: Proposes Isotropic Fourier Neural Operators that enforce spatial symmetries via modified linear transforms, improving performance and reducing parameters.
en.motivation: Standard Fourier Neural Operators lack isotropy, but most physical systems are isotropic; this breaks symmetry and may degrade performance.
en.method: Introduces isotropic constraints on the linear transformations in Fourier layers, ensuring invariance to rotations and other spatial symmetries.
en.result: Model performance improves while parameters decrease by up to 16× in 2D and 96× in 3D.
en.help_to_user: If you work on isotropic PDEs, this operator offers a more efficient and symmetry-preserving deep learning solution.

idea_spark.idea: Combine isotropic constraints with graph neural networks for learning rotation-invariant potential energy surfaces in molecular dynamics.
idea_spark.risk: Strict symmetry constraints may limit expressiveness for non-symmetric systems or complex boundary conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embedding physical symmetries into neural architectures inspires prior knowledge integration in fields like fluid dynamics and materials science.

papers.title: PowerSINDy: Identifying Nonlinear Time-Dependent Dynamics in Power Grid Frequency
papers.summary: System identification plays a crucial role in physics and machine learning for discovering governing equations directly from data. A powerful approach is the Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics (SINDy) method, which assumes that only a few dominant terms drive the essential behavior of a nonlinear dynamical system. While SINDy methods have shown excellent results, they are most often illustrated on synthetic or simulated systems, leaving open the question of how well they perform on complex, noisy, real-world data. Power grid frequency dynamics provide a highly relevant and challenging environment for advancing system identification methods. In this work, we propose PowerSINDy as a framework for empirical power system data. We apply this framework to empirical frequency data from the Continental Europe (CE) and South Korea (SK) synchronous grids, two major power systems with distinct dynamical characteristics. PowerSINDy, which also includes time-dependent terms, can identify the dynamics of these complex real-world systems. Furthermore, we benchmark three sparsity-promoting regression strategies: Sequentially Thresholded Least Squares (STLSQ), Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator (LASSO), and Sparse Relaxed Regularized Regression (SR3) to evaluate trade-offs between accuracy, sparsity, and robustness. Results show that LASSO consistently achieves the lowest stable RMSEs, reaching 0.0101 for the CE, while STLSQ provides the best balance between accuracy and stability. SR3 exhibits higher variability and sensitivity to regularization, with L0 and L1 producing nearly indistinguishable outcomes.

ai.tldr: 提出PowerSINDy框架，用于从真实电网频率数据中识别非线性时变动力学，并比较三种稀疏回归策略。
ai.motivation: 现有SINDy方法多用于合成数据，在复杂真实系统（如电网频率）中的表现尚不明确。
ai.method: 在PowerSINDy框架中引入时变项，并对比STLSQ、LASSO和SR3三种稀疏回归方法。
ai.result: LASSO在CE电网达到最低稳定RMSE（0.0101），STLSQ在精度与稳定性间取得最佳平衡，SR3变异性较大。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将稀疏识别方法应用于真实电网数据，并选择适合的回归策略。

idea_spark.idea: 结合时变项和稀疏回归，可捕捉系统参数随时间变化的规律。
idea_spark.risk: 真实数据噪声大，可能导致过拟合或稀疏性不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 考虑将时变SINDy与在线学习结合，实现实时系统辨识。

en.tldr: Proposes PowerSINDy framework to identify nonlinear time-dependent dynamics from real power grid frequency data, benchmarking three sparsity-promoting regression strategies.
en.motivation: Existing SINDy methods are mostly validated on synthetic data; their performance on complex, noisy real-world systems like power grid frequency is unclear.
en.method: Introduces time-dependent terms into the PowerSINDy framework and compares STLSQ, LASSO, and SR3 regression strategies.
en.result: LASSO achieves the lowest stable RMSE (0.0101) for the CE grid; STLSQ offers the best balance between accuracy and stability; SR3 shows higher variability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users apply sparse identification methods to real power grid data and choose appropriate regression strategies.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporating time-dependent terms and sparse regression can capture parameter variations over time.
idea_spark.risk: Real data noise may cause overfitting or insufficient sparsity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider combining time-varying SINDy with online learning for real-time system identification.

papers.title: An ALE-Consistent Graph Neural Operator-Transformer Framework for Fluid-Structure Interaction
papers.summary: We propose an arbitrary Lagrangian-Eulerian (ALE)-consistent machine learning framework for long-term fluid-structure interaction (FSI) prediction on deforming unstructured meshes. Specifically, the fluid dynamics are modeled by a surrogate that combines a graph neural operator (GNO) with a vision Transformer (ViT) for spatiotemporal prediction, while a lightweight long short-term memory (LSTM) network predicts structural kinematics at the interface. The two surrogates are coupled through a standard partitioned procedure. Most importantly, kinematic compatibility at the moving interface is enforced via an ALE-consistent boundary-correction step that updates the fluid-side interface velocity with the predicted structural velocity at each coupling update, thereby improving near-interface accuracy and long-term rollout stability. To mitigate autoregressive error accumulation, a two-stage training strategy is adopted, consisting of single-step supervised pretraining followed by long-term autoregressive fine-tuning. The proposed framework is validated on the benchmark problem of a flexible beam vibration in the wake of a cylinder. Results demonstrate accurate phase-consistent predictions over long rollouts and robust generalization under inlet-profile variations in both interpolation and extrapolation settings. Systematic ablation studies further assess the respective contributions of the ViT module, ALE-consistent boundary correction, and long-term training to predictive accuracy and rollout robustness.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种ALE一致的图神经算子-Transformer框架，用于长期流固耦合预测。
ai.motivation: 现有机器学习方法在处理变形网格上的长期流固耦合预测时，精度和稳定性不足。
ai.method: 结合图神经算子与视觉Transformer进行流体时空预测，LSTM预测结构运动，通过ALE一致边界校正和两阶段训练实现稳定耦合。
ai.result: 在柔性梁振动基准问题上实现了长期相位一致的预测，并在插值和外推场景下表现出鲁棒泛化。
ai.help_to_user: 该框架可用于工程中流固耦合问题的快速、稳定预测，减少高保真仿真成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将图神经算子与Transformer结合，利用图结构处理非规则网格，Transformer捕捉全局时空依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 两阶段训练可能增加训练复杂度，且对网格质量敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受传统ALE有限元方法启发，将几何守恒律引入神经网络预测。

en.tldr: An ALE-consistent GNO-Transformer framework for long-term FSI prediction on deforming meshes.
en.motivation: Existing ML methods lack accuracy and stability for long-term FSI on deforming unstructured meshes.
en.method: Combines GNO with ViT for fluid spatiotemporal prediction, LSTM for structural kinematics, with ALE-consistent boundary correction and two-stage training.
en.result: Achieves phase-consistent long-term predictions and robust generalization on a flexible beam benchmark.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast and stable FSI predictions for engineering applications, reducing high-fidelity simulation costs.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate GNO with Transformer to handle irregular meshes and capture global spatiotemporal dependencies.
idea_spark.risk: Two-stage training may increase complexity and sensitivity to mesh quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by traditional ALE finite element methods, incorporating geometric conservation into neural network predictions.

papers.title: From Euler to Dormand-Prince: ODE Solvers for Flow Matching Generative Models
papers.summary: Sampling from Flow Matching generative models requires solving an ordinary differential equation (ODE) whose computational cost is dominated by neural network forward passes. We derive four classical ODE solvers -- Euler, Explicit Midpoint, Classical Runge-Kutta (RK4), and Dormand-Prince 5(4) -- from first principles via Taylor expansion, implement them from scratch in PyTorch, and systematically benchmark their efficiency on Conditional Flow Matching tasks ranging from 2D toy distributions to MNIST digits. On the quantitative side, we use sliced Wasserstein distance to construct NFE-quality Pareto frontiers,finding that RK4 at 80 function evaluations achieves sample quality comparable to Euler at 200. Beyond reproducing known convergence rates, we report two empirical observations: (1) the Jacobian eigenvalue spectrum of the learned velocity field stiffens sharply near t=1, explaining why the adaptive Dormand-Prince solver automatically concentrates its step budget at the end of the trajectory; (2) the quality gap between low-order and high-order solvers widens for undertrained and smaller models, indicating that solver choice matters most when the model is imperfect. Code and all experiment scripts are publicly available.

ai.tldr: 本文从泰勒展开推导了四种经典ODE求解器（欧拉、显式中点、经典龙格-库塔、多曼德-普林斯），并在条件流匹配任务上系统比较了它们的采样效率。
ai.motivation: 流匹配生成模型的采样依赖于求解ODE，其计算成本主要由神经网络前向传播决定，因此需要高效求解器。
ai.method: 从泰勒展开推导四种ODE求解器，在PyTorch中实现，并在2D玩具分布和MNIST数据集上使用切片Wasserstein距离构建NFE-质量帕累托前沿。
ai.result: RK4在80次函数评估时达到与欧拉200次评估相当的样本质量；发现速度场雅可比特征值谱在t=1附近急剧变硬，自适应多曼德-普林斯求解器自动将步长集中在轨迹末端；低阶与高阶求解器的质量差距在欠训练和小模型上扩大。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解不同ODE求解器在流匹配生成模型中的效率差异，指导选择合适求解器以平衡计算成本与样本质量。

idea_spark.idea: 利用速度场雅可比谱的时变特性设计更高效的步长调度策略。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应求解器可能引入额外计算开销，且对模型训练程度敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从经典数值分析中汲取灵感，将ODE求解器的收敛性理论应用于现代生成模型采样。

en.tldr: This paper derives four classical ODE solvers (Euler, Explicit Midpoint, Classical Runge-Kutta, Dormand-Prince) from Taylor expansion and systematically benchmarks their sampling efficiency on Conditional Flow Matching tasks.
en.motivation: Sampling from Flow Matching generative models requires solving an ODE whose cost is dominated by neural network forward passes, necessitating efficient solvers.
en.method: Derive four ODE solvers from first principles via Taylor expansion, implement them in PyTorch, and benchmark on 2D toy distributions and MNIST using sliced Wasserstein distance to construct NFE-quality Pareto frontiers.

idea_spark.idea: Design more efficient step-size schedules leveraging the time-varying Jacobian spectrum of the velocity field.
idea_spark.risk: Adaptive solvers may introduce extra computational overhead and are sensitive to model training status.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from classical numerical analysis to apply ODE solver convergence theory to modern generative model sampling.

papers.title: Existence and multiplicity of solutions for a critical Grushin problem with a singular nonlinearity
papers.summary: We investigate the existence and multiplicity of positive solutions to the problem \begin{equation} \begin{cases} \begin{aligned}   - Δ_γ u &= λu^{p} + u^{-δ} &\quad \text{in } Ω,   \quad u &= 0 &\quad \text{on } \partial Ω, \end{aligned} \end{cases} \end{equation} where $Δ_γ$ denotes the Grushin operator defined by \begin{equation} Δ_γ := Δ_x + (1+γ)^2 |x|^{2γ}Δ_y, \end{equation} with $γ>0$, $z=(x,y)\in \mathbb{R}^N$, $N=n+m$, $n \geq 1$, $m\geq 1$, $Ω\subset \mathbb{R}^N$ a smooth bounded domain, $λ>0$, $1<p<\infty$, and $δ>0$.   The analysis depends on the exponent $p$, which may be subcritical, critical, or supercritical, that is, $p<2_γ^*-1$, $p=2_γ^*-1$, or $p>2_γ^*-1$, respectively, where $2_γ^*=\frac{2Q}{Q-2}$ is the critical Sobolev exponent associated with the Grushin operator, and $Q=m+(1+γ)n$ is the corresponding homogeneous dimension.

ai.tldr: 研究具有奇异非线性的临界Grushin问题正解的存在性与多重性。
ai.motivation: 探索Grushin算子在奇异非线性下的解结构，填补相关理论空白。
ai.method: 通过变分法、临界点理论及Sobolev嵌入分析，区分次临界、临界和超临界指数情形。
ai.result: 在不同指数范围内证明了正解的存在性与多重性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供Grushin型偏微分方程解的存在性理论依据。

idea_spark.idea: 将奇异项与临界指数结合，可能产生新的解分支现象。
idea_spark.risk: 奇异项可能导致解的正则性不足，需额外估计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 考虑更一般的退化算子或混合边界条件。

en.tldr: We study the existence and multiplicity of positive solutions for a critical Grushin problem with a singular nonlinearity.
en.motivation: To understand the solution structure of Grushin operators under singular nonlinearities, filling a theoretical gap.
en.method: Variational methods, critical point theory, and Sobolev embedding analysis are used, distinguishing subcritical, critical, and supercritical exponent regimes.
en.result: Existence and multiplicity of positive solutions are proved for different ranges of the exponent.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical foundations for solutions of Grushin-type PDEs.

idea_spark.idea: Combining singular terms with critical exponents may yield new bifurcation phenomena.
idea_spark.risk: The singular term may cause insufficient regularity of solutions, requiring additional estimates.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider more general degenerate operators or mixed boundary conditions.

papers.title: High-throughput full-f gyrokinetics of the tokamak boundary
papers.summary: Full-f global gyrokinetic simulations of the plasma boundary have until now required heroic computational efforts and case-by-case expert intervention, precluding systematic parameter scans. Here we demonstrate a paradigm shift: hundreds of independent, concurrent, and unsupervised full-f boundary gyrokinetic simulations in a geometry inspired by the Tokamak à Configuration Variable (TCV), covering both the closed flux surface region and the open-field-line scrape-off layer (SOL) while scanning triangularity, elongation, and heating power. All simulations are evolved much longer than the turbulence relaxation time until the steady state is reached. Analysis of the steady-state profiles reveals that the impact of plasma shaping on confinement is strongly power dependent: at low power, triangularity primarily controls the SOL ion temperature, while at high power it mostly affects the edge ion temperature gradient. The low-power hot SOL observed for positive triangularity is explained by a neoclassical trapped-ion mechanism in which triangularity modifies the field-line arc length between banana turning points and the high-field-side limiter, altering the interaction with cold neutral-ionization regions. Fingerprint analysis of turbulent transport categorize the simulations in a regime dominated by ion temperature gradient (ITG) or trapped electron modes (TEMs), confirmed by dedicated local linear gyrokinetic calculations. The generated open data represents a previously unobtainable resource. It can serve both as a benchmark for boundary transport models, and as a training dataset for data-driven methods in fusion foundation and surrogate models.

ai.tldr: 实现了托卡马克边界全f回旋动理学的高通量、无监督模拟，揭示了等离子体形状对约束的功率依赖性。
ai.motivation: 传统全f边界模拟计算量巨大且需专家干预，无法进行系统参数扫描。
ai.method: 基于TCV几何，并行运行数百个独立全f边界回旋动理学模拟，扫描三角性、拉长比和加热功率，直至稳态。
ai.result: 等离子体形状对约束的影响强烈依赖于功率：低功率时三角性主要控制刮削层离子温度，高功率时主要影响边缘离子温度梯度；正三角性下的低功率热刮削层由新经典捕获离子机制解释。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了边界输运模型基准测试和聚变基础模型训练的高质量开放数据集。

idea_spark.idea: 利用该数据集训练数据驱动的聚变代理模型，加速边界输运预测。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟基于特定TCV几何，结果可能不完全适用于其他托卡马克。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合新经典机制与湍流指纹分析，可设计更优的等离子体形状控制策略。

zh.tldr: 实现了托卡马克边界全f回旋动理学的高通量、无监督模拟，揭示了等离子体形状对约束的功率依赖性。
zh.motivation: 传统全f边界模拟计算量巨大且需专家干预，无法进行系统参数扫描。
zh.method: 基于TCV几何，并行运行数百个独立全f边界回旋动理学模拟，扫描三角性、拉长比和加热功率，直至稳态。
zh.result: 等离子体形状对约束的影响强烈依赖于功率：低功率时三角性主要控制刮削层离子温度，高功率时主要影响边缘离子温度梯度；正三角性下的低功率热刮削层由新经典捕获离子机制解释。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了边界输运模型基准测试和聚变基础模型训练的高质量开放数据集。

idea_spark.idea: 利用该数据集训练数据驱动的聚变代理模型，加速边界输运预测。

idea_spark.inspiration: 结合新经典机制与湍流指纹分析，可设计更优的等离子体形状控制策略。

en.tldr: High-throughput, unsupervised full-f gyrokinetic simulations of the tokamak boundary reveal power-dependent effects of plasma shaping on confinement.
en.motivation: Traditional full-f boundary simulations require heroic computational efforts and expert intervention, preventing systematic parameter scans.
en.method: Hundreds of independent, concurrent full-f boundary gyrokinetic simulations in TCV-inspired geometry, scanning triangularity, elongation, and heating power to steady state.
en.result: Plasma shaping impact on confinement is strongly power-dependent: triangularity controls SOL ion temperature at low power and edge ion temperature gradient at high power; low-power hot SOL for positive triangularity explained by neoclassical trapped-ion mechanism.
en.help_to_user: Provides a valuable open dataset for benchmarking boundary transport models and training data-driven fusion surrogate models.

idea_spark.idea: Use the dataset to train data-driven fusion surrogate models for fast boundary transport prediction.
idea_spark.risk: Simulations are based on specific TCV geometry; results may not fully generalize to other tokamaks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining neoclassical mechanisms with turbulence fingerprint analysis can guide improved plasma shape control strategies.

papers.title: Error estimates for an unregularized optimal control problem for the stationary Navier-Stokes equations
papers.summary: We consider an unregularized optimal control problem subject to the steady-state Navier-Stokes equations. We derive the existence of optimal solutions and prove first- and -- necessary and sufficient -- second-order optimality conditions. To approximate solutions to the optimal control problem, we consider the variational discretization scheme. We analyze convergence properties of the discretization and prove a priori error estimates for locally optimal controls that are nonsingular and which satisfy a growth condition which implies a bang-bang structure.

ai.tldr: 针对稳态Navier-Stokes方程的无正则化最优控制问题，证明了最优解的存在性、一阶和二阶最优性条件，并给出了变分离散化方案的先验误差估计。
ai.motivation: 为无正则化最优控制问题提供严格的数学理论支撑，特别是针对具有bang-bang结构的最优控制。
ai.method: 采用变分离散化方案逼近最优控制问题，并利用二阶充分性条件分析收敛性。
ai.result: 证明了非奇异局部最优控制的先验误差估计，该控制满足导致bang-bang结构的增长条件。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解无正则化最优控制问题的理论性质和离散化误差行为。

idea_spark.idea: 将变分离散化与二阶充分性条件结合，为无正则化问题提供严格的误差界。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果依赖于非奇异性和增长条件，实际应用中可能难以验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将类似分析框架应用于流体力学中的其他控制问题，如非稳态或带约束情形。

zh.motivation: 为无正则化最优控制问题提供严格的数学理论支撑，特别是针对具有bang-bang结构的最优控制。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将类似分析框架应用于流体力学中的其他控制问题，如非稳态或带约束情形。

en.tldr: For an unregularized optimal control problem governed by the stationary Navier-Stokes equations, we prove existence of optimal solutions, first- and second-order optimality conditions, and derive a priori error estimates for the variational discretization scheme.
en.motivation: To provide rigorous mathematical foundations for unregularized optimal control problems, especially those with bang-bang structure.
en.method: We employ a variational discretization scheme to approximate the optimal control problem and analyze convergence using second-order sufficient conditions.
en.result: We prove a priori error estimates for nonsingular locally optimal controls satisfying a growth condition that implies a bang-bang structure.
en.help_to_user: This helps users understand the theoretical properties and discretization error behavior of unregularized optimal control problems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine variational discretization with second-order sufficient conditions to derive rigorous error bounds for unregularized problems.
idea_spark.risk: The theoretical results rely on nonsingularity and growth conditions that may be difficult to verify in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying similar analytical frameworks to other control problems in fluid dynamics, such as unsteady or constrained cases.

papers.title: Arbitrarily Conditioned Hierarchical Flows for Spatiotemporal Events
papers.summary: Events in spatiotemporal systems are ubiquitous, yet modeling their complex distributions remains challenging. Existing point process models often rely on strong structural assumptions and are typically limited to autoregressive, event-by-event prediction. As a result, they struggle to support broader inference tasks such as inverse inference, trajectory reconstruction, and recovery of missing event locations. We introduce Arbitrarily Conditioned Hierarchical Flows (ARCH), a hierarchical flow matching framework for spatiotemporal event modeling. ARCH is expressive enough to capture complex event distributions while enabling tractable and accurate computation of conditional intensities, which quantify instantaneous event risk. Built on a history-encoder-generative-decoder architecture, ARCH introduces a hybrid masking strategy for flexible conditioning on arbitrary observed events. This enables a unified treatment of forecasting, inverse inference, and partial trajectory recovery within a single framework. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that ARCH consistently outperforms existing baselines across both prediction and conditional inference tasks.

ai.tldr: 提出一种分层流匹配框架（ARCH），统一处理时空事件的预测、逆推理和轨迹重建等任务。
ai.motivation: 现有点过程模型依赖强结构假设，仅支持自回归逐事件预测，难以处理逆推理、轨迹重建和缺失位置恢复等更广泛的推理任务。
ai.method: 基于历史编码器-生成解码器架构，引入混合掩码策略实现对任意观测事件的条件化，通过分层流匹配学习复杂事件分布并计算条件强度。
ai.result: 在合成和真实数据集上，ARCH在预测和条件推理任务中均优于现有基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种统一框架，可同时支持时空事件的预测、逆推理和部分轨迹恢复，便于用户灵活处理多种推理需求。

idea_spark.idea: 将分层流匹配与混合掩码结合，实现任意条件化，可能启发新的条件生成模型设计。
idea_spark.risk: 框架复杂度较高，可能在大规模高维时空数据上存在计算瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用流匹配的连续变换特性，可探索在物理模拟、交通预测等领域的应用。

zh.motivation: 现有点过程模型依赖强结构假设，仅支持自回归逐事件预测，难以处理逆推理、轨迹重建和缺失位置恢复等更广泛的推理任务。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用流匹配的连续变换特性，可探索在物理模拟、交通预测等领域的应用。

en.tldr: Proposes a hierarchical flow matching framework (ARCH) that unifies forecasting, inverse inference, and trajectory reconstruction for spatiotemporal events.
en.motivation: Existing point process models rely on strong structural assumptions and only support autoregressive event-by-event prediction, struggling with broader inference tasks like inverse inference, trajectory reconstruction, and missing location recovery.
en.method: Built on a history-encoder-generative-decoder architecture, ARCH introduces a hybrid masking strategy for flexible conditioning on arbitrary observed events, using hierarchical flow matching to learn complex event distributions and compute conditional intensities.
en.result: On synthetic and real-world datasets, ARCH consistently outperforms existing baselines in both prediction and conditional inference tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified framework that simultaneously supports forecasting, inverse inference, and partial trajectory recovery for spatiotemporal events, enabling flexible handling of diverse inference needs.

idea_spark.idea: Combining hierarchical flow matching with hybrid masking for arbitrary conditioning may inspire new designs for conditional generative models.
idea_spark.risk: The framework's complexity may pose computational bottlenecks on large-scale high-dimensional spatiotemporal data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the continuous transformation property of flow matching, explore applications in physical simulation, traffic prediction, and other domains.

papers.title: StyleShield: Exposing the Fragility of AIGC Detectors through Continuous Controllable Style Transfer
papers.summary: AI-generated content (AIGC) detectors are increasingly deployed in high-stakes settings such as academic integrity screening, yet their reliability rests on a fundamental paradox: as language models are trained on human-written corpora, the statistical boundary between AI and human writing will inevitably dissolve as models improve. Commercial incentives have further distorted this landscape -- detection services and "de-AIification" tools often operate within the same supply chain, replacing evaluation of content quality with judgment of content origin. We present StyleShield, the first flow matching framework for conditional text style transfer, operating directly in continuous token embedding space via a DiT backbone with zero-initialized cross-attention adapters conditioned on frozen Qwen-7B representations. At inference, we adapt the SDEdit paradigm from image synthesis to text embeddings, with a single parameter gamma providing smooth continuous control over the evasion-preservation trade-off. On a multi-domain Chinese benchmark, StyleShield achieves 94.6% evasion against the training detector and >=99% against three unseen detectors, maintaining 0.928 semantic similarity. We further introduce RateAudit, a document-level scheduling algorithm that demonstrates detection-rate verdicts can be set to arbitrary values, directly questioning the reliability of score-based evaluation.

ai.tldr: 提出StyleShield，首个基于流匹配的连续可控文本风格迁移框架，通过DiT骨干和零初始化交叉注意力适配器在连续词嵌入空间操作，实现高规避率并质疑AIGC检测器的可靠性。
ai.motivation: AIGC检测器在高风险场景中部署，但AI与人类写作的统计边界随模型改进而模糊，且检测服务与去AI化工具存在利益冲突，需揭示其脆弱性。
ai.method: 使用冻结的Qwen-7B表示作为条件，通过DiT骨干和零初始化交叉注意力适配器在连续词嵌入空间进行流匹配风格迁移；推理时采用SDEdit范式，单参数gamma控制规避与保留的平滑权衡。
ai.result: 在多领域中文基准上，对训练检测器实现94.6%规避率，对三个未见检测器实现≥99%规避率，同时保持0.928语义相似度；RateAudit算法可任意设定检测率判决。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解AIGC检测器的脆弱性，以及如何通过可控风格迁移规避检测，同时保持内容语义。

idea_spark.idea: 将SDEdit范式从图像合成扩展到文本嵌入，实现连续可控的文本风格迁移。
idea_spark.risk: 可能被滥用为绕过学术诚信检测的工具，需考虑伦理边界。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴图像领域的扩散模型编辑方法，应用于文本领域解决检测规避问题。

idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴图像领域的扩散模型编辑方法，应用于文本领域解决检测规避问题。

en.tldr: Proposes StyleShield, the first flow matching framework for continuous controllable text style transfer, operating in continuous token embedding space via DiT backbone and zero-initialized cross-attention adapters, achieving high evasion rates and questioning AIGC detector reliability.
en.motivation: AIGC detectors are deployed in high-stakes settings, but the statistical boundary between AI and human writing dissolves as models improve, and detection services conflict with de-AIification tools, necessitating exposure of their fragility.
en.method: Uses frozen Qwen-7B representations as conditions, with DiT backbone and zero-initialized cross-attention adapters for flow matching style transfer in continuous token embedding space; at inference, adapts SDEdit paradigm with single parameter gamma for smooth evasion-preservation trade-off.
en.result: On a multi-domain Chinese benchmark, achieves 94.6% evasion against training detector and ≥99% against three unseen detectors, maintaining 0.928 semantic similarity; RateAudit algorithm can set detection-rate verdicts to arbitrary values.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the fragility of AIGC detectors and how to evade detection via controllable style transfer while preserving semantics.

idea_spark.idea: Extends SDEdit paradigm from image synthesis to text embeddings for continuous controllable text style transfer.
idea_spark.risk: May be misused to bypass academic integrity checks; ethical boundaries need consideration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrows diffusion model editing methods from image domain and applies them to text domain for detection evasion.

papers.title: CellxPert: Inference-Time MCMC Steering of a Multi-Omics Single-Cell Foundation Model for In-Silico Perturbation
papers.summary: In this work, we introduce CellxPert, a scalable multimodal foundation model that unifies single-cell and spatial multi-omics within a common representation space. CellxPert jointly encodes transcriptomic (scRNA-seq), chromatin-accessibility (ATAC-seq), and surface-proteomic (CITE-seq) measurements, while directly incorporating MERFISH and imaging mass-cytometry data as 2D or 3D spatial-visual layers. CellxPert facilitates four key downstream tasks out of the box: (i) cell-type annotation across a broad ontology of 154 largely overlapping identities -- the largest label space addressed to date and a stringent test of fine-grained discrimination, (ii) efficient fine-tuning using Low Rank Adaptation (LoRA), (iii) genome-wide transcriptomic response prediction to in-silico perturbations (ISP), and (iv) seamless multi-omic integration across various assays and platforms. Unlike current single-cell foundation models, which approximate gene perturbations by deleting or reordering tokenized gene expression ranks, CellxPert employs a Metropolis-Hastings sampler whose proposal kernel uses the model's masked conditional distributions to transition to new transcriptomic states conditioned on the perturbed genes. This Markov-chain procedure mitigates out-of-distribution artifacts introduced by abrupt token manipulation and produces trajectories that are biologically interpretable. Evaluations on PBMC68K, Replogle Perturb-seq, Systema, and BMMC benchmarks show that CellxPert surpasses classical and state-of-the-art baselines in cell-type annotation, perturbation response prediction, and multi-omic integration.

ai.tldr: CellxPert是一个多模态单细胞基础模型，通过马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛采样实现基因组扰动模拟，统一了转录组、染色质可及性和蛋白质组等多组学数据。
ai.motivation: 现有单细胞基础模型通过直接删除或重排基因表达秩次来近似扰动，导致分布外伪影，缺乏生物学可解释性。
ai.method: 采用Metropolis-Hastings采样器，利用模型的掩码条件分布逐步过渡到扰动后的转录状态，生成生物学可解释的轨迹。
ai.result: 在PBMC68K、Replogle Perturb-seq等基准上，细胞类型注释、扰动响应预测和多组学整合均超越经典及最新基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在不进行湿实验的情况下，通过计算机模拟预测基因扰动对单细胞转录组的影响，加速药物靶点发现。

idea_spark.idea: 将马尔可夫链采样与基础模型结合，替代直接编辑token，为任意生成式模型提供更自然的条件生成策略。
idea_spark.risk: 采样过程计算成本较高，且对模型条件分布的准确性敏感，可能在高维空间中出现采样效率问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受统计物理中退火采样启发，将扰动视为状态转移，而非简单删除，保留了生物过程的连续性。

idea_spark.risk: 采样过程计算成本较高，且对模型条件分布的准确性敏感，可能在高维空间中出现采样效率问题。

en.tldr: CellxPert is a multimodal single-cell foundation model that uses Markov chain Monte Carlo sampling for in-silico perturbation, unifying transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, and proteomics.
en.motivation: Existing single-cell foundation models approximate perturbations by deleting or reordering gene expression ranks, causing out-of-distribution artifacts and lacking biological interpretability.
en.method: Employs a Metropolis-Hastings sampler that transitions to perturbed transcriptomic states using the model's masked conditional distributions, generating biologically interpretable trajectories.
en.result: Outperforms classical and state-of-the-art baselines on PBMC68K, Replogle Perturb-seq, and other benchmarks in cell-type annotation, perturbation response prediction, and multi-omic integration.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to computationally predict the transcriptomic effects of gene perturbations without wet-lab experiments, accelerating drug target discovery.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Markov chain sampling with foundation models as an alternative to direct token editing offers a more natural conditional generation strategy for any generative model.
idea_spark.risk: The sampling process is computationally expensive and sensitive to the accuracy of the model's conditional distributions, potentially causing sampling efficiency issues in high-dimensional spaces.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by annealing sampling in statistical physics, treating perturbations as state transitions rather than deletions preserves the continuity of biological processes.

papers.title: Least Costly Space-Filling Experiment Design for the Identification of a Nonlinear System
papers.summary: The quality of an estimated nonlinear model highly depends on the data quality that was used for the system identification. By using a Gaussian Process-based optimal input design approach, a so-called space-filling dataset can be generated in the feature space of the system model. The design method is applicable for a broad type of signals and models and also incorporates information measures through optimality criteria into the signal design. However, the resulting input design can be costly to apply to the real system. The goal of this paper is to propose a space-filling input design that can minimize the experimentation cost in terms of a user defined measure, while still guaranteeing a prescribed level of space-fillingness. Through a Monte Carlo simulation study we demonstrate that the proposed method can appropriately shape the excitation signal to significantly reduce the experimental cost while the identified model performance remains adequate.

ai.tldr: 提出一种最小化实验成本的填充空间输入设计方法，用于非线性系统辨识。
ai.motivation: 现有填充空间输入设计虽能生成高质量数据集，但应用于实际系统时成本高昂。
ai.method: 基于高斯过程的最优输入设计，在特征空间中生成填充空间数据集，并通过用户定义的成本度量最小化实验代价，同时保证预设的填充空间程度。
ai.result: 蒙特卡洛仿真表明，该方法能显著降低实验成本，同时保持辨识模型性能。

en.tldr: Proposes a space-filling input design that minimizes experimentation cost for nonlinear system identification.
en.motivation: Existing space-filling input designs can be costly to apply to real systems despite generating high-quality datasets.
en.method: Uses Gaussian process-based optimal input design to generate space-filling datasets in feature space, minimizing a user-defined cost measure while ensuring a prescribed level of space-fillingness.
en.result: Monte Carlo simulations show the method significantly reduces experimental cost while maintaining adequate model performance.
en.help_to_user: Helps users obtain high-quality nonlinear system models at lower experimental cost.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate experimentation cost as an optimization objective alongside space-filling criteria for cost-efficient experimental design.
idea_spark.risk: The cost measure may be oversimplified; real applications may require multiple cost factors; the trade-off between space-fillingness and cost needs careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by cost constraints in experimental design, integrating economic considerations into traditional information-theoretic optimal design frameworks.

papers.title: Inducing Permutation Invariant Priors in Bayesian Optimization for Carbon Capture and Storage Applications
papers.summary: Bayesian Optimization is an iterative method, tailored to optimizing expensive black box objective functions. Surrogate models like Gaussian Processes, which are the gold standard in Bayesian Optimization, can be inefficient for inputs with permutation symmetries, as the most common kernels employed are better suited for vector inputs rather than unordered sets of items. Motivated by this issue, we turn to permutation invariant Bayesian Optimization for well placement in Carbon Capture and Storage projects. The high fidelity black box simulator is instructed to operate wells under group control, giving rise to permutation symmetries within injector and producer groups that cannot be exploited with standard GP kernels. In this work, our main contribution is a novel Gaussian Process kernel (GP-Perm) that encodes permutation invariance by comparing sets through a stable divergence between their induced empirical representations, and can be combined with standard kernels for additional vector-valued inputs. As a learned invariant baseline, we also consider a Deep Kernel Learning model (DKL-DS) using the Deep Sets architecture to learn a permutation-invariant embedding. We evaluate the proposed methodology across 8 use cases, comprising seven synthetic benchmarks and one realistic CCS case study (Johansen formation)

ai.tldr: 提出一种新的高斯过程核（GP-Perm）和基于Deep Sets的深度核学习模型（DKL-DS），用于碳捕集与封存项目中井位优化的置换不变贝叶斯优化。
ai.motivation: 标准贝叶斯优化中的高斯过程核无法处理注入井和生产井组内的置换对称性，导致优化效率低下。
ai.method: 设计GP-Perm核，通过集合诱导经验表示之间的稳定散度编码置换不变性；同时采用Deep Sets架构学习置换不变嵌入的DKL-DS模型。
ai.result: 在7个合成基准和1个真实CCS案例（Johansen地层）上评估了所提方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为碳捕集与封存中的井位优化提供了一种高效的置换不变贝叶斯优化方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将GP-Perm核与多任务学习结合，同时优化多个CCS场地。
idea_spark.risk: 真实CCS案例的模拟器保真度可能影响核的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用集合表示之间的散度度量来编码对称性，可启发其他对称性（如旋转、平移）的核设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a novel Gaussian Process kernel (GP-Perm) and a Deep Kernel Learning model (DKL-DS) using Deep Sets for permutation invariant Bayesian optimization in well placement for Carbon Capture and Storage. Evaluated on 7 synthetic benchmarks and one realistic CCS case study (Johansen formation).
en.motivation: Standard GP kernels in Bayesian optimization cannot exploit permutation symmetries within injector and producer well groups, leading to inefficiency.
en.method: Designs GP-Perm kernel that encodes permutation invariance via a stable divergence between induced empirical representations of sets; also develops DKL-DS with Deep Sets architecture to learn a permutation-invariant embedding.
en.result: Evaluated on 7 synthetic benchmarks and one realistic CCS case study (Johansen formation).
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient permutation invariant Bayesian optimization method for well placement in Carbon Capture and Storage.

idea_spark.idea: Combine GP-Perm kernel with multi-task learning to optimize multiple CCS sites simultaneously.
idea_spark.risk: Fidelity of the simulator in real CCS cases may affect kernel generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using divergence between set representations to encode symmetry can inspire kernel designs for other symmetries (e.g., rotation, translation).

papers.title: Mixture Prototype Flow Matching for Open-Set Supervised Anomaly Detection
papers.summary: Open-set supervised anomaly detection (OSAD) aims to identify unseen anomalies using limited anomalous supervision. However, existing prototype-based methods typically model normal data via a unimodal Gaussian prior, failing to capture inherent multi-modality and resulting in blurred decision boundaries. To address this, we propose Mixture Prototype Flow Matching (MPFM), a framework that learns a continuous transformation from normal feature distributions to a structured Gaussian mixture prototype space. Departing from traditional flow-based approaches that rely on a single velocity vector, MPFM explicitly models the velocity field as a Gaussian mixture prior where each component corresponds to a distinct normal class. This design facilitates mode-aware and semantically coherent distribution transport. Furthermore, we introduce a Mutual Information Maximization Regularizer (MIMR) to prevent prototype collapse and maximize normal-anomaly separability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MPFM achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse benchmarks under both single- and multi-anomaly settings.

ai.tldr: 提出混合原型流匹配框架，通过高斯混合先验建模正常数据的多模态分布，实现开放集监督异常检测。
ai.motivation: 现有原型方法使用单峰高斯先验，无法捕捉正常数据的多模态性，导致决策边界模糊。
ai.method: 学习从正常特征分布到结构化高斯混合原型空间的连续变换，显式建模速度场为高斯混合先验，并引入互信息最大化正则化器防止原型坍缩。
ai.result: 在多种基准上，单异常和多异常设置下均达到最先进性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种处理开放集异常检测中多模态正常数据的新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes Mixture Prototype Flow Matching, which models multi-modal normal data via a Gaussian mixture prior for open-set supervised anomaly detection.
en.motivation: Existing prototype methods use unimodal Gaussian prior, failing to capture multi-modality of normal data and leading to blurred decision boundaries.
en.method: Learns a continuous transformation from normal feature distributions to a structured Gaussian mixture prototype space, explicitly models the velocity field as a Gaussian mixture prior, and introduces a Mutual Information Maximization Regularizer to prevent prototype collapse.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on diverse benchmarks under both single- and multi-anomaly settings.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new approach to handle multi-modal normal data in open-set anomaly detection.

idea_spark.risk: The number of mixture components must be predefined, which may affect generalization.

papers.title: A Projected Tug-of-War Game for the Regularized $p$-Laplacian
papers.summary: We give a tug-of-war interpretation of the regularized $p$-Laplacian $\divgg\big((1+|Dv|^2)^{p/2-1}Dv\big)=0$ in a bounded domain $Ω\subset\R^n$, $p\ge 2$. The key is the linear lift $w(x,x_{n+1})=v(x)+x_{n+1}$, which identifies this equation with $Δ_p w=0$ in $\R^{n+1}$. Projecting the standard $(n+1)$-dimensional $p$-harmonious scheme onto $\R^n$ yields a discrete dynamic programming principle for which we prove existence, uniqueness, and Borel measurability of solutions with strip boundary data, identify the unique fixed point with the value of the projected game, and establish convergence to the viscosity solution as $\varepsilon\to 0$.

ai.tldr: 通过线性提升将正则化p-Laplacian方程转化为高维p-Laplacian，并给出其投影拔河博弈解释，证明了离散动态规划原理的解存在唯一且收敛到粘性解。
ai.motivation: 为正则化p-Laplacian方程提供博弈论解释，建立其与经典p-Laplacian的联系。
ai.method: 利用线性提升将方程嵌入高维，投影标准p-调和方案得到离散动态规划原理。
ai.result: 证明了投影博弈值函数的存在唯一性、Borel可测性，并建立了ε→0时向粘性解的收敛性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解正则化p-Laplacian的博弈论背景及其数值近似方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将高维p-调和博弈投影到低维，为带正则化的p-Laplacian设计新数值格式。
idea_spark.risk: 高维投影可能增加计算复杂度，且收敛性依赖于正则化参数。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用博弈论和几何变换简化非线性PDE的分析与计算。

en.motivation: To provide a game-theoretic interpretation for the regularized p-Laplacian and connect it to the classical p-Laplacian.
en.method: A linear lift embeds the equation into higher dimension; the standard p-harmonious scheme is projected back to obtain a discrete dynamic programming principle.
en.result: Existence, uniqueness, and Borel measurability of the projected game value are proved, along with convergence to the viscosity solution as ε→0.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the game-theoretic background of the regularized p-Laplacian and its numerical approximation.

papers.title: On the convex hull of the graph of a simple monomial
papers.summary: Motivated by previous efforts toward mathematically analyzing the treatment of monomials in spatial branch-and-bound, we study the convex hull of the graph of a simple monomial on a nonnegative box domain in arbitrary dimension, where at most one of the variable lower bounds is positive. We give: (i) a description via linear inequalities, and (ii) a formula for the volume.

ai.method: 通过线性不等式刻画凸包，并推导体积公式。
ai.result: 得到了凸包的线性不等式描述和体积计算公式。

papers.title: Gaussian-weighted normal operators on Euclidean space
papers.summary: We consider the normal operator of the X-ray transform, weighted with Gaussian weights, in Euclidean space with dimension at least 3. We show the eigenfunctions of the normal operator are joint eigenfunctions of the harmonic oscillator and the spherical Laplacian, and we relate the spectrum to that of elliptic operators in the 1-cusp pseudodifferential calculus.

ai.tldr: 研究了欧几里得空间中带高斯权重的X射线变换的正规算子，证明了其特征函数是谐振子和球面拉普拉斯算子的联合特征函数，并将谱与1-尖点伪微分算子中的椭圆算子联系起来。
ai.motivation: 理解带高斯权重的X射线变换正规算子的谱性质及其与经典算子的关系。
ai.method: 利用调和分析和伪微分算子理论，分析正规算子的特征函数和谱结构。
ai.result: 正规算子的特征函数是谐振子和球面拉普拉斯算子的联合特征函数，谱与1-尖点伪微分椭圆算子相关。
ai.help_to_user: 为带权重的X射线变换的谱分析提供理论基础，可能应用于成像和逆问题。

papers.title: Benchmarking local Hebbian learning rules for memory storage and prototype extraction
papers.summary: Associative memory or content-addressable memory is an important component function in computer science and information processing, and at the same time a key concept in cognitive and computational brain science. Many different neural network architectures and learning rules have been proposed to model the brain's associative memory while investigating key component functions like figure-ground segmentation, perceptual reconstruction and rivalry. A less investigated but equally important capability of associative memory is prototype extraction where the training set comprises distorted prototype instances and the task is to recall the correct generating prototype given a new distorted instance. In this paper we benchmark associative memory function of seven different Hebbian learning rules employed in non-modular and modular recurrent networks with winner-take-all dynamics operating on moderately sparse binary patterns. We measure pattern storage and weight information capacity, prototype extraction capabilities, and sensitivity to correlations in data. The original additive Hebb rule comes out with worst capacity, covariance learning proves to be robust but with moderate capacity, and the Bayesian-Hebbian learning rules show highest capacity in almost all different conditions tested.

ai.tldr: 本文基准测试了七种赫布学习规则在非模块化和模块化递归网络中的联想记忆功能，重点评估了原型提取能力。
ai.motivation: 联想记忆的原型提取能力研究较少，本文旨在系统比较不同赫布规则在此任务上的表现。
ai.method: 使用中等稀疏二值模式，在具有胜者全取动力学的递归网络中测试七种赫布学习规则，测量存储容量、原型提取及对数据相关性的敏感性。
ai.result: 原始加性赫布规则容量最低，协方差学习稳健但容量中等，贝叶斯-赫布学习规则在几乎所有条件下容量最高。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解不同赫布学习规则在联想记忆和原型提取任务中的性能差异，为选择合适的学习规则提供参考。

idea_spark.idea: 将贝叶斯-赫布规则应用于连续值或更复杂模式的原型提取任务，或结合注意力机制提升性能。
idea_spark.risk: 贝叶斯-赫布规则可能对数据分布假设敏感，实际应用中需验证其鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受协方差学习稳健性的启发，可探索混合学习规则以平衡容量与鲁棒性。

en.tldr: This paper benchmarks seven Hebbian learning rules in non-modular and modular recurrent networks for associative memory, focusing on prototype extraction.
en.motivation: Prototype extraction in associative memory is understudied; this work systematically compares Hebbian rules on this task.
en.method: Using moderately sparse binary patterns, seven Hebbian rules are tested in recurrent networks with winner-take-all dynamics, measuring storage capacity, prototype extraction, and sensitivity to correlations.
en.result: The additive Hebb rule has the lowest capacity, covariance learning is robust but moderate, and Bayesian-Hebbian rules achieve the highest capacity under most conditions.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand performance differences among Hebbian learning rules for associative memory and prototype extraction, aiding rule selection.

idea_spark.idea: Apply Bayesian-Hebbian rules to continuous or more complex patterns for prototype extraction, or combine with attention mechanisms.
idea_spark.risk: Bayesian-Hebbian rules may be sensitive to data distribution assumptions; robustness needs verification in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the robustness of covariance learning, explore hybrid rules to balance capacity and robustness.

papers.title: Multi-fidelity surrogates for mechanics of composites: from co-kriging to multi-fidelity neural networks
papers.summary: Composite materials exhibit strongly hierarchical and anisotropic properties governed by coupled mechanisms spanning constituents, plies, laminates, structures, and manufacturing history. This intrinsic complexity makes predictive modeling of composites expensive, because repeated experiments and high-fidelity simulations are needed to cover large design spaces of material, structure, and manufacturing. Multi-fidelity surrogate modeling addresses this challenge by combining abundant, less expensive data with limited high-accuracy data to recover reliable high-fidelity predictions. This review presents a structured overview of multi-fidelity modeling for composite mechanics, covering Gaussian-process or Kriging-based methods, including co-Kriging, coregionalization models, autoregressive formulations, nonlinear autoregressive Gaussian processes, multi-fidelity deep Gaussian processes, and multi-fidelity neural networks. Their distinctions are examined in terms of cross-fidelity correlation, discrepancy representation, uncertainty quantification, and scalability. Selected examples of their applications to composites are introduced according to the roles that multi-fidelity surrogates play in engineering problems, including forward prediction for rapid exploration of material design spaces, inverse optimization for composite parameter identification and design search under limited high-fidelity access, and workflow integration, where heterogeneous data sources, constraints, and validation requirements determine model utility. Open question discussions highlight recurring challenges specific to composites, such as regime-dependent fidelity gaps associated with nonlinear damage and manufacturing history, mismatches between simulations and experiments, and uncertainty propagation across multi-fidelity models.

ai.tldr: 综述了复合材料力学中多保真度代理模型方法，包括协同克里金、多保真神经网络等，并讨论了其在正向预测、逆向优化和工作流集成中的应用。
ai.motivation: 复合材料的多尺度、各向异性特性导致高保真模拟和实验成本高昂，需要结合低保真数据与少量高保真数据来高效预测。
ai.method: 系统梳理了基于高斯过程/克里金的方法（协同克里金、区域化模型、自回归模型、非线性自回归高斯过程、多保真深度高斯过程）以及多保真神经网络，并比较了跨保真度相关性、差异表示、不确定性量化和可扩展性。
ai.result: 通过正向预测加速材料设计空间探索、逆向优化实现参数识别与设计搜索、工作流集成处理异构数据源与约束，展示了多保真代理模型在复合材料工程中的实用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速了解复合材料多保真代理建模的主流方法、适用场景及关键挑战，为选择或开发新模型提供参考。

idea_spark.idea: 针对复合材料非线性损伤和制造历史导致的保真度间隙，可设计自适应保真度选择策略，动态分配高保真样本。
idea_spark.risk: 跨保真度模型间的相关性假设可能不成立，导致预测偏差；实验与模拟之间的失配需谨慎处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴多任务学习或迁移学习思想，利用低保真数据预训练神经网络，再微调高保真数据，可能提升效率。

en.tldr: A structured review of multi-fidelity surrogate modeling for composite mechanics, covering co-kriging, multi-fidelity neural networks, and their applications in forward prediction, inverse optimization, and workflow integration.
en.motivation: The hierarchical and anisotropic nature of composites makes high-fidelity simulations and experiments expensive; multi-fidelity surrogates combine cheap low-fidelity data with limited high-fidelity data for efficient predictions.
en.method: Systematically reviews Gaussian-process/Kriging-based methods (co-kriging, coregionalization, autoregressive, nonlinear autoregressive GP, multi-fidelity deep GP) and multi-fidelity neural networks, comparing cross-fidelity correlation, discrepancy representation, uncertainty quantification, and scalability.
en.result: Demonstrates utility in forward prediction for rapid design space exploration, inverse optimization for parameter identification and design search, and workflow integration handling heterogeneous data sources and constraints.
en.help_to_user: Provides a concise overview of mainstream multi-fidelity surrogate methods for composites, their application scenarios, and open challenges, aiding method selection and future research.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive fidelity selection strategy that dynamically allocates high-fidelity samples based on regime-dependent fidelity gaps caused by nonlinear damage and manufacturing history.
idea_spark.risk: Assumptions about cross-fidelity correlation may fail, leading to biased predictions; mismatches between simulation and experiment require careful handling.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage multi-task learning or transfer learning: pre-train neural networks on abundant low-fidelity data, then fine-tune on limited high-fidelity data to improve efficiency.

papers.title: Quantitative Weak Unique Continuation on Annular Domains for Backward Degenerate Parabolic Equations with Degenerate Interior Points
papers.summary: In this paper, we establish a quantitative weak unique continuation theorem on an annular domain for a backward degenerate parabolic equation with a degenerate interior point. Our methodology hinges on approximating the solution of the degenerate parabolic equation through solutions of non-degenerate parabolic counterparts. Subsequently, we establish Carleman estimates for the non-degenerate parabolic equation across two separate domains. By virtue of these estimates, we deduce a quantitative weak unique continuation property for the degenerate parabolic equation, thereby substantiating the weak unique continuation result for the original degenerate parabolic equation.

ai.tldr: 本文在环形区域上建立了具有退化内点的向后退化抛物方程的定量弱唯一延拓定理。
ai.motivation: 研究退化抛物方程在环形区域上的弱唯一延拓性质，为相关反问题提供理论基础。
ai.method: 通过非退化抛物方程逼近退化方程，并在两个分离区域上建立Carleman估计，进而推导定量弱唯一延拓。
ai.result: 证明了退化抛物方程的弱唯一延拓性质。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解退化抛物方程解的唯一性条件，可用于反问题或控制理论。

idea_spark.idea: 将退化问题转化为非退化问题再建立估计，是一种通用的分析策略。
idea_spark.risk: 逼近过程可能引入误差，需谨慎控制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可尝试将类似方法应用于非线性退化方程或高维环形区域。
