en.tldr: This tutorial systematically introduces the generalized Lyapunov method for analyzing input-to-state stability (ISS) of PDEs, with step-by-step functional constructions for parabolic and hyperbolic equations.
en.motivation: Classical Lyapunov methods have limitations when dealing with PDEs subject to complex boundary disturbances, necessitating a more flexible and efficient stability analysis tool.
en.method: The generalized Lyapunov method (GLM) based on generalized Lyapunov functionals (GLFs) that explicitly depend on the external input, offering flexibility for Dirichlet-type boundary disturbances.
en.result: Successful construction of GLFs and explicit ISS estimates for three representative PDEs: nonlinear parabolic, first-order nonlinear hyperbolic, and second-order linear wave equations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic procedure for constructing GLFs, enabling users to analyze boundary-disturbed PDE stability in practice.

idea_spark.idea: Combine GLF construction with adaptive or robust control to achieve online stability guarantees under boundary disturbances.
idea_spark.risk: GLF construction may rely on specific PDE structures, lacking generality for complex nonlinear or time-varying systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the input-dependent nature of GLFs, one could explore input-dependent Lyapunov functions in neural network stability analysis.

papers.title: Improved semidefinite programming bounds for the maximum $k$-colorable subgraph problem
papers.summary: We study the maximum $k$-colorable subgraph (M$k$CS) problem, which consists in finding a largest $k$-colorable induced subgraph in a given graph. We consider a Semidefinite Programming (SDP) relaxation for the M$k$CS problem and regard its resulting upper bound as a graph parameter. We present several properties of this graph parameter, from which we obtain that the M$k$CS problem is solvable in polynomial time for $k$-perfect graphs. We further derive two novel families of valid inequalities to strengthen the SDP relaxation. The first family reduces to a family of inequalities for the Boolean quadric polytope when $k = 1$, and the second family generalizes the family of rank inequalities for binary linear programming formulations of the stable set problem. We efficiently solve the strengthened SDP relaxation using a cutting-plane algorithm that is based on the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM). Extensive computational experiments show that the obtained upper bounds outperform the best upper bounds from the literature. To complement our SDP-based upper bounds, we propose an integer ADMM variant that uses an exact Binary Semidefinite Programming (BSDP) formulation of the M$k$CS problem to produce high-quality feasible solutions. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first application of the ADMM to compute integer solutions to a BSDP problem.

ai.tldr: 本文研究最大k-可着色子图问题，提出一种半定规划松弛并分析其图参数性质，引入两类有效不等式加强松弛，利用ADMM切割平面算法高效求解，数值实验表明上界优于现有结果，并首次用ADMM求解整数BSDP问题。
ai.motivation: 最大k-可着色子图问题是经典组合优化难题，现有上界不够紧，需要更精确的松弛方法和高效求解算法。
ai.method: 提出半定规划松弛并分析其图参数性质，推导两类有效不等式（分别推广布尔二次多面体不等式和稳定集秩不等式），采用基于ADMM的切割平面算法求解加强松弛，并设计整数ADMM变体直接求解二进制半定规划。
ai.result: 数值实验显示所得上界优于文献中最佳上界，整数ADMM能产生高质量可行解。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种更紧的上界计算方法和高效求解框架，可用于图着色、最大团等问题的精确或近似求解。

idea_spark.idea: 将半定规划松弛与切割平面结合，并利用ADMM同时处理连续松弛和整数解，形成统一框架。
idea_spark.risk: ADMM收敛性对参数敏感，整数变体可能陷入局部最优；大规模图时计算成本仍较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从图参数性质出发设计有效不等式，启发我们利用问题结构定制松弛加强策略。

en.tldr: This paper studies the maximum k-colorable subgraph problem, proposes an SDP relaxation and analyzes its graph parameter properties, introduces two families of valid inequalities to strengthen the relaxation, solves the strengthened SDP efficiently via an ADMM-based cutting-plane algorithm, and demonstrates superior upper bounds in experiments; it also presents the first ADMM-based integer solver for binary SDP.
en.motivation: The maximum k-colorable subgraph problem is a classic combinatorial optimization challenge; existing upper bounds are not tight enough, motivating the need for stronger relaxations and efficient algorithms.
en.method: An SDP relaxation is proposed and its graph parameter properties are analyzed. Two families of valid inequalities are derived (generalizing Boolean quadric polytope inequalities and rank inequalities for stable sets). The strengthened SDP is solved via a cutting-plane algorithm based on ADMM. An integer ADMM variant is also designed to directly solve the binary SDP formulation.
en.result: Numerical experiments show that the obtained upper bounds outperform the best bounds in the literature, and the integer ADMM produces high-quality feasible solutions.
en.help_to_user: Provides tighter upper bounds and an efficient solution framework applicable to graph coloring, maximum clique, and related problems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine SDP relaxation with cutting planes and use ADMM to handle both continuous relaxation and integer solutions in a unified framework.
idea_spark.risk: ADMM convergence is sensitive to parameters; the integer variant may get stuck in local optima; computational cost remains high for large graphs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Deriving valid inequalities from graph parameter properties inspires us to leverage problem structure for customized relaxation strengthening.

papers.title: DynoSLAM: Dynamic SLAM with Generative Graph Neural Networks for Real-World Social Navigation
papers.summary: Traditional Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) algorithms rely heavily on the static environment assumption, which severely limits their applicability in real-world spaces populated by moving entities, such as pedestrians. In this work, we propose DynoSLAM, a tightly-coupled Dynamic GraphSLAM architecture that integrates socially-aware Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) directly into the factor graph optimization. Unlike conventional approaches that use rigid constant-velocity heuristics or deterministic single-agent neural priors, our framework formulates pedestrian motion forecasting as a stochastic World Model. By utilizing Monte Carlo rollouts from a trained GNN, we capture the multimodal epistemic uncertainty of human interactions and embed it into the SLAM graph via a dynamic Mahalanobis distance factor. We demonstrate through extensive simulated experiments that this stochastic formulation not only maintains highly accurate retrospective tracking but also prevents the optimization failures caused by the deterministic "argmax problem". Ultimately, extracting the empirical mean and covariance matrices of future pedestrian states provides a mathematically rigorous, probabilistic safety envelope for downstream local planners, enabling anticipatory and collision-free robot navigation in densely crowded environments.

ai.tldr: 提出DynoSLAM，一种将图神经网络直接集成到因子图优化中的动态SLAM架构，通过随机世界模型和蒙特卡洛展开捕捉行人交互的多模态认知不确定性，实现密集人群中的安全导航。
ai.motivation: 传统SLAM依赖静态环境假设，无法处理真实世界中移动实体（如行人）带来的动态干扰。
ai.method: 将行人运动预测建模为随机世界模型，利用训练好的GNN进行蒙特卡洛展开，提取未来行人状态的均值和协方差矩阵，作为动态马氏距离因子嵌入SLAM图优化。
ai.result: 在模拟实验中，该随机公式不仅保持高精度回溯跟踪，还避免了确定性“argmax问题”导致的优化失败，为下游规划器提供概率安全包络。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助机器人研究者和工程师在拥挤动态环境中实现更鲁棒、更安全的SLAM和导航。

idea_spark.idea: 将行人交互的图神经网络预测与SLAM因子图紧耦合，通过蒙特卡洛展开量化不确定性。
idea_spark.risk: GNN训练需要大量行人轨迹数据，模拟环境与真实世界可能存在分布偏移。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受社会力模型和概率机器人学启发，将社会感知与不确定性量化结合到SLAM中。

idea_spark.risk: GNN训练需要大量行人轨迹数据，模拟环境与真实世界可能存在分布偏移。

en.tldr: Proposes DynoSLAM, a dynamic SLAM architecture that integrates Graph Neural Networks directly into factor graph optimization, using a stochastic world model and Monte Carlo rollouts to capture multimodal epistemic uncertainty of pedestrian interactions for safe navigation in dense crowds.
en.motivation: Traditional SLAM relies on static environment assumption, failing in real-world spaces with moving entities like pedestrians.
en.method: Models pedestrian motion forecasting as a stochastic World Model, performs Monte Carlo rollouts from a trained GNN, and extracts empirical mean and covariance matrices of future pedestrian states as dynamic Mahalanobis distance factors embedded into the SLAM graph.
en.result: In simulated experiments, the stochastic formulation maintains highly accurate retrospective tracking and prevents optimization failures caused by the deterministic 'argmax problem', providing a probabilistic safety envelope for downstream planners.
en.help_to_user: Helps robotics researchers and engineers achieve more robust and safe SLAM and navigation in crowded dynamic environments.

idea_spark.idea: Tightly coupling GNN-based pedestrian interaction prediction with SLAM factor graph via Monte Carlo rollouts to quantify uncertainty.
idea_spark.risk: GNN training requires large pedestrian trajectory datasets; simulation-to-real distribution shift may occur.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by social force models and probabilistic robotics, combining social awareness with uncertainty quantification in SLAM.

papers.title: Quantum jump trajectories, hybrid systems, non-Hermitian evolutions, quantum/classical walks
papers.summary: Quantum stochastic master equations of jump type are formulated in a general way and connections with quantum/classical hybrid systems and quantum filtering theory are discussed. By introducing the notion of ``typical trajectory", we show how to recursively construct the solution of the non-linear stochastic master equation (the conditional state). Moreover, by the notion of ``exclusive probability densities" we can describe all the probabilities related to the jumps, in particular, the waiting times of the jumps and their probability distributions. This general formulation and the idea of hybrid system allow to unify and generalize different fields: evolutions under non-Hermitian Hamiltonians, unitary dynamics interspersed by quantum channels at random times, quantum renewal processes, continuous time open quantum walks, Lindblad rate equation, ...

ai.tldr: 本文以一般形式构建了量子随机主方程（跳跃型），并探讨了其与量子/经典混合系统及量子滤波理论的联系。
ai.motivation: 统一并推广非厄米哈密顿量演化、随机时间量子信道、量子更新过程、连续时间开放量子行走等多个领域。

ai.result: 建立了跳跃型随机主方程的一般框架，并展示了混合系统视角下的统一表述。

en.tldr: This paper formulates quantum stochastic master equations of jump type in a general way and discusses connections with quantum/classical hybrid systems and quantum filtering theory.
en.motivation: To unify and generalize different fields: non-Hermitian evolutions, unitary dynamics interspersed by quantum channels, quantum renewal processes, continuous time open quantum walks, etc.
en.method: Introduces the notion of 'typical trajectory' to recursively construct the conditional state, and uses 'exclusive probability densities' to describe waiting times and probability distributions of jumps.
en.result: Establishes a general framework for jump-type stochastic master equations and demonstrates a unified description from the hybrid system perspective.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the mathematical structure of quantum jump trajectories, hybrid systems, and non-Hermitian evolutions.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the quantum/classical hybrid system framework to noise modeling in quantum computing.
idea_spark.risk: The mathematical formalism is abstract, and physical implementation may be complex.
idea_spark.inspiration: From quantum filtering theory, one can design more efficient quantum state estimation schemes.

papers.title: Optimal control problem for a nonlinear nonlocal evolution system describing an interacting ternary mixture with an evaporating component: 2D case with bulk evaporation
papers.summary: We present an optimal control problem to guide the selection of morphology classes arising in organic solar cells. The study focuses on phase separation processes in polymer solvent mixtures, with particular attention to solvent evaporation as a mechanism to arrest morphology formation. We establish the existence of optimal controls and analyze the Frechet derivative of the control to state mapping. Finally, we derive the first order necessary optimality condition via the corresponding adjoint system.

ai.tldr: 针对有机太阳能电池中形态选择的最优控制问题，研究了含蒸发组分的三元混合物的非线性非局部演化系统，证明了最优控制的存在性并推导了一阶必要条件。
ai.motivation: 有机太阳能电池的性能依赖于活性层的微观形态，而溶剂蒸发可有效冻结相分离过程，因此需要数学框架来指导形态控制。
ai.method: 建立非线性非局部演化系统的最优控制问题，分析控制到状态映射的Fréchet导数，并利用伴随系统推导一阶最优性条件。
ai.result: 证明了最优控制的存在性，并得到了伴随系统形式的一阶必要条件。
ai.help_to_user: 为有机太阳能电池制备中的工艺参数优化提供了严格的数学基础。

idea_spark.idea: 将溶剂蒸发速率作为控制变量，通过伴随方法实时调整工艺参数以锁定目标形态。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设与实际蒸发动力学可能存在偏差，且高维计算成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合机器学习代理模型加速伴随求解，实现形态的在线调控。

en.tldr: An optimal control problem for morphology selection in organic solar cells is studied, focusing on a nonlinear nonlocal evolution system for ternary mixtures with evaporation; existence of optimal controls and first-order necessary conditions via adjoint system are established.
en.motivation: The performance of organic solar cells depends on the active layer morphology, and solvent evaporation can arrest phase separation, motivating a mathematical framework to guide morphology control.
en.method: An optimal control problem is formulated for a nonlinear nonlocal evolution system; the Fréchet derivative of the control-to-state map is analyzed, and first-order necessary optimality conditions are derived using the adjoint system.
en.result: Existence of optimal controls is proved, and first-order necessary conditions in terms of the adjoint system are obtained.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for optimizing process parameters in organic solar cell fabrication.

idea_spark.idea: Use solvent evaporation rate as a control variable and adjust it in real time via adjoint methods to lock in a target morphology.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions may deviate from actual evaporation dynamics, and high-dimensional computation can be costly.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine machine learning surrogate models to accelerate adjoint solves for online morphology control.

papers.title: Probability Geometry and Kolmogorov Expectations via Coordinate Charts
papers.summary: This paper develops a geometric reinterpretation of probability in which expectation arises from averaging in probability coordinates rather than in value space. By interpreting the cumulative distribution functions as coordinate maps, a real-valued random variable is transported into the unit interval, where averaging becomes a linear operation in probability coordinates and is then pulled back to the value space.   Within this representation, the resulting quantities coincide with Kolmogorov means, thereby linking the construction to the classical theory of generalized means associated with Kolmogorov, Nagumo, de Finetti, and Chisini. This connection clarifies that these means are not merely algebraic devices, but arise from a change of representation of probability.   The framework provides a natural setting in which expectation exists beyond classical integrability assumptions. In particular, heavy-tailed phenomena are reinterpreted geometrically: divergence in value space corresponds to boundary concentration in probability coordinates. Laws of large numbers and central limit theorems are established in this coordinate system, showing that asymptotic behaviour can be recovered after this geometric representation.   The perspective also connects to broader constructions based on generalized means, including Rényi-type formulations, and suggests that expectation should be viewed not as a fixed numerical functional, but as the outcome of a chosen probability representation.

ai.tldr: 本文通过将累积分布函数视为坐标映射，重新解释了概率的几何意义，使得期望值在概率坐标中线性平均后拉回值空间，从而与Kolmogorov均值相联系。
ai.motivation: 经典期望定义依赖于可积性假设，难以处理重尾分布；本文旨在提供一种几何框架，使期望在更弱的条件下存在。
ai.method: 将随机变量通过CDF映射到单位区间，在概率坐标中进行线性平均，再通过逆映射拉回值空间，得到Kolmogorov均值。
ai.result: 建立了该坐标系下的大数定律和中心极限定理，重尾现象被几何解释为概率坐标中的边界集中。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解期望的几何本质，以及如何将重尾分布纳入统一的概率框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将任意统计泛函视为在某种坐标下的线性平均，可能统一多种均值理论。
idea_spark.risk: 坐标变换的非线性可能导致实际计算复杂，且需验证逆映射的良定义性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受微分几何中坐标图思想的启发，将概率空间视为流形，期望视为局部线性操作。

en.tldr: This paper reinterprets probability geometrically by treating cumulative distribution functions as coordinate maps, making expectation a linear average in probability coordinates pulled back to value space, linking to Kolmogorov means.
en.motivation: Classical expectation requires integrability, failing for heavy-tailed distributions; this work provides a geometric framework where expectation exists under weaker conditions.
en.method: Map random variables via CDF to the unit interval, average linearly in probability coordinates, then pull back via inverse CDF to obtain Kolmogorov means.
en.result: Laws of large numbers and central limit theorems are established in this coordinate system; heavy tails are reinterpreted as boundary concentration in probability coordinates.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the geometric nature of expectation and how heavy-tailed distributions fit into a unified probabilistic framework.

idea_spark.idea: View any statistical functional as a linear average in some coordinate system, potentially unifying various mean theories.
idea_spark.risk: Nonlinear coordinate transforms may complicate practical computation and require well-defined inverse maps.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by coordinate charts in differential geometry, treating probability space as a manifold and expectation as a local linear operation.

papers.title: Large time behavior of critical marked Hawkes processes with heavy tailed marks and related branching particle systems
papers.summary: We study large time behavior of critical marked Hawkes processes and related branching particle systems. In case of marked Hawkes processes we assume that the kernel function has multiplicative form and the marks corresponding to the events are nonnegative and are assigned independently from a common distribution. This distribution is in the normal domain of attraction of a $(1+β)$-stable law with $0<β<1$. Moreover, we assume that the mean number of events triggered by a single event is equal to $1$ (criticality). We show that, as the time is speeded up, if $β$ is small enough then, the event counting process, appropriately normalized, converges to a spectrally positive $1/(1+β)$ stable Lévy process. The convergence holds in law in the Skorokhod space of càdlàg functions equipped with $M_1$ topology. We also study a borderline case. The present paper complements the results of [A.Talarczyk:``A generalized central limit theorem for critical marked Hawkes processes'', arXiv:2504.11612], where the same model was studied in case of ``large'' $β$. We employ techniques involving a branching representation of marked Hawkes processes. This approach allows to study more general branching processes with branching mechanism in the normal domain of attraction of $(1+β)$-stable law.

ai.tldr: 研究了临界标记Hawkes过程在重尾标记下的长时间行为，当β足够小时，归一化的计数过程收敛到谱正稳定Lévy过程。
ai.motivation: 补充先前关于大β情形的结果，完整刻画临界标记Hawkes过程的极限行为。
ai.method: 利用分支表示将标记Hawkes过程转化为分支粒子系统，并应用稳定律的吸引域理论。
ai.result: 在β小的情况下，计数过程在Skorokhod空间M1拓扑下依分布收敛到1/(1+β)稳定Lévy过程。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解临界Hawkes过程在重尾标记下的极限分布，为金融、网络等领域建模提供理论依据。

idea_spark.idea: 将Hawkes过程的分支结构与稳定律结合，可能用于模拟极端事件聚集。
idea_spark.risk: β的临界值依赖具体模型，实际应用中参数估计可能困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 考虑将结果应用于地震余震或社交媒体级联的长期行为分析。

en.tldr: Studies the large-time behavior of critical marked Hawkes processes with heavy-tailed marks; when β is small, the normalized counting process converges to a spectrally positive stable Lévy process.
en.motivation: Complements previous results for large β to fully characterize the limiting behavior of critical marked Hawkes processes.
en.method: Uses a branching representation to transform marked Hawkes processes into branching particle systems, and applies domain-of-attraction theory for stable laws.
en.result: For small β, the counting process converges in law to a 1/(1+β)-stable Lévy process in the Skorokhod space with M1 topology.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical insight into the limiting distribution of critical Hawkes processes with heavy-tailed marks, useful for modeling in finance, networks, etc.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Hawkes branching structure with stable laws may model extreme event clustering.
idea_spark.risk: The critical β threshold is model-dependent; parameter estimation may be challenging in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider applying results to long-term behavior of aftershocks or social media cascades.

papers.title: SAMamba3D: adapting Segment Anything for generalizable 3D segmentation of multiphase pore-scale images
papers.summary: Reliable segmentation of multiphase pore-scale X-ray images of rocks is necessary to quantify fluid saturation, connectivity, and interfacial geometry. However, current 3D segmentation methods are typically dataset-specific, requiring retraining or extensive fine-tuning whenever rock type, fluid pattern, scanner, or acquisition conditions change. Foundation models such as the Segment Anything Model (SAM) provide strong 2D boundary priors, but they are not directly applicable to 3D data.   We present SAMamba3D, a parameter-efficient framework that adapts a largely frozen SAM encoder to generalizable 3D pore-scale segmentation by coupling it with Mamba-based volumetric context modeling and progressive cross-scale feature interaction. For sandstone and carbonate datasets, with different fluids, wettability, and scanning conditions, SAMamba3D matches or outperforms current 3D baselines while reducing the need for case-specific retraining. The resulting segmented images preserve physically meaningful descriptors, including fluid saturation, connectivity, and interface morphology, enabling more reliable and rapid analysis of large 3D multiphase images.

ai.tldr: 提出SAMamba3D，将冻结的SAM编码器与Mamba体积上下文建模结合，实现多相孔隙尺度X射线图像的高泛化3D分割。
ai.motivation: 现有3D分割方法依赖特定数据集，岩石类型、流体模式或扫描条件变化时需重新训练或微调，缺乏泛化能力。
ai.method: 冻结SAM编码器提取2D边界先验，通过Mamba模块建模3D体积上下文，并引入渐进式跨尺度特征交互。
ai.result: 在砂岩和碳酸盐岩数据集上，SAMamba3D匹配或超越现有3D基线，减少案例特定重训练，保留流体饱和度、连通性等物理描述符。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需大量重训练即可泛化到不同岩石和流体条件的3D分割工具，加速多相图像分析。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将SAM的2D先验与轻量级3D上下文模型（如Mamba）结合，实现高效跨域分割。
idea_spark.risk: Mamba对长程依赖的建模能力可能不如Transformer，在复杂3D结构中边界精度可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用冻结基础模型+轻量适配模块的思路，可推广到其他需要泛化性的3D视觉任务。

en.tldr: SAMamba3D adapts a frozen SAM encoder with Mamba-based volumetric context modeling for generalizable 3D segmentation of multiphase pore-scale images.
en.motivation: Current 3D segmentation methods are dataset-specific and require retraining when rock type, fluid pattern, or scanning conditions change.
en.method: Freeze SAM encoder for 2D boundary priors, couple with Mamba for 3D volumetric context, and use progressive cross-scale feature interaction.
en.result: Matches or outperforms 3D baselines on sandstone and carbonate datasets, reduces case-specific retraining, and preserves physical descriptors like fluid saturation and connectivity.
en.help_to_user: Provides a generalizable 3D segmentation tool that adapts to different rocks and fluids without extensive retraining, accelerating multiphase image analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Combine frozen 2D foundation models with lightweight 3D context models (e.g., Mamba) for efficient cross-domain segmentation.
idea_spark.risk: Mamba may underperform on long-range dependencies compared to Transformers, potentially reducing boundary accuracy in complex 3D structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: The frozen backbone + lightweight adapter approach can inspire generalizable solutions for other 3D vision tasks.

papers.title: A computational comparison of handling distance constraints in MINLP
papers.summary: Minimum distance constraints (minDCs) appear in many geometric optimization problems. They pose major challenges for mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) due to their reverse-convexity. We develop new algorithms for tightening variable bounds in general MINLPs with minDCs. Because many such problems exhibit substantial symmetry, we further introduce a practical approach for handling rotation symmetries via separation of lexicographic constraints induced by Givens rotations. In a computational study, we examine the performance of the various methods and determine the scenarios in which each approach demonstrates superiority.

ai.tldr: 本文针对混合整数非线性规划中的最小距离约束，开发了新的变量界收紧算法，并利用Givens旋转处理旋转对称性。
ai.motivation: 最小距离约束在几何优化中常见，但其反凸性给MINLP求解带来困难，且问题常具有对称性。
ai.method: 提出通用MINLP中最小距离约束的变量界收紧算法，并通过Givens旋转诱导的字典序约束分离来处理旋转对称性。
ai.result: 计算实验比较了各方法性能，确定了每种方法表现最优的场景。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解并选择处理含最小距离约束的MINLP问题的有效算法。

idea_spark.idea: 将Givens旋转与字典序约束结合，为旋转对称性提供了一种实用的分离方法。
idea_spark.risk: 算法性能可能高度依赖问题结构，通用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将几何变换与组合约束结合以处理优化中的对称性。

zh.result: 计算实验比较了各方法性能，确定了每种方法表现最优的场景。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解并选择处理含最小距离约束的MINLP问题的有效算法。

idea_spark.idea: 将Givens旋转与字典序约束结合，为旋转对称性提供了一种实用的分离方法。
idea_spark.risk: 算法性能可能高度依赖问题结构，通用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将几何变换与组合约束结合以处理优化中的对称性。

en.tldr: This paper develops new variable bound tightening algorithms for MINLPs with minimum distance constraints and handles rotation symmetries via Givens rotations and lexicographic constraints.
en.motivation: Minimum distance constraints are common in geometric optimization but challenging due to reverse-convexity, and many such problems exhibit symmetry.
en.method: Proposes bound tightening algorithms for general MINLPs with minDCs, and introduces a practical approach for rotation symmetries using separation of lexicographic constraints induced by Givens rotations.
en.result: Computational study compares methods and identifies scenarios where each approach excels.
en.help_to_user: Helps users select effective algorithms for MINLP problems with minimum distance constraints.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Givens rotations with lexicographic constraints provides a practical separation method for rotation symmetries.
idea_spark.risk: Algorithm performance may be highly problem-dependent; generality needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining geometric transformations with combinatorial constraints to handle symmetries in optimization.

papers.title: Edge-Efficient Image Restoration: Transformer Distillation into State-Space Models
papers.summary: We propose a modular framework for hybrid image restoration that integrates transformer and state-space model (SSM) blocks with a focus on improving runtime efficiency on edge hardware. While transformers provide strong global modeling through self-attention, their attention kernels incur substantial latency on mobile devices, especially for high-resolution inputs. In contrast, SSMs such as Mamba offer lineartime sequence modeling with lower runtime overhead but may underperform on fine grained restoration tasks. To balance accuracy and efficiency, we train lightweight SSM blocks as feature-distilled surrogates of transformer blocks and use them to construct hybrid U-Net-style architectures. To automatically discover effective block combinations, we introduce Efficient Network Search (ENS), a multi-objective search strategy that selects task-specific hybrid configurations from pre-aligned components. ENS optimizes restoration quality while penalizing transformer usage, serving as a lightweight proxy for latency and enabling architecture discovery without repeated hardware profiling. On a Snapdragon 8 Elite CPU, the Restormer baseline requires 10119.52 ms for inference. In contrast, ENS-discovered hybrids significantly reduce runtime: ENS-Deblurring runs in 2973 ms (3.4x faster), ENS-Deraining in 5816 ms (1.74x faster), and ENS-Denoising in 8666 ms (1.17x faster), while maintaining competitive restoration quality.

ai.tldr: 提出一种混合图像恢复框架，将Transformer蒸馏为状态空间模型，并通过多目标搜索自动发现高效架构，在边缘设备上显著加速。
ai.motivation: Transformer在图像恢复中效果好，但自注意力在移动端高分辨率输入下延迟高；SSM（如Mamba）线性时间建模但细粒度恢复性能不足。
ai.method: 训练轻量SSM块作为Transformer块的特征蒸馏替代，构建混合U-Net；引入Efficient Network Search（ENS）多目标搜索，自动选择任务最优混合配置，同时惩罚Transformer使用以代理延迟。
ai.result: 在Snapdragon 8 Elite CPU上，Restormer基线需10119.52ms；ENS发现的混合架构显著降低延迟：去模糊2973ms（3.4倍）、去雨5816ms（1.74倍）、去噪8666ms（1.17倍），同时保持竞争性恢复质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不牺牲太多恢复质量的前提下，大幅提升边缘设备上的图像恢复速度。

idea_spark.idea: 将ENS扩展为在线自适应搜索，根据运行时资源动态调整Transformer/SSM比例。
idea_spark.risk: 搜索空间可能随任务增多而爆炸；蒸馏质量依赖教师Transformer的预训练。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用硬件代理指标（如Transformer块计数）替代真实延迟测量，避免重复硬件剖析，可启发其他效率导向的NAS方法。

en.tldr: A hybrid image restoration framework that distills transformers into state-space models and uses multi-objective search to discover efficient architectures, achieving significant speedup on edge devices.
en.motivation: Transformers excel in image restoration but suffer high latency on mobile devices for high-resolution inputs due to self-attention; SSMs like Mamba offer linear-time modeling but underperform on fine-grained tasks.
en.method: Train lightweight SSM blocks as feature-distilled surrogates of transformer blocks to build hybrid U-Net architectures; introduce Efficient Network Search (ENS), a multi-objective search strategy that selects task-specific hybrid configurations while penalizing transformer usage as a latency proxy.
en.result: On a Snapdragon 8 Elite CPU, Restormer baseline takes 10119.52ms; ENS-discovered hybrids reduce runtime significantly: ENS-Deblurring 2973ms (3.4x), ENS-Deraining 5816ms (1.74x), ENS-Denoising 8666ms (1.17x), while maintaining competitive restoration quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to drastically improve inference speed on edge devices without major sacrifice in restoration quality.

idea_spark.idea: Extend ENS to online adaptive search that dynamically adjusts the transformer/SSM ratio based on runtime resources.
idea_spark.risk: Search space may explode with more tasks; distillation quality depends on pre-trained teacher transformers.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using hardware proxy metrics (e.g., transformer block count) instead of real latency measurements to avoid repeated profiling can inspire other efficiency-oriented NAS methods.

papers.title: Controllable and Verifiable Process Data Synthesis for Process Reward Models
papers.summary: Process reward models (PRMs) rely on high-quality process supervision data, yet existing construction methods often provide limited control over error location, error type, and trajectory consistency. We propose a controllable and verifiable framework for synthesizing process supervision data for PRMs. Our framework first constructs a correct symbolic reasoning chain, injects a template-aware error into an intermediate step, recomputes subsequent steps under the corrupted state, and verifies that the injected step is not derivable from its prefix. The resulting paired trajectories are prefix-invalid at the first error while remaining trajectory-consistent after symbolic recomputation, and are translated into aligned natural-language processes for PRM training and evaluation. Experiments show that the synthesized data improve Best-of-8 reranking on logical reasoning benchmarks and transfer to mathematical reasoning. Step-level evaluation further shows that first-error localization remains substantially more challenging than overall step classification, highlighting the need for fine-grained and verifiable process supervision.

ai.tldr: 提出可控可验证的过程监督数据合成框架，通过符号推理链注入错误并验证，生成高质量配对轨迹用于过程奖励模型训练。
ai.motivation: 现有过程监督数据构建方法对错误位置、类型和轨迹一致性的控制有限。
ai.method: 先构建正确符号推理链，在中间步骤注入模板感知错误，在错误状态下重算后续步骤，验证注入步骤不可从前缀推导，最后翻译为自然语言过程。
ai.result: 合成数据在逻辑推理的Best-of-8重排序中提升性能，并迁移至数学推理；首次错误定位比整体步骤分类更具挑战性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过可控错误注入和验证生成高质量过程监督数据，提升过程奖励模型的训练与评估。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将符号验证与自然语言生成结合，自动生成带标注错误的推理轨迹。
idea_spark.risk: 错误注入模板可能覆盖不全，导致合成数据多样性不足；验证步骤可能引入计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受符号推理与验证结合的启发，可用于构建更可靠的AI推理训练数据。

en.tldr: Proposes a controllable and verifiable framework for synthesizing process supervision data by injecting errors into symbolic reasoning chains and verifying prefix-invalidity, improving process reward model training.
en.motivation: Existing process supervision data construction methods offer limited control over error location, type, and trajectory consistency.
en.method: Construct a correct symbolic reasoning chain, inject a template-aware error at an intermediate step, recompute subsequent steps under the corrupted state, verify the injected step is not derivable from its prefix, and translate into natural language processes.
en.result: Synthesized data improves Best-of-8 reranking on logical reasoning benchmarks and transfers to mathematical reasoning; first-error localization remains more challenging than overall step classification.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to generate high-quality process supervision data with controllable error injection and verification for training and evaluating process reward models.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining symbolic verification with natural language generation to automatically produce reasoning trajectories with annotated errors.
idea_spark.risk: Error injection templates may lack coverage, reducing diversity; verification steps may introduce computational overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the combination of symbolic reasoning and verification for building more reliable AI training data.

papers.title: Information Accessibility Limits in Structured NP Search
papers.summary: We study the problem of locating violating principal minors in structured matrix families that lie near the boundary of P-matrices and admit sparse violations under perturbation. Viewing violation search as an information acquisition problem, we show that, despite strong underlying structure, the location of a violation is globally encoded and not accessible through local queries.   This leads to an information-theoretic bottleneck: each query reveals only vanishing information about the violating subset, so that polynomially many queries accumulate insufficient information to identify it. Using mutual information and Fano's inequality, we show that any algorithm restricted to polynomially many queries cannot recover the violating subset with constant success probability.   Our analysis highlights a fundamental distinction between structure and accessibility: even highly structured problems can be computationally intractable when the information required to locate a solution is not accessible through the available queries.

ai.tldr: 研究在结构化矩阵族中定位违反主次元的极限，发现即使结构很强，违反位置也全局编码，局部查询无法获取足够信息。
ai.motivation: 理解结构化矩阵族中违反主次元的可定位性，揭示信息可访问性对计算复杂性的根本限制。
ai.method: 将违反搜索建模为信息获取问题，利用互信息和Fano不等式分析查询的信息量。
ai.result: 任何使用多项式次查询的算法都无法以恒定成功概率恢复违反子集。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解结构性与信息可访问性之间的区别，以及查询限制如何导致计算困难。

idea_spark.idea: 设计非局部或自适应查询策略来绕过信息瓶颈。
idea_spark.risk: 非局部查询可能在实际系统中不可行或成本过高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在机器学习中考虑查询设计对信息获取效率的影响。

en.tldr: We study the limits of locating violating principal minors in structured matrix families, showing that even with strong structure, the violation location is globally encoded and not accessible via local queries.
en.motivation: To understand the fundamental limits of locating violations in structured matrices, revealing how information accessibility affects computational tractability.
en.method: Modeling violation search as an information acquisition problem and using mutual information and Fano's inequality to analyze query information content.
en.result: Any algorithm restricted to polynomially many queries cannot recover the violating subset with constant success probability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the distinction between structure and accessibility, and how query limitations lead to computational intractability.

idea_spark.idea: Design non-local or adaptive query strategies to bypass the information bottleneck.
idea_spark.risk: Non-local queries may be infeasible or too costly in practical systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires consideration of query design's impact on information acquisition efficiency in machine learning.

papers.title: Neural Decision-Propagation for Answer Set Programming
papers.summary: Integration of Answer Set Programming (ASP) with neural networks has emerged as a promising tool in Neuro-symbolic AI. While existing approaches extend the capabilities of ASP to real world domains, their reasoning pipelines depend on classical solvers, which is a bottleneck for scalability. To tackle this problem, we propose a new method to compute stable models, called decision-propagation (DProp), which alternates falsity decisions and truth propagations. Successful DProp computations are shown to capture the stable model semantics. We then develop Neural DProp (NDProp), a differentiable extension of DProp with neural computation for decisions and fuzzy evaluation for propagations. We evaluate the capabilities of NDProp for learning decision heuristics as well as neuro-symbolic integration, and compare it with existing neuro-symbolic approaches. The results show that NDProp can learn to efficiently compute stable models, and it improves accuracy and scalability on neuro-symbolic benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 提出一种新的决策传播方法，将神经网络与回答集编程结合，实现可微的稳定模型计算，提升可扩展性。
ai.motivation: 现有神经符号方法依赖经典求解器，成为可扩展性瓶颈。
ai.method: 设计决策传播（DProp）交替假决策与真传播，并扩展为可微的神经决策传播（NDProp）。
ai.result: NDProp能学习高效计算稳定模型，在神经符号基准上提升准确率和可扩展性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可微的ASP推理方法，可用于学习决策启发式并集成神经符号系统。

idea_spark.idea: 利用神经网络的模糊评估替代经典传播规则，实现端到端可微推理。
idea_spark.risk: 模糊传播可能丢失精确语义保证，需验证稳定模型捕获的完整性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 交替决策与传播的机制可启发设计更高效的神经符号推理架构。

en.tldr: Proposes a novel decision-propagation method that integrates neural networks with Answer Set Programming for differentiable stable model computation, improving scalability.
en.motivation: Existing neuro-symbolic approaches rely on classical solvers, which become a scalability bottleneck.
en.method: Designs decision-propagation (DProp) alternating falsity decisions and truth propagations, and extends it to differentiable Neural DProp (NDProp).
en.result: NDProp learns to efficiently compute stable models, improving accuracy and scalability on neuro-symbolic benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a differentiable ASP reasoning method that can learn decision heuristics and integrate neuro-symbolic systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use neural fuzzy evaluation to replace classical propagation rules for end-to-end differentiable reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Fuzzy propagation may lose precise semantic guarantees; completeness of stable model capture needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: The alternating decision and propagation mechanism can inspire more efficient neuro-symbolic reasoning architectures.

papers.title: Zero-Shot Signal Temporal Logic Planning with Disjunctive Branch Selection in Dynamic Semantic Maps
papers.summary: Signal Temporal Logic (STL) offers verifiable task specifications and is crucial for safety-critical control. Yet STL planning remains challenging: exact optimization-based methods are often too slow, and learning-based methods struggle to generalize across varying environments. We propose a zero-shot STL planning solver for variable-map environments that generates feasible trajectories without retraining. By integrating a map-conditioned Transformer architecture with a lightweight heuristic, our approach effectively handles complex disjunctive (OR) subformulas. Furthermore, we leverage Transitive Reinforcement Learning (TRL) to ensure consistent temporal grounding and logical coherence across decomposed sub-tasks. Experiments on dynamic semantic maps with diverse obstacle layouts demonstrate consistent gains, highlighting the framework's superior zero-shot generalization to changing environments and broad STL coverage.

ai.tldr: 提出一种零样本信号时序逻辑规划求解器，结合地图条件Transformer和轻量启发式方法，在动态语义地图中无需重新训练即可生成可行轨迹。
ai.motivation: 现有STL规划方法要么优化速度慢，要么学习泛化差，无法适应动态环境。
ai.method: 集成地图条件Transformer架构与轻量启发式，处理析取子公式；利用传递强化学习确保时间一致性和逻辑连贯性。
ai.result: 在多种障碍布局的动态语义地图上，零样本泛化能力和STL覆盖范围均优于基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在安全关键控制中快速生成满足时序逻辑约束的轨迹，无需针对新环境重新训练。

idea_spark.idea: 将地图条件Transformer与传递强化学习结合，实现零样本泛化。
idea_spark.risk: 复杂析取子公式可能仍存在计算瓶颈，且对地图表示质量敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受零样本学习和强化学习在组合任务中应用的启发。

en.tldr: A zero-shot STL planning solver using a map-conditioned Transformer and lightweight heuristic to generate feasible trajectories in dynamic semantic maps without retraining.
en.motivation: Existing STL planning methods are either too slow or fail to generalize across varying environments.
en.method: Integrates a map-conditioned Transformer with a lightweight heuristic to handle disjunctive subformulas, and uses Transitive Reinforcement Learning for consistent temporal grounding.
en.result: Consistent gains on dynamic semantic maps with diverse obstacle layouts, demonstrating superior zero-shot generalization and broad STL coverage.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to quickly generate trajectories satisfying temporal logic constraints in safety-critical control without retraining for new environments.

idea_spark.idea: Combine map-conditioned Transformer with transitive reinforcement learning for zero-shot generalization.
idea_spark.risk: Complex disjunctive subformulas may still pose computational challenges, and performance may be sensitive to map representation quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by zero-shot learning and reinforcement learning for compositional tasks.

papers.title: Where Do Prompt Perturbations Break Generation? A Segment-Level View of Robustness in LoRA-Tuned Language Models
papers.summary: Large language models are sensitive to minor prompt perturbations, yet existing robustness methods usually enforce consistency at the whole-sequence level. This holistic view can hide an important failure mode: a perturbed response may remain globally similar to the clean one while drifting on a critical entity, relation, or conclusion. We introduce S$^2$R$^2$, a segment-level framework for robust LoRA fine-tuning. S$^2$R$^2$ decomposes clean and perturbed generations into semantic segments, aligns them with an optimal-transport objective, and penalises the segments with the largest meaning drift. To connect this output-side objective with model adaptation, we add an adapter-stability regulariser motivated by segment-level attention reallocation, using LoRA norm control as a tractable proxy for limiting perturbation-amplified evidence shifts. A PAC-Bayesian complexity view further explains why controlling adapter growth may support transfer beyond observed perturbations. Experiments on summarisation benchmarks show that S$^2$R$^2$ improves robustness under typographical noise, deletion, synonym replacement, and paraphrasing, while maintaining competitive clean performance and stronger cross-dataset transfer than consistency-based baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出S²R²框架，从片段级别提升LoRA微调语言模型对提示扰动的鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 现有鲁棒性方法在整句层面强制一致性，忽略了关键实体、关系或结论的漂移。
ai.method: 将生成文本分解为语义片段，用最优传输对齐，惩罚漂移最大的片段，并加入适配器稳定性正则化。
ai.result: 在摘要任务上，对打字错误、删除、同义词替换和释义等扰动鲁棒性提升，干净性能保持，跨数据集迁移更强。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何从片段层面增强模型对输入扰动的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将片段级鲁棒性约束与最优传输结合，并利用PAC-Bayes解释适配器增长。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从注意力重分配角度控制适配器范数，启发更细粒度的鲁棒微调策略。

idea_spark.risk: Segment parsing relies on semantic analysis, which may introduce extra errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Controlling adapter norm from an attention reallocation perspective inspires finer-grained robust fine-tuning strategies.

papers.title: Sparse Representation Learning for Vessels
papers.summary: Analyzing human vasculature and vessel-like, tubular structures, such as airways, is crucial for disease diagnosis and treatment. Current methods often rely on small sub-regions or simplified tree-like structures, rendering analysis of entire organ-level networks at clinical resolution computationally challenging. To this end, we propose VAEsselSparse, an efficient encoder-decoder model to obtain a meaningful yet compact representation of the entire organ-level vascular network at sub-millimeter resolution. VAEsselSparse leverages the inherent sparsity of 3D vascular structures via sparse convolutions and attention mechanisms, achieving substantial spatial compression rates of 8 x 8 x 8. We demonstrate superior reconstruction performance compared to dense counterparts and previous methods. Importantly, the resulting latent space retains clinically relevant discriminative features readily usable for classification tasks, such as aneurysm/stenosis or subvariants of the circle of Willis. Moreover, the compact latent space of VAEsselSparse serves as an effective representation for learning vessel-specific priors through generative models, enabling the synthesis of realistic vasculature.

ai.tldr: 提出VAEsselSparse模型，利用稀疏卷积和注意力机制高效编码整个器官级血管网络，实现8×8×8空间压缩，并保留临床判别特征。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以在临床分辨率下分析整个器官级血管网络，计算开销大。
ai.method: 基于稀疏卷积和注意力机制的编码器-解码器模型，利用血管结构的固有稀疏性。
ai.result: 相比密集模型和先前方法，重建性能更优；潜在空间保留临床相关特征，可用于分类和生成。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效压缩和表示血管网络，支持疾病诊断和血管合成。

idea_spark.idea: 将稀疏卷积与变分自编码器结合，实现高压缩率且保留语义信息。
idea_spark.risk: 稀疏结构可能丢失细节；泛化到不同血管形态需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据固有稀疏性设计高效模型，启发其他医学图像压缩任务。

en.tldr: Proposes VAEsselSparse, an efficient encoder-decoder model leveraging sparse convolutions and attention to encode entire organ-level vascular networks with 8×8×8 spatial compression while retaining clinically discriminative features.
en.motivation: Current methods struggle to analyze whole organ-level vascular networks at clinical resolution due to computational cost.
en.method: Encoder-decoder model using sparse convolutions and attention mechanisms, exploiting the inherent sparsity of 3D vascular structures.
en.result: Superior reconstruction performance over dense counterparts and prior methods; latent space retains clinically relevant features for classification and generation.
en.help_to_user: Helps users efficiently compress and represent vascular networks for disease diagnosis and realistic vasculature synthesis.

idea_spark.idea: Combining sparse convolutions with variational autoencoders achieves high compression while preserving semantic information.
idea_spark.risk: Sparse structures may lose fine details; generalization to diverse vessel morphologies needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging data sparsity for efficient model design inspires other medical image compression tasks.

papers.title: Statistical Inference of Day-to-Day Traffic Dynamics
papers.summary: Day-to-day traffic dynamics are widely used to model flow evolution due to travelers' learning and adjustment behavior, yet empirical analysis of these models often relies on descriptive calibration with limited inferential content. This paper develops a statistical inference framework for day-to-day route choice dynamics based on a stochastic individual-level adjustment model. The framework enables uncertainty quantification and formal inference for behavioral parameters from trajectory data. We establish identifiability and consistency under mild conditions, and extend the framework to accommodate demand variation, user heterogeneity through a hierarchical structure, and anonymized observability caused by privacy constraints on trajectory data. Simulation studies demonstrate good finite-sample performance, calibrated uncertainty, and robustness to model misspecification. Empirical analyses of controlled laboratory experiments and real-world trajectory data from Ann Arbor, Michigan, show that the framework can generate novel behavioral insights across settings: it reveals the inadequacy of a purely inter-day learning model once en-route information is introduced, recovers systematic behavioral differences across participant types, and uncovers meaningful day-to-day learning together with substantial demand variation in real-world commuting behavior.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于随机个体调整模型的日间交通动态统计推断框架，实现行为参数的不确定性量化与正式推断，并通过模拟和实证验证其有效性。
ai.motivation: 现有日间交通动态模型多依赖描述性校准，缺乏推断能力，无法量化不确定性或进行正式统计检验。
ai.method: 构建随机个体层面的路径选择调整模型，利用轨迹数据进行参数推断；通过可识别性分析和一致性证明保证理论性质；引入层次结构处理异质性，并考虑隐私约束下的匿名观测。
ai.result: 模拟显示良好的有限样本性能和稳健性；实验室实验和真实轨迹数据（密歇根州安娜堡）揭示：引入途中信息后纯日间学习模型失效，恢复不同参与者类型的系统行为差异，并发现通勤行为中日间学习与需求变化的显著作用。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助交通研究者或工程师从轨迹数据中可靠地估计行为参数，量化不确定性，并理解日间交通演化的驱动因素。

idea_spark.idea: 将层次结构扩展至多源数据融合（如手机信令、GPS轨迹、问卷调查），以更全面地刻画异质性并提升推断精度。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设（如随机调整形式）可能不适用于所有场景，且隐私约束下的匿名化可能导致信息损失，影响推断效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴计量经济学中的面板数据方法，结合贝叶斯推断，可进一步处理缺失数据或部分观测问题，增强框架实用性。

en.tldr: Proposes a statistical inference framework for day-to-day traffic dynamics based on a stochastic individual-level adjustment model, enabling uncertainty quantification and formal inference of behavioral parameters from trajectory data, validated through simulations and empirical studies.
en.motivation: Existing day-to-day traffic models rely on descriptive calibration with limited inferential content, lacking uncertainty quantification and formal statistical testing.
en.method: Develops a stochastic individual-level route choice adjustment model for trajectory data; establishes identifiability and consistency; extends to demand variation, user heterogeneity via hierarchical structure, and anonymized observability under privacy constraints.
en.result: Simulations show good finite-sample performance and robustness; empirical analyses (lab experiments and real-world data from Ann Arbor, MI) reveal inadequacy of pure inter-day learning with en-route information, recover systematic behavioral differences across participant types, and uncover meaningful day-to-day learning with substantial demand variation in commuting.
en.help_to_user: Helps traffic researchers or practitioners reliably estimate behavioral parameters from trajectory data, quantify uncertainty, and understand drivers of day-to-day traffic evolution.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the hierarchical structure to fuse multi-source data (e.g., mobile phone signals, GPS trajectories, surveys) to better capture heterogeneity and improve inference accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions (e.g., specific adjustment form) may not hold in all settings, and anonymization under privacy constraints could cause information loss, affecting inference quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing on panel data methods from econometrics combined with Bayesian inference could further handle missing data or partial observations, enhancing framework practicality.

papers.title: Genealogical structures under interactive neutral reproduction: factorial moment duality via a Frankenstein process
papers.summary: We establish a genealogical framework for an existing analytical moment duality between a Wright--Fisher type SDE and a counting process with interaction. To achieve this, we construct a finite-population Moran model featuring interactive neutral reproduction as a novel mechanism. In the corresponding events, an individual, regardless of its own type, can only reproduce if a randomly encountered partner is of the ``fit'' type. This Moran model has a relatively simple counting process as its factorial moment dual, whose genealogical meaning appears to be cryptic: after all, the line-counting process of the natural genealogical process of the model, namely the ancestral influence graph (AIG), exhibits a complex hierarchical structure not reflected in the factorial moment dual. Since moment duality is a property in expectation, we are allowed to systematically remove information from the AIG and merge different realizations of the ancestry. We call the result the \emph{Frankenstein process}. Based on this, we establish the factorial moment duality from a genealogical perspective. The moment duality in the diffusion limit follows in a natural way.

ai.tldr: 通过构建一个“弗兰肯斯坦过程”，从谱系角度解释了Wright-Fisher型随机微分方程与计数过程之间的阶乘矩对偶性。
ai.motivation: 现有的阶乘矩对偶性在分析上已经建立，但其谱系意义尚不明确，需要从谱系角度进行解释。
ai.method: 构建了一个具有交互中性繁殖机制的有限种群Moran模型，并基于其祖先影响图（AIG）通过信息移除和合并不同实现，构造了弗兰肯斯坦过程。
ai.result: 利用弗兰肯斯坦过程，从谱系角度建立了阶乘矩对偶性，并自然推广到扩散极限下的矩对偶。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解交互中性繁殖模型中的谱系结构与矩对偶之间的关系。

idea_spark.idea: 通过构造一个简化的谱系过程（弗兰肯斯坦过程）来揭示复杂谱系结构中的矩对偶关系。
idea_spark.risk: 简化过程可能丢失部分谱系细节，需验证对偶性在简化后是否仍然成立。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从矩对偶的期望性质出发，允许系统性地移除谱系信息，启发了一种新的谱系简化策略。

zh.tldr: 通过构建一个“弗兰肯斯坦过程”，从谱系角度解释了Wright-Fisher型随机微分方程与计数过程之间的阶乘矩对偶性。
zh.motivation: 现有的阶乘矩对偶性在分析上已经建立，但其谱系意义尚不明确，需要从谱系角度进行解释。
zh.method: 构建了一个具有交互中性繁殖机制的有限种群Moran模型，并基于其祖先影响图（AIG）通过信息移除和合并不同实现，构造了弗兰肯斯坦过程。
zh.result: 利用弗兰肯斯坦过程，从谱系角度建立了阶乘矩对偶性，并自然推广到扩散极限下的矩对偶。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解交互中性繁殖模型中的谱系结构与矩对偶之间的关系。

idea_spark.idea: 通过构造一个简化的谱系过程（弗兰肯斯坦过程）来揭示复杂谱系结构中的矩对偶关系。
idea_spark.risk: 简化过程可能丢失部分谱系细节，需验证对偶性在简化后是否仍然成立。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从矩对偶的期望性质出发，允许系统性地移除谱系信息，启发了一种新的谱系简化策略。

en.tldr: By constructing a 'Frankenstein process', we explain the factorial moment duality between a Wright-Fisher type SDE and a counting process from a genealogical perspective.
en.motivation: The existing factorial moment duality is established analytically, but its genealogical meaning remains cryptic and needs to be interpreted from a genealogical viewpoint.
en.method: We construct a finite-population Moran model with interactive neutral reproduction, and based on its ancestral influence graph (AIG), we systematically remove information and merge different realizations of ancestry to obtain the Frankenstein process.
en.result: Using the Frankenstein process, we establish the factorial moment duality from a genealogical perspective, which naturally extends to the moment duality in the diffusion limit.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the relationship between genealogical structures and moment duality in models with interactive neutral reproduction.

idea_spark.idea: Construct a simplified genealogical process (Frankenstein process) to reveal moment duality relationships in complex genealogical structures.
idea_spark.risk: The simplification may lose some genealogical details; it needs to be verified that the duality still holds after simplification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the expectation property of moment duality to systematically remove genealogical information inspires a new strategy for simplifying genealogies.

papers.title: Improving Imbalanced Multi-Label Chest X-Ray Diagnosis via CBAM-Enhanced CNN Backbones
papers.summary: Chest radiography is a widely used imaging modality for thoracic disease diagnosis, yet its conventional interpretation remains time-consuming and heavily dependent on expert knowledge. While deep learning has improved diagnostic efficiency through automated feature extraction, challenges such as class imbalance and the localization of multiple co-existing pathologies remain unsolved. In this paper, inspired by the strength of Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) in feature refinement and the capability of CNN blocks in feature extraction, we propose a strategy to integrate CBAM into traditional CNN blocks to enhance performance in multi-label classification tasks. Our method achieves a mean AUC of 0.8695 on ChestXray14 dataset, outperforming several state-of-the-art baselines.Our source code is available at: https://github.com/NNNguyenDuyyy/FETC_CBAM_Enhanced_CNN.git

ai.tldr: 提出将卷积块注意力模块（CBAM）集成到传统CNN骨干中，以改善胸部X光多标签分类中的类别不平衡和多病灶定位问题。
ai.motivation: 胸部X光诊断中类别不平衡和多种共存病灶的定位仍是挑战。
ai.method: 在CNN块中嵌入CBAM模块，增强特征细化能力，用于多标签分类。
ai.result: 在ChestXray14数据集上平均AUC达0.8695，优于多个基线方法。
ai.help_to_user: 可帮助用户理解如何通过注意力机制提升医学影像多标签分类性能。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将CBAM与不同CNN架构（如ResNet、DenseNet）结合，并针对类别不平衡设计损失函数。
idea_spark.risk: CBAM可能增加计算开销，且对极端不平衡数据集的泛化能力需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 注意力机制与CNN的融合思路可启发其他特征细化方法（如自注意力、通道注意力）在医学影像中的应用。

idea_spark.inspiration: 注意力机制与CNN的融合思路可启发其他特征细化方法（如自注意力、通道注意力）在医学影像中的应用。

en.tldr: Proposes integrating Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into traditional CNN backbones to improve multi-label chest X-ray classification under class imbalance and multiple co-existing pathologies.
en.motivation: Class imbalance and localization of multiple co-existing pathologies remain challenges in chest X-ray diagnosis.
en.method: Embed CBAM into CNN blocks to enhance feature refinement for multi-label classification.
en.result: Achieves mean AUC of 0.8695 on ChestXray14 dataset, outperforming several state-of-the-art baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how attention mechanisms can boost multi-label classification performance in medical imaging.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining CBAM with different CNN architectures (e.g., ResNet, DenseNet) and designing loss functions for class imbalance.
idea_spark.risk: CBAM may increase computational overhead, and generalization to extremely imbalanced datasets needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: The fusion of attention mechanisms with CNNs can inspire other feature refinement methods (e.g., self-attention, channel attention) in medical imaging.

papers.title: Uncertainty-Aware Trip Purpose Inference from GPS Trajectories via POI Semantic Zones and Pareto Calibration
papers.summary: Large-scale GPS trajectory data offer rich observations of human mobility, yet assigning trip purposes to detected stops remains challenging due to the absence of individual-level ground truth, spatial uncertainty from GPS noise and incomplete points of interest (POIs) coverage, and fundamental behavioral differences across trip purposes. We propose a weakly supervised framework integrating neighborhood-level POI semantic zones with distance-weighted spatial likelihoods, differentiated inference strategies for mandatory and non-mandatory activities, and a multi-phase Pareto optimization that jointly minimizes distributional divergence from household travel survey statistics and maximizes inference reliability without requiring annotated labels. Evaluated on over 81 million staypoints in Los Angeles, the framework reduces activity type frequency Jensen-Shannon distance (JSD) by 23%, start time JSD by 48%, and duration JSD by 12% respectively relative to a comparable baseline. The proposed approach provides a scalable and uncertainty-aware path from raw GPS trajectories to semantically annotated mobility data for travel demand modeling and transportation policy analysis.

ai.tldr: 提出一种弱监督框架，利用POI语义区域和帕累托校准从GPS轨迹中推断出行目的，无需标注数据。
ai.motivation: 大规模GPS轨迹数据缺乏个体级真值、存在空间不确定性和行为差异，难以准确推断出行目的。
ai.method: 集成邻域级POI语义区域与距离加权空间似然，区分强制与非强制活动，并通过多阶段帕累托优化最小化分布差异。
ai.result: 在洛杉矶8100万个停留点上，活动类型频率JSD降低23%，开始时间JSD降低48%，持续时间JSD降低12%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展、不确定性感知的方法，将原始GPS轨迹转化为语义标注的出行数据，支持交通需求建模和政策分析。

idea_spark.idea: 结合帕累托优化与弱监督学习，无需标注即可校准推断结果，适用于缺乏真值标签的时空数据任务。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖POI数据的完整性和家庭出行调查的统计分布，数据偏差可能影响泛化性能。

en.tldr: A weakly supervised framework that infers trip purposes from GPS trajectories using POI semantic zones and Pareto calibration without labeled data.
en.motivation: Large-scale GPS trajectory data lack individual-level ground truth, suffer from spatial uncertainty and behavioral differences, making trip purpose inference challenging.
en.method: Integrates neighborhood-level POI semantic zones with distance-weighted spatial likelihoods, differentiates mandatory vs. non-mandatory activities, and uses multi-phase Pareto optimization to minimize distributional divergence.
en.result: On 81 million staypoints in Los Angeles, reduces activity type frequency JSD by 23%, start time JSD by 48%, and duration JSD by 12% over a baseline.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable, uncertainty-aware method to convert raw GPS trajectories into semantically annotated mobility data for travel demand modeling and policy analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Combines Pareto optimization with weak supervision to calibrate inference without labels, applicable to spatiotemporal data tasks lacking ground truth.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on POI data completeness and household travel survey statistics; data biases may affect generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introduces multi-objective optimization into uncertainty-aware weak supervision, offering a new approach for other unlabeled inference problems like activity recognition.

papers.title: Priority Scheduling in the M/G/1 with Preemption Overhead
papers.summary: Virtually all practical settings where preemptive scheduling is employed are susceptible to preemption overhead, and accounting for these overheads is necessary to make informed scheduling design decisions. However, preemption overhead is almost never accounted for in queueing-theoretic analyses of preemptive scheduling policies. This is true even for simple preemptive policies in simple queueing models: even the stability region, let alone the response time distribution, is difficult to analyze under overhead.   In this work, we give the first response time distribution analysis of an M/G/1 under a preemptive scheduling policy with preemption overhead. Specifically, we consider class-based preemptive priority, where a stochastic overhead is incurred when pausing or resuming a job. We derive a recursive formula for the Laplace transform of response time for jobs of any given class, from which all response time moments can be extracted.   Beyond the specific policy and model we analyze, our broader aim is to provide a first step towards a general framework for analyzing queues with preemption overhead. To that end, we perform much of our analysis in a way that applies to a wide variety of overhead models by introducing a new theoretical tool called the job joint transform.

ai.tldr: 首次在M/G/1队列中分析含抢占开销的优先级调度策略的响应时间分布。
ai.motivation: 实际抢占式调度都存在开销，但排队论分析几乎从未考虑，导致设计决策缺乏依据。
ai.method: 引入“作业联合变换”工具，推导出任意类别作业响应时间拉普拉斯变换的递归公式，可提取各阶矩。
ai.result: 给出了含随机抢占开销的基于类别的抢占式优先级调度下响应时间分布的解析结果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解抢占开销对系统响应时间的影响，指导实际调度策略设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将抢占开销建模为随机变量，并利用递归变换分析响应时间。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设可能过于简化实际开销的复杂依赖关系。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在更多排队系统中显式建模操作开销，提升理论实用性。

en.tldr: First analysis of response time distribution in M/G/1 queue under preemptive priority scheduling with preemption overhead.
en.motivation: Preemptive scheduling in practice incurs overhead, yet queueing analyses almost never account for it, hindering informed design.
en.method: Introduces a new tool called job joint transform to derive a recursive formula for the Laplace transform of response time for each job class.
en.result: Provides analytical response time moments for class-based preemptive priority with stochastic overhead.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the impact of preemption overhead on system response times, aiding scheduling design.

idea_spark.idea: Model preemption overhead as random variables and analyze response time via recursive transforms.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions may oversimplify real-world overhead dependencies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Encourages explicit modeling of operational overheads in queueing theory for better practical relevance.

papers.title: Quantitative Estimates for Mean-Field Limits and Correlation Functions through a Duality Framework
papers.summary: We investigate the mean-field limit for interacting particle systems through a duality-based framework and obtain quantitative estimates on the convergence of marginals as well as on correlation functions. In particular, for merely square-integrable interaction forces, we derive the natural fluctuation-scale rate $\mathcal{O}(N^{-1/2})$. By introducing an iterative argument on the hierarchy of dual cumulants, we leverage this bound to recover the optimal mean-field rate $\mathcal{O}(N^{-1})$ and to obtain robust estimates on the dual cumulants, at the expense of corresponding regularity assumptions on the interaction kernel. Finally, using the relation between dual and direct correlations, we transfer these bounds to direct cumulants, yielding refined information on correlations and deviations from chaos.

ai.tldr: 通过基于对偶的框架，研究了相互作用粒子系统的平均场极限，获得了边际分布和相关函数的定量估计。
ai.motivation: 为平均场极限提供定量收敛速率，并深入理解相关函数和混沌偏差。
ai.method: 利用对偶框架和迭代论证处理对偶累积量层次，从波动尺度率推导最优平均场率。
ai.result: 对平方可积相互作用力得到自然波动尺度率 O(N^{-1/2})，在正则性假设下恢复最优平均场率 O(N^{-1})，并给出对偶和直接累积量的稳健估计。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解平均场极限的收敛速率和相关函数结构，适用于多粒子系统的理论分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将累积量层次与对偶方法结合，可能用于研究非平衡统计力学中的相关函数。
idea_spark.risk: 对相互作用核的正则性假设可能限制实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从对偶累积量到直接累积量的转换思路，可启发其他领域中的相关函数分析。

en.tldr: Using a duality-based framework, we study the mean-field limit for interacting particle systems and obtain quantitative estimates on marginals and correlation functions.
en.motivation: To provide quantitative convergence rates for the mean-field limit and to gain insight into correlations and deviations from chaos.
en.method: Leveraging a duality framework and an iterative argument on the hierarchy of dual cumulants, we derive fluctuation-scale rates and recover optimal mean-field rates.
en.result: We obtain the natural fluctuation-scale rate O(N^{-1/2}) for square-integrable interactions, recover the optimal mean-field rate O(N^{-1}) under regularity assumptions, and provide robust estimates on dual and direct cumulants.
en.help_to_user: This helps users understand convergence rates in mean-field limits and correlation structures, useful for theoretical analysis of many-particle systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combining cumulant hierarchies with duality may be applied to study correlation functions in non-equilibrium statistical mechanics.
idea_spark.risk: Regularity assumptions on the interaction kernel may limit practical applicability.
idea_spark.inspiration: The transfer from dual to direct cumulants can inspire correlation analysis in other fields.

papers.title: Quantitative homogenization of elliptic equations with infinitely many scales
papers.summary: In this paper, we develop a general homogenization theory for elliptic equations with coefficients that oscillate periodically at infinitely many scales $\varepsilon = (\varepsilon_1, \varepsilon_2, \cdots) \in (0,1)^\infty$, with $\varepsilon_1>\varepsilon_2>\cdots$ and $\varepsilon_n \to 0$ as $n \to \infty$. Such problems arise naturally in the study of fractal materials and diffusion in fluids. Under suitable scale-separation assumptions, we prove a qualitative homogenization theorem and obtain optimal $L^2$ convergence rates. We also establish interior and boundary Lipschitz estimates that are uniform in $\varepsilon$.

ai.tldr: 本文针对系数在无穷多个周期尺度上振荡的椭圆方程，建立了定量均匀化理论，证明了定性均匀化定理并获得了最优L²收敛速率，同时建立了均匀的Lipschitz估计。
ai.motivation: 研究分形材料和流体扩散中出现的具有无穷多尺度振荡系数的椭圆方程，填补该领域均匀化理论的空白。
ai.method: 在适当的尺度分离假设下，通过多尺度分析和迭代论证，推导定性均匀化定理、最优L²收敛速率以及内边界Lipschitz估计。
ai.result: 证明了定性均匀化定理，得到了最优L²收敛速率，并建立了关于ε一致的内边界Lipschitz估计。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理具有无穷多尺度结构的偏微分方程提供了严格的数学框架和收敛性保证，可用于分形材料建模和流体扩散模拟。

idea_spark.idea: 将无穷多尺度均匀化与机器学习结合，学习尺度分离假设下的有效系数，加速多尺度模拟。
idea_spark.risk: 尺度分离假设在实际问题中可能不严格成立，导致理论结果与数值实验存在偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受分形材料和流体扩散中多尺度现象的启发，将均匀化理论从有限尺度扩展到无穷多尺度情形。

en.tldr: This paper develops a quantitative homogenization theory for elliptic equations with coefficients oscillating periodically at infinitely many scales, proving a qualitative homogenization theorem, optimal L² convergence rates, and uniform Lipschitz estimates.
en.motivation: To address elliptic equations with infinitely many oscillatory scales arising in fractal materials and fluid diffusion, filling a gap in homogenization theory.
en.method: Under suitable scale-separation assumptions, using multiscale analysis and iterative arguments to derive the qualitative homogenization theorem, optimal L² convergence rates, and interior/boundary Lipschitz estimates.
en.result: Proved a qualitative homogenization theorem, obtained optimal L² convergence rates, and established interior and boundary Lipschitz estimates uniform in ε.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework and convergence guarantees for PDEs with infinitely many scales, applicable to modeling fractal materials and diffusion in fluids.

idea_spark.idea: Combine infinite-scale homogenization with machine learning to learn effective coefficients under scale-separation assumptions, accelerating multiscale simulations.
idea_spark.risk: Scale-separation assumptions may not hold strictly in real problems, causing discrepancies between theory and numerical experiments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multiscale phenomena in fractal materials and fluid diffusion, extending homogenization theory from finite to infinitely many scales.

papers.title: Stackelberg-Nash controllability for a multi-objective Stefan problem
papers.summary: We investigate a hierarchical control problem for a one-dimensional Stefan system with localized distributed controls. The setting combines a Stackelberg strategy with a Nash equilibrium among multiple followers, yielding a multi-objective free-boundary problem. The interaction between the hierarchical control and the moving interface results in a nonlinear optimality system, and we show that the original problem reduces to the null controllability of this optimality system. Under suitable geometric conditions on the control regions, we establish a local null controllability result. The proof relies on an observability inequality for a linearized system, obtained through Carleman estimates adapted to the presence of a moving boundary. These results constitute, to the best of our knowledge, the first treatment of a Stefan system within a Stackelberg-Nash framework.

ai.tldr: 研究了一维Stefan系统在局部分布控制下的层次控制问题，结合Stackelberg策略与多个跟随者的Nash均衡，建立了局部零能控性结果。
ai.motivation: 填补Stefan系统在Stackelberg-Nash框架下可控性研究的空白。
ai.method: 将原问题转化为最优性系统的零能控性，利用Carleman估计推导线性化系统的可观测不等式。
ai.result: 在控制区域的适当几何条件下，证明了局部零能控性。
ai.help_to_user: 为多目标自由边界问题的层次控制提供了理论分析工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将Stackelberg-Nash框架应用于相变材料的热控制。
idea_spark.risk: 非线性耦合可能导致可控性条件更严格。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合博弈论与偏微分方程控制，设计多智能体热管理系统。

idea_spark.idea: Apply the Stackelberg-Nash framework to thermal control of phase-change materials.
idea_spark.risk: Nonlinear coupling may impose stricter controllability conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine game theory with PDE control to design multi-agent thermal management systems.

papers.title: Closed Forms for Gaussian Kullback--Leibler Unbalanced Optimal Transport without Coupling Entropy
papers.summary: We obtain an explicit solution for the static Kullback--Leibler (KL) unbalanced optimal transport problem between finite non-degenerate Gaussian measures with quadratic cost, two independent positive marginal relaxation parameters, and no entropy penalty on the coupling. The minimizer is a scaled Wasserstein coupling between two adjusted Gaussian marginals and is supported on an affine graph; in entropic Gaussian unbalanced transport, by contrast, the optimal plan is non-degenerate on the product space. The covariance map is the unique positive definite solution of a Riccati equation and admits a principal-square-root representation. Compared with the known equal-penalty Gaussian Hellinger--Kantorovich endpoint, the result treats the asymmetric two-sided Kullback--Leibler relaxation and gives the modified marginals, joint minimizer, value, and a direct quadratic KL-dual certificate. The large-relaxation limit recovers the Gaussian Wasserstein cost for equal masses.

ai.tldr: 本文给出了有限非退化高斯测度之间静态KL非平衡最优传输问题的显式解，其中无耦合熵惩罚。
ai.motivation: 现有高斯Hellinger-Kantorovich结果仅处理等惩罚情形，而实际应用中常需非对称双边的KL松弛，且无耦合熵惩罚的显式解尚缺。
ai.method: 通过求解Riccati方程得到协方差映射的唯一正定解，并利用主平方根表示，构造了缩放Wasserstein耦合。
ai.result: 最优计划是仿射图上的缩放Wasserstein耦合，协方差映射满足Riccati方程，大松弛极限恢复高斯Wasserstein代价。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了非对称KL松弛下高斯非平衡最优传输的闭式解，便于在机器学习、统计推断中直接应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将无熵惩罚的KL非平衡传输与缩放Wasserstein耦合结合，可能简化计算。
idea_spark.risk: 仅适用于高斯测度，非高斯情形需另寻方法。
idea_spark.inspiration: 考虑将主平方根表示用于其他协方差结构优化问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将无熵惩罚的KL非平衡传输与缩放Wasserstein耦合结合，可能简化计算。
idea_spark.risk: 仅适用于高斯测度，非高斯情形需另寻方法。
idea_spark.inspiration: 考虑将主平方根表示用于其他协方差结构优化问题。

en.tldr: This paper obtains an explicit solution for the static KL unbalanced optimal transport problem between finite non-degenerate Gaussian measures without coupling entropy. The minimizer is a scaled Wasserstein coupling supported on an affine graph, and the covariance map solves a Riccati equation.
en.motivation: Existing Gaussian Hellinger-Kantorovich results only handle equal penalties, while asymmetric two-sided KL relaxation without coupling entropy is needed in practice but lacks explicit solutions.
en.method: The covariance map is obtained as the unique positive definite solution of a Riccati equation via a principal-square-root representation, leading to a scaled Wasserstein coupling.
en.result: The optimal plan is a scaled Wasserstein coupling on an affine graph; the covariance map satisfies a Riccati equation; the large-relaxation limit recovers the Gaussian Wasserstein cost.
en.help_to_user: Provides closed-form solutions for Gaussian unbalanced optimal transport with asymmetric KL relaxation, enabling direct application in machine learning and statistical inference.

idea_spark.idea: Combining KL unbalanced transport without coupling entropy with scaled Wasserstein coupling may simplify computations.
idea_spark.risk: The method is limited to Gaussian measures; non-Gaussian cases require alternative approaches.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider using the principal-square-root representation for other covariance optimization problems.

papers.title: Developing a Strong Pre-Trained Base Model for Plant Leaf Disease Classification
papers.summary: Plants, crops and their yields are essential to our very existence, but diseases and pests cause large losses every year. As such it is vital to ensure that diseases can be spotted early and treated accordingly and stopping the spread while still possible. Manual and traditional methods require personal to walk through the field and check for symptoms 'by hand'. This is very laborious and very time consuming, so ML methods have been applied as a result and they have garnered promising results. CNN models are especially efficient as they can automatically extract features from images without any manual feature construction before then feeding the features to a classifier. Datasets are largely influential to the final performance of the model. Despite the importance that datasets pose to the field, there still seems to be somewhat of a discrepancy between what is publicly available for use and what would be required to sufficiently train fully capable models. To overcome these shortcomings, as part of this thesis open datasets for the field of plant leaf disease classification have been identified as well as models that can be trained on them and extensive benchmarks have been carried out to identify their suitability. Then a new dataset was constructed based on those findings as well as on the findings of a augmentation applicability study, which will be used to train a new Base Model based on the DenseNet201 architecture, which managed to outperform the baseline model on said new dataset as well as outperforming it on plant leaf disease classification domain specific Transfer-Learning experiments on another new dataset. This new model manages to train models through Transfer-Learning (TL) faster, more robust, more stable, and with less data than general model would, overcoming a large number of issues that the field still suffers from.

ai.tldr: 本文针对植物叶片病害分类领域，构建了一个基于DenseNet201的预训练基础模型，并通过新数据集和迁移学习实验验证其性能优于基线模型。
ai.motivation: 现有公开数据集不足以训练出高性能模型，且传统人工检测耗时费力，亟需一种更高效、鲁棒的自动分类方法。
ai.method: 首先识别并基准测试现有开放数据集，然后基于数据增强研究构建新数据集，最后使用DenseNet201架构训练基础模型，并通过迁移学习进行微调。
ai.result: 新模型在新数据集上表现优于基线模型，在领域特定的迁移学习实验中训练更快、更稳定、更鲁棒，且所需数据更少。
ai.help_to_user: 为植物病害分类提供了一种高效的基础模型和数据集构建策略，可帮助用户快速部署高精度分类系统。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将自监督学习或对比学习用于植物病害特征提取，以进一步减少对标注数据的依赖。

idea_spark.inspiration: 结合多模态数据（如光谱图像或环境传感器数据）可能提升早期病害检测的准确性。

en.tldr: This paper develops a strong pre-trained base model for plant leaf disease classification using DenseNet201, outperforming baselines on a new dataset and transfer learning experiments.
en.motivation: Existing public datasets are insufficient for training high-performance models, and manual inspection is labor-intensive, necessitating an efficient and robust automated classification method.
en.method: Identified and benchmarked open datasets, constructed a new dataset based on augmentation studies, and trained a DenseNet201 base model fine-tuned via transfer learning.
en.result: The new model outperformed the baseline on the new dataset and achieved faster, more stable, and more robust training with less data in domain-specific transfer learning experiments.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective base model and dataset construction strategy for plant disease classification, enabling users to deploy high-accuracy systems quickly.

idea_spark.idea: Explore self-supervised or contrastive learning for plant disease feature extraction to further reduce reliance on labeled data.
idea_spark.risk: The new dataset may still suffer from class imbalance or limited environmental diversity, affecting model generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating multimodal data (e.g., spectral images or environmental sensors) could improve early disease detection accuracy.

papers.title: Linear Decision Tree Policies for Integer Linear Programs
papers.summary: We study optimal decision policies for integer linear programs with a fixed feasible set and varying cost vectors, represented as linear decision trees. Once synthesized for a given feasible set, they return an optimal solution for any queried cost vector through a sequence of linear tests. We show that there exists a policy performing this operation in a polynomial number of arithmetic operations in the worst case. Along with this theoretical guarantee, we develop a practical construction framework to synthesize policies within a specific subclass of linear decision trees. Our computational experiments show that, although policy synthesis can be time-intensive, it allows retrieving optimal solutions orders of magnitude faster than classical and specialized solution methods on repeated queries. Overall, this paradigm provides a new perspective on the complexity of integer linear programs and offers an offline--online approach for solving them.

ai.tldr: 提出线性决策树策略，用于在固定可行集、变化成本向量的整数线性规划中快速返回最优解。
ai.motivation: 传统整数线性规划求解方法在重复查询时效率低下，需要一种离线-在线范式来加速。
ai.method: 构建线性决策树策略，通过一系列线性测试在多项式时间内返回最优解，并开发了实际合成框架。
ai.result: 策略合成耗时，但重复查询时比经典方法快数个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用离线预计算加速在线整数线性规划求解。

idea_spark.idea: 将决策树与机器学习结合，自动学习最优测试序列，减少人工设计。
idea_spark.risk: 策略合成计算成本高，可能不适用于可行集频繁变化的问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受离线-在线算法和决策树分类器启发，将决策树用于优化决策。

en.tldr: Proposes linear decision tree policies for integer linear programs with fixed feasible sets and varying cost vectors, enabling optimal solutions in polynomial time. A practical synthesis framework is developed, showing orders-of-magnitude speedup on repeated queries.

en.method: Constructs linear decision tree policies that return optimal solutions via a sequence of linear tests in polynomial time, with a practical synthesis framework.
