en.tldr: FT-RAG decomposes tables into entry-level semantic units to build a structured graph, uses structural neighbor expansion and multi-modal fusion, achieving significant gains in complex table reasoning.
en.motivation: Conventional RAG systems underperform on structured tabular data due to coarse retrieval granularity and insufficient table semantic comprehension.
en.method: Decompose tables into entry-level semantic units to construct a structured graph; employ structural neighbor expansion for graph retrieval and multi-modal fusion to consolidate context.
en.result: Improves table-level and cell-level Hit Rates by 23.5% and 59.2%, and exact value accuracy recall by 62.2%, establishing new SOTA on mixed-modality documents.
en.help_to_user: Enables more accurate retrieval and generation of factual answers from complex tables, especially for cross-table and table-text fusion reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Combine table semantic units with graph neural networks for dynamic context expansion, potentially improving multi-hop reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Computational overhead of graph construction and neighbor expansion may be high; robustness to diverse table structures (e.g., nested tables) needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by entity linking and path reasoning in knowledge graphs, treating tables as a special kind of knowledge graph for retrieval augmentation.

papers.title: Deep neural networks with Fisher vector encoding for medical image classification
papers.summary: Orderless encoding methods have shown to improve Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image classification in the context of limited availability of data. Additionally, hybrid CNN + Vision Transformers (ViT) models have been recently proposed to address CNN locality bias issues. These models outperformed CNN-only approaches. Despite that, the integration of such hybrid models with more elaborated feature representation can be highly beneficial and remains large unexplored in the literature. In this context, we propose the introduction of an orderless encoding method, Fisher Vectors, to hybrid CNN + ViT architectures, aiming at achieving a model suitable for both small and large datasets. Such enconding method relies on estimating a Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) on image features. In large datasets, computational costs of the GMM estimation is a limiting factor for the application of Fisher Vectors. Thus, we propose a method to limit the growth of GMM estimation costs as we increase the size of the dataset. We explore the feasibility of our method in the context of medical image classification by appling it to MedMNIST (v2), Clean-CC-CCII and ISIC2018. This collection of datasets contains a wide variety of data scales and modalities. We outperform benchmark results in all MedMNIST (v2) datasets and obtain literature-competitive results in Clean-CC-CCII and ISIC2018.

ai.tldr: 提出将Fisher向量编码引入混合CNN+ViT架构，用于医学图像分类，在多个数据集上取得领先结果。
ai.motivation: 现有混合CNN+ViT模型未充分利用有序编码方法，且Fisher向量在大数据集上计算成本高。
ai.method: 在CNN+ViT中插入Fisher向量编码层，并设计高斯混合模型估计成本控制策略。
ai.result: 在MedMNIST v2所有数据集上超越基准，在Clean-CC-CCII和ISIC2018上达到竞争性结果。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种适用于小样本和大规模医学图像分类的混合模型方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将Fisher向量与自注意力机制结合，替代传统池化层。
idea_spark.risk: GMM估计在超大数据集上仍可能成为瓶颈，需进一步优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受自然语言处理中词袋模型启发，将无序编码引入视觉Transformer。

en.tldr: Proposes integrating Fisher vector encoding into hybrid CNN+ViT architectures for medical image classification, achieving state-of-the-art results on multiple datasets.
en.motivation: Existing hybrid CNN+ViT models underutilize orderless encoding methods, and Fisher vectors suffer from high computational cost on large datasets.
en.method: Inserts Fisher vector encoding layer into CNN+ViT and designs a cost-control strategy for Gaussian mixture model estimation.
en.result: Outperforms benchmarks on all MedMNIST v2 datasets and achieves competitive results on Clean-CC-CCII and ISIC2018.
en.help_to_user: Provides a hybrid model suitable for both small and large-scale medical image classification.

idea_spark.idea: Explore replacing traditional pooling layers with Fisher vectors combined with self-attention.
idea_spark.risk: GMM estimation may still become a bottleneck on extremely large datasets, requiring further optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by bag-of-words models in NLP, introducing orderless encoding into visual transformers.

papers.title: A Light Weight Multi-Features-View Convolution Neural Network For Plant Disease Identification
papers.summary: Agriculture is a key sector of the economies of developing countries. It serves as a primary source of income and employment for rural populations. However, each year, a large portion of crops is wasted because of pests and diseases. Well-timed prediction of plant diseases is crucial to sustainable, high-quality agricultural production. Detection of plant diseases through conventional methods is both labour-intensive and time-consuming. Researchers have developed image classification based automated techniques for this purpose. Most accurate methods are based on deep convolutional neural networks, which are computationally intensive, with many layers and millions of trainable parameters. In resource-constrained settings, especially in rural areas, it is difficult to deploy deep convolutional neural network models for efficient plant disease identification. To address these issues, an efficient and light-weight Multi-View Convolutional Neural Network is proposed. These additional features aid the proposed model to identify the plant diseases accurately and efficiently with less number of parameters. The proposed model is tested on a benchmark Plantvillage dataset and achieves an improvement of $ 2.9\%$ in classification accuracy compared to the baseline convolutional neural network model, which was trained only on Red, Green, and Blue (RGB) plant images. Compared with state-of-the-art deep convolutional neural network models, the proposed model is less computationally expensive and achieves comparable accuracy for plant disease identification on the PlantVillage dataset.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级多视角卷积神经网络，在资源受限环境下高效识别植物病害，在PlantVillage数据集上准确率提升2.9%。
ai.motivation: 传统深度CNN模型计算量大、参数多，难以部署在农村等资源受限环境，需要轻量高效的植物病害识别方法。
ai.method: 设计轻量多视角卷积神经网络，融合RGB图像外的额外特征，减少参数数量同时保持识别精度。
ai.result: 在PlantVillage基准数据集上，相比仅用RGB图像的基线CNN，分类准确率提升2.9%，且计算成本低于现有深度CNN模型。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解如何在计算资源有限的情况下实现高效的植物病害自动识别。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在边缘设备（如手机、无人机）上部署该模型，实现实时田间病害诊断。
idea_spark.risk: 额外特征的获取可能增加数据采集成本，且模型在真实复杂背景下的泛化能力需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受资源受限场景启发，思考如何平衡模型复杂度与识别精度，推动AI在农业领域的实际落地。

en.tldr: Proposes a lightweight multi-view convolutional neural network for efficient plant disease identification in resource-constrained settings, achieving 2.9% accuracy improvement on PlantVillage dataset.
en.motivation: Deep CNNs are computationally intensive with many parameters, making them difficult to deploy in rural areas; a lightweight yet accurate model is needed.
en.method: Designs a lightweight multi-view CNN that incorporates additional features beyond RGB images, reducing parameters while maintaining accuracy.
en.result: On the PlantVillage benchmark dataset, the proposed model improves classification accuracy by 2.9% over a baseline CNN trained only on RGB images, and is less computationally expensive than state-of-the-art deep CNNs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to achieve efficient plant disease identification under limited computational resources.

idea_spark.idea: Explore deploying the model on edge devices like smartphones or drones for real-time field disease diagnosis.
idea_spark.risk: Acquiring additional features may increase data collection cost, and the model's generalization in real complex backgrounds needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by resource-constrained scenarios, think about balancing model complexity and accuracy to promote practical AI deployment in agriculture.

papers.title: SAIL: Structure-Aware Interpretable Learning for Anatomy-Aligned Post-hoc Explanations in OCT
papers.summary: Optical coherence tomography (OCT), a commonly used retinal imaging modality, plays a central role in retinal disease diagnosis by providing high-resolution visualization of retinal layers. While deep learning (DL) has achieved expert-level accuracy in OCT-based retinal disease detection, its "black box" nature poses challenges for clinical adoption, where explainability is essential for clinical trust and regulatory approval. Existing post-hoc explainable AI (XAI) methods often struggle to delineate fine-grained lesion structures, respect anatomical boundaries, or suppress noise, limiting the trustworthiness of their explanations. To bridge these gaps, we propose a Structure-Aware Interpretable Learning (SAIL) framework that integrates retinal anatomical priors at the representation level and couples them with semantic features via a fusion design. Without modifying standard post-hoc explainability methods, this representation yields sharper and more anatomically aligned attribution maps. Comprehensive experiments on diverse OCT datasets demonstrate that our structure-aware method consistently enhances interpretability, producing clinically meaningful and anatomy-aware explanations. Ablation studies further show that strong interpretability requires both structural priors and semantic features, and that properly fusing the two is critical to achieve the best explanation quality. Together, these results highlight structure-aware representations as a key step toward reliable explainability in OCT.

ai.tldr: 提出SAIL框架，通过融合视网膜解剖先验与语义特征，在不修改现有事后可解释方法的前提下，生成更清晰、解剖对齐的归因图，提升OCT解释的临床可信度。
ai.motivation: 现有事后XAI方法在OCT中难以描绘精细病变结构、尊重解剖边界或抑制噪声，限制了临床信任。
ai.method: 在表示层面整合视网膜解剖先验，并通过融合设计与语义特征耦合，增强标准事后可解释方法的输出。
ai.result: 在多个OCT数据集上，SAIL持续产生更清晰、解剖对齐的归因图，消融实验表明结构先验与语义特征均不可或缺，且融合方式至关重要。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解OCT深度学习模型决策的解剖合理性，提升临床可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 将解剖先验作为可学习的软约束嵌入表示空间，而非硬编码，以自适应不同病变形态。
idea_spark.risk: 解剖先验的获取可能依赖专家标注或分割模型，引入额外偏差；融合设计可能增加计算复杂度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受视网膜层状结构启发，可推广至其他具有明确解剖层次的组织（如皮肤、血管）的解释性增强。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解OCT深度学习模型决策的解剖合理性，提升临床可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 将解剖先验作为可学习的软约束嵌入表示空间，而非硬编码，以自适应不同病变形态。
idea_spark.risk: 解剖先验的获取可能依赖专家标注或分割模型，引入额外偏差；融合设计可能增加计算复杂度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受视网膜层状结构启发，可推广至其他具有明确解剖层次的组织（如皮肤、血管）的解释性增强。

en.tldr: Proposes SAIL, a framework that integrates retinal anatomical priors with semantic features at the representation level to produce sharper, anatomy-aligned attribution maps for OCT without modifying existing post-hoc explainability methods.
en.motivation: Existing post-hoc XAI methods struggle to delineate fine lesion structures, respect anatomical boundaries, or suppress noise in OCT, limiting clinical trust.
en.method: Incorporates retinal anatomical priors at the representation level and couples them with semantic features via a fusion design to enhance standard post-hoc explainability outputs.
en.result: On multiple OCT datasets, SAIL consistently yields sharper, anatomy-aligned attribution maps; ablation studies confirm both structural priors and semantic features are necessary, and their fusion is critical.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the anatomical plausibility of deep learning model decisions in OCT, improving clinical interpretability.

idea_spark.idea: Embed anatomical priors as learnable soft constraints in the representation space rather than hard-coded rules, to adapt to diverse lesion morphologies.
idea_spark.risk: Acquiring anatomical priors may rely on expert annotations or segmentation models, introducing bias; fusion design may increase computational complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by retinal layered structure, can generalize to other tissues with clear anatomical hierarchies (e.g., skin, blood vessels) for enhanced interpretability.

papers.title: Recall to Predict: Grounding Motion Forecasting in Interpretable Motion Bank
papers.summary: Motion forecasting often requires trading interpretability for predictive accuracy. Standard anchor-based architectures rely on opaque latent queries that are highly prone to latent collapse, or naive trajectory sampling that limits multi-modal diversity. We propose an end-to-end differentiable framework that grounds predictions in a comprehensive "motion bank", a structured embedding space of physically realizable trajectories constructed via contrastive learning. Rather than regressing paths from a blank slate, our architecture dynamically retrieves explicit motion priors using a novel Anchor Retrieval Layer. This module adapts orthogonally initialized queries via a Dual-Level Gated Cross-Attention mechanism and executes discrete trajectory selection using a Straight-Through Gumbel-Softmax estimator to preserve continuous gradient flow. The retrieved semantically grounded anchors are then geometrically refined by a DETR-style decoder, optimized jointly with a Winner-Takes-All (WTA) kinematic Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM), a latent diversity penalty, and a soft-min weighted endpoint loss. By strictly conditioning the decoding phase on diverse, interpretable motion primitives, our approach eliminates the "black box" of standard latent queries while achieving competitive multi-modal accuracy on the Argoverse 2 and Waymo Open Motion datasets. Code is available at: https://github.com/abviv/recall2predict

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于可解释运动库的端到端运动预测框架，通过检索显式运动先验替代黑箱潜变量查询，在保持多模态精度的同时提升可解释性。
ai.motivation: 现有运动预测方法在可解释性与预测精度之间存在权衡：基于锚点的架构使用不透明的潜变量查询易导致潜变量坍塌，而简单轨迹采样限制了多模态多样性。
ai.method: 构建对比学习得到的结构化运动库嵌入空间；设计锚点检索层，通过正交初始化查询和双层门控交叉注意力机制动态检索显式运动先验；使用直通Gumbel-Softmax估计器进行离散轨迹选择；经DETR风格解码器几何精炼，联合WTA运动学GMM损失、潜多样性惩罚和软最小加权端点损失优化。
ai.result: 在Argoverse 2和Waymo Open Motion数据集上达到具有竞争力的多模态预测精度，同时消除了标准潜变量查询的黑箱特性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过显式运动先验检索替代隐式潜变量，在运动预测中实现可解释性与精度的平衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将运动库概念扩展到多智能体交互场景，构建联合运动先验库以捕捉智能体间的依赖关系。
idea_spark.risk: 运动库的构建依赖对比学习，若训练数据分布偏移可能导致检索到的先验不准确；离散选择的Gumbel-Softmax估计可能引入梯度偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类利用经验运动模式进行预测的启发，将先验知识显式编码为可检索的嵌入空间，为可解释AI提供新思路。

zh.motivation: 现有运动预测方法在可解释性与预测精度之间存在权衡：基于锚点的架构使用不透明的潜变量查询易导致潜变量坍塌，而简单轨迹采样限制了多模态多样性。

zh.result: 在Argoverse 2和Waymo Open Motion数据集上达到具有竞争力的多模态预测精度，同时消除了标准潜变量查询的黑箱特性。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过显式运动先验检索替代隐式潜变量，在运动预测中实现可解释性与精度的平衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将运动库概念扩展到多智能体交互场景，构建联合运动先验库以捕捉智能体间的依赖关系。
idea_spark.risk: 运动库的构建依赖对比学习，若训练数据分布偏移可能导致检索到的先验不准确；离散选择的Gumbel-Softmax估计可能引入梯度偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类利用经验运动模式进行预测的启发，将先验知识显式编码为可检索的嵌入空间，为可解释AI提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes an end-to-end motion forecasting framework grounded in an interpretable motion bank, retrieving explicit motion priors instead of opaque latent queries, achieving competitive multi-modal accuracy while enhancing interpretability.
en.motivation: Existing motion forecasting methods trade interpretability for accuracy: anchor-based architectures use opaque latent queries prone to collapse, while naive trajectory sampling limits multi-modal diversity.
en.method: Constructs a structured motion bank embedding space via contrastive learning; designs an Anchor Retrieval Layer with orthogonally initialized queries and Dual-Level Gated Cross-Attention to dynamically retrieve explicit motion priors; uses Straight-Through Gumbel-Softmax for discrete trajectory selection; geometrically refines via a DETR-style decoder, jointly optimized with WTA kinematic GMM loss, latent diversity penalty, and soft-min weighted endpoint loss.
en.result: Achieves competitive multi-modal prediction accuracy on Argoverse 2 and Waymo Open Motion datasets while eliminating the black-box nature of standard latent queries.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how explicit motion prior retrieval can replace implicit latent queries to balance interpretability and accuracy in motion forecasting.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the motion bank concept to multi-agent interaction scenarios by constructing a joint motion prior bank that captures inter-agent dependencies.
idea_spark.risk: Motion bank construction relies on contrastive learning; distribution shift in training data may lead to inaccurate retrieved priors; Gumbel-Softmax estimation for discrete selection may introduce gradient bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human reliance on learned motion patterns, explicitly encoding prior knowledge as a retrievable embedding space offers a new direction for interpretable AI.

papers.title: PointCSP: Cross-Sample Semantic Propagation and Stability Preservation in Self-Supervised Point Cloud Learning
papers.summary: Scene-level point cloud self-supervised learning (PC-SSL) has demonstrated potential in enhancing the generalization capability of 3D vision models. Despite the advances in the field through existing methods, the sample-independent modeling paradigm still poses significant limitations in terms of maintaining consistent semantic representations across scenes. This challenge hinders the construction of a unified and transferable semantic space. To address this issue, we propose a PC-SSL framework based on cross-sample semantic propagation (CSP), in which samples within a batch are serialized into continuous input and processed by a state-space model to enable semantic state propagation. This mechanism explicitly models the dynamic dependencies across samples in the state space, allowing the network to establish cross-sample semantic consistency in the latent space and achieve global semantic alignment. Since serialization-based pretraining requires batch-level input organization, we further introduce an asymmetric semantic preservation distillation (SPD) during finetuning to achieve structural alignment of semantic transfer and eliminate inconsistencies caused by batch dependency. The proposed SPD ensures stable transfer of pretrained semantics through a heterogeneous input mechanism and a semantic feature alignment constraint. This enables the model to maintain structured semantic consistency and robustness under single-scene testing conditions. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both performance and semantic consistency.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于跨样本语义传播的点云自监督学习框架，通过状态空间模型实现样本间语义一致性，并引入非对称语义保持蒸馏消除批次依赖。
ai.motivation: 现有样本独立建模范式难以保持场景间一致的语义表示，阻碍了统一可迁移语义空间的构建。
ai.method: 将批次内样本序列化输入状态空间模型实现语义状态传播，微调时采用非对称语义保持蒸馏实现语义迁移的结构对齐。
ai.result: 在多个基准数据集上，该方法在性能和语义一致性上均优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过跨样本语义传播和蒸馏技术提升点云自监督学习的语义一致性和迁移能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将状态空间模型用于点云自监督学习，通过序列化批次样本实现语义状态传播。
idea_spark.risk: 批次依赖可能导致测试时单样本场景下的语义不一致，需蒸馏技术缓解。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从自然语言处理中的状态空间模型（如Mamba）获得灵感，将其应用于3D点云序列建模。

en.tldr: Proposes a point cloud self-supervised learning framework with cross-sample semantic propagation using a state-space model to achieve semantic consistency across samples, and introduces asymmetric semantic preservation distillation to eliminate batch dependency.
en.motivation: Existing sample-independent modeling paradigms struggle to maintain consistent semantic representations across scenes, hindering the construction of a unified and transferable semantic space.
en.method: Serializes samples within a batch into continuous input processed by a state-space model for semantic state propagation, and uses asymmetric semantic preservation distillation during finetuning for structural alignment of semantic transfer.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple benchmark datasets in both performance and semantic consistency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how cross-sample semantic propagation and distillation techniques improve semantic consistency and transferability in point cloud self-supervised learning.

idea_spark.idea: Applies state-space models to point cloud self-supervised learning by serializing batch samples for semantic state propagation.
idea_spark.risk: Batch dependency may cause semantic inconsistency in single-sample testing scenarios, requiring distillation to mitigate.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by state-space models in natural language processing (e.g., Mamba) for 3D point cloud sequence modeling.

papers.title: LLM Output Detectability and Task Performance Can be Jointly Optimized
papers.summary: Detecting machine-generated text is essential for transparency and accountability when deploying large language models (LLMs). Among detection approaches, watermarking is a statistically reliable method by design -- it embeds detectable signals into LLM outputs by biasing their token distributions. However, it has been reported that watermarked LLMs often perform worse on downstream tasks. We propose PUPPET, a framework that fine-tunes an LLM via reinforcement learning to generate text that is both more detectable and better performing on downstream tasks. We use two reward functions: a detector that outputs a machine-class likelihood and an evaluator that measures a task-specific metric. Experiments on long-form QA, summarization, and essay writing show that LLMs trained with PUPPET achieve high detectability competitive with watermarking methods while outperforming them on downstream tasks. The analysis shows that this optimization can be performed efficiently with only a few thousand samples in 1--2 GPU hours. Moreover, these gains are consistent across out-of-domain tasks, different LLM families, and model sizes, and are even robust to paraphrasing attacks.

ai.tldr: 提出PUPPET框架，通过强化学习联合优化LLM输出的可检测性和下游任务性能。
ai.motivation: 水印方法虽能可靠检测机器生成文本，但会降低LLM在下游任务上的表现。
ai.method: 使用两个奖励函数：检测器输出机器文本概率，评估器衡量任务特定指标，通过强化学习微调LLM。
ai.result: 在长问答、摘要和论文写作任务中，PUPPET训练后的LLM可检测性媲美水印方法，且下游性能更优，仅需数千样本和1-2 GPU小时，对跨域任务、不同模型族和大小均有效，甚至能抵抗释义攻击。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不牺牲任务性能的前提下提升LLM输出的可检测性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将PUPPET框架应用于多模态生成（如图像描述）或对话系统，以同时保证可追溯性和质量。
idea_spark.risk: 强化学习训练可能引入不稳定或过拟合，需谨慎设计奖励函数。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从水印与性能的权衡出发，启发我们重新思考LLM部署中的透明度与实用性关系。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从水印与性能的权衡出发，启发我们重新思考LLM部署中的透明度与实用性关系。

en.tldr: Proposes PUPPET, a framework that jointly optimizes LLM output detectability and downstream task performance via reinforcement learning.
en.motivation: Watermarking reliably detects machine-generated text but often degrades LLM performance on downstream tasks.
en.method: Uses two reward functions: a detector outputting machine-class likelihood and an evaluator measuring task-specific metrics, fine-tuning the LLM with reinforcement learning.
en.result: On long-form QA, summarization, and essay writing, LLMs trained with PUPPET achieve detectability competitive with watermarking while outperforming it on downstream tasks, requiring only a few thousand samples and 1-2 GPU hours, consistent across out-of-domain tasks, model families and sizes, and robust to paraphrasing attacks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to improve LLM output detectability without sacrificing task performance.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applying PUPPET to multimodal generation (e.g., image captioning) or dialogue systems to ensure traceability and quality simultaneously.
idea_spark.risk: Reinforcement learning training may introduce instability or overfitting; careful reward design is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: The trade-off between watermarking and performance inspires rethinking the relationship between transparency and utility in LLM deployment.

papers.title: Human Activity Recognition Method for Moderate Violence Detection
papers.summary: Physical violence in public spaces is a significant public health concern, with minor incidents such as pushing often serving as precursors to more severe escalations. This research develops an automated system for the real-time detection of moderate physical violence, specifically pushing, in surveillance camera footage. The proposed solution integrates state-of-the-art computer vision models, utilizing YOLO11 and YOLO11-Pose for human detection and skeletal keypoint extraction. By calculating body inclination and joint angles between shoulders and hips, a Random Forest classifier was trained to distinguish between normal behavior and aggressive physical contact. The system's performance was evaluated through three progressive case studies representing increasing levels of difficulty. In controlled environments with frontal views, the model achieved a precision of 0.98. In the most challenging scenario, featuring high-altitude, steep-angle recordings from real-world surveillance infrastructure, the system maintained a precision of 0.72 despite significant perspective distortion and visual noise. These results demonstrate the feasibility of using skeletal analysis for early violence intervention in urban security contexts.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于YOLO11和骨骼关键点提取的实时监控视频中推搡行为检测系统，在受控场景下精度达0.98，真实监控场景下为0.72。
ai.motivation: 公共场所的轻微暴力（如推搡）常升级为严重事件，现有系统缺乏对中等暴力程度的实时自动检测。
ai.method: 使用YOLO11和YOLO11-Pose进行人体检测与骨骼关键点提取，计算身体倾斜度和肩髋关节角度，训练随机森林分类器区分正常行为与推搡。
ai.result: 在正面受控场景中精度0.98，在真实监控高角度场景中精度0.72，验证了骨骼分析用于早期暴力干预的可行性。
ai.help_to_user: 可帮助安防人员实时识别监控中的推搡行为，提前预警潜在暴力升级。

idea_spark.idea: 结合时序信息（如LSTM）分析连续帧中的骨骼变化，提升对复杂动作的识别鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 真实监控场景中视角畸变和遮挡可能导致关键点提取失败，降低精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用身体倾斜度作为暴力行为的简单几何特征，启发设计轻量级实时检测方案。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于YOLO11和骨骼关键点提取的实时监控视频中推搡行为检测系统，在受控场景下精度达0.98，真实监控场景下为0.72。

zh.result: 在正面受控场景中精度0.98，在真实监控高角度场景中精度0.72，验证了骨骼分析用于早期暴力干预的可行性。
zh.help_to_user: 可帮助安防人员实时识别监控中的推搡行为，提前预警潜在暴力升级。

idea_spark.idea: 结合时序信息（如LSTM）分析连续帧中的骨骼变化，提升对复杂动作的识别鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 真实监控场景中视角畸变和遮挡可能导致关键点提取失败，降低精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用身体倾斜度作为暴力行为的简单几何特征，启发设计轻量级实时检测方案。

en.tldr: Proposes a real-time pushing detection system using YOLO11 and skeletal keypoints, achieving 0.98 precision in controlled settings and 0.72 in real surveillance footage.
en.motivation: Minor physical violence like pushing often escalates; existing systems lack automated detection for moderate violence in public spaces.
en.method: Uses YOLO11 and YOLO11-Pose for human detection and skeletal keypoint extraction, computes body inclination and shoulder-hip angles, and trains a Random Forest classifier to distinguish pushing from normal behavior.
en.result: Precision of 0.98 in frontal controlled scenarios and 0.72 in real high-angle surveillance footage, demonstrating feasibility of skeletal analysis for early violence intervention.
en.help_to_user: Enables security personnel to detect pushing in real-time surveillance, providing early warning of potential violence escalation.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate temporal information (e.g., LSTM) to analyze skeletal changes across frames, improving robustness for complex actions.
idea_spark.risk: Perspective distortion and occlusion in real surveillance may cause keypoint extraction failures, reducing precision.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using body inclination as a simple geometric feature for violence detection inspires lightweight real-time solutions.

papers.title: Intervention-Based Self-Supervised Learning: A Causal Probe Paradigm for Remote Photoplethysmography
papers.summary: Remote Photoplethysmography (rPPG) enables convenient non-contact physiological measurement. Existing Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) methods commonly fall into a correlation trap: they tend to learn the most dominant periodic signals in the data, such as high-energy motion or illumination noise, rather than the faint, true rPPG signal, leading to poor model generalization. To address this, we propose a new SSL paradigm, Physiological Causal Probing (PCP), which treats the latent rPPG signal as the underlying physical source and the resulting pixel chrominance variations as its visual manifestation. Its core idea is to shift from passive correlation learning to active, precise intervention: it intervenes on the video based on a proposed rPPG hypothesis, and verifies whether the post-intervention changes match physical expectations. We propose the Interv-rPPG framework to implement PCP: an rPPG extractor named PhysMambaFormer hypothesizes the rPPG signal, while a Controllable Physiological Signal Editor conducts precise chrominance-domain interventions on videos based on this hypothesis. Interv-rPPG validates the physical realism of the hypothesis through `Falsifiability via Nulling' and `Axiomatic Equivariance'. Our editor achieves precise editing of the rPPG signal by intervening in the low-frequency chrominance components of the video. Our method improves both in-domain and cross-domain performance on challenging datasets such as VIPL-HR and MMPD. Furthermore, it surpasses the supervised baseline in complex cross-dataset settings, while remaining competitive on clean datasets where the intervention mechanism may introduce slight residual chrominance noise. Extensive experiments, including diagnostic analysis of nuisance sensitivity, demonstrate that the PCP paradigm effectively resists motion and illumination artifacts.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于因果干预的自监督学习范式，通过主动编辑视频色度分量来验证rPPG假设的物理真实性，从而避免相关陷阱。
ai.motivation: 现有自监督rPPG方法容易学到运动或光照噪声等强周期信号，而非真实的微弱rPPG信号，导致泛化差。
ai.method: 设计PhysMambaFormer提取rPPG假设，并用可控生理信号编辑器在视频低频色度分量上实施精确干预，通过“归零可证伪性”和“公理等变性”验证假设。
ai.result: 在VIPL-HR和MMPD等数据集上提升了域内和跨域性能，在复杂跨数据集设置中超越有监督基线，仅在干净数据集上因干预引入轻微残留噪声而略有竞争。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过因果干预而非被动相关来提升rPPG模型的鲁棒性和泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将视频色度分量视为物理信号的视觉表现，通过主动编辑验证假设，类似可解释性中的反事实推理。
idea_spark.risk: 干预机制可能引入额外噪声，在高质量数据上性能略有下降；需要精心设计编辑器以避免破坏信号完整性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断中的do-operator获得灵感，将自监督学习从相关学习转向主动因果验证。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于因果干预的自监督学习范式，通过主动编辑视频色度分量来验证rPPG假设的物理真实性，从而避免相关陷阱。
zh.motivation: 现有自监督rPPG方法容易学到运动或光照噪声等强周期信号，而非真实的微弱rPPG信号，导致泛化差。

zh.result: 在VIPL-HR和MMPD等数据集上提升了域内和跨域性能，在复杂跨数据集设置中超越有监督基线，仅在干净数据集上因干预引入轻微残留噪声而略有竞争。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过因果干预而非被动相关来提升rPPG模型的鲁棒性和泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将视频色度分量视为物理信号的视觉表现，通过主动编辑验证假设，类似可解释性中的反事实推理。
idea_spark.risk: 干预机制可能引入额外噪声，在高质量数据上性能略有下降；需要精心设计编辑器以避免破坏信号完整性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断中的do-operator获得灵感，将自监督学习从相关学习转向主动因果验证。

en.tldr: Proposes a causal intervention-based self-supervised learning paradigm that actively edits video chrominance components to verify the physical realism of rPPG hypotheses, avoiding correlation traps.
en.motivation: Existing self-supervised rPPG methods tend to learn dominant periodic signals like motion or illumination noise instead of the faint true rPPG signal, leading to poor generalization.
en.method: Designs PhysMambaFormer to hypothesize the rPPG signal and a Controllable Physiological Signal Editor to perform precise chrominance-domain interventions on videos, validated via 'Falsifiability via Nulling' and 'Axiomatic Equivariance'.
en.result: Improves in-domain and cross-domain performance on challenging datasets like VIPL-HR and MMPD; surpasses supervised baselines in complex cross-dataset settings, while remaining competitive on clean datasets with slight residual noise from intervention.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how causal intervention rather than passive correlation can improve robustness and generalization of rPPG models.

idea_spark.idea: Treat video chrominance components as visual manifestations of physical signals and actively edit them to test hypotheses, akin to counterfactual reasoning in explainability.
idea_spark.risk: The intervention mechanism may introduce extra noise, causing slight performance drop on high-quality data; careful editor design is needed to avoid signal corruption.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the do-operator in causal inference, shifting self-supervised learning from correlation learning to active causal verification.

papers.title: DiagramNet: An End-to-End Recognition Framework and Dataset for Non-Standard System-Level Diagrams
papers.summary: System-level diagrams encode the architectural blueprint of chip design, specifying module functions, dataflows, and interface protocols. However, non-standardized symbols and the scarcity of structured training data hinder existing multimodal large language models (MLLMs) from recognizing these diagrams. To address this gap, we introduce DiagramNet, the first multimodal dataset for system-level diagrams, comprising 10,977 connection annotations and 15,515 chain-of-thought QA pairs across four tasks: Listing, Localization, Connection, and Circuit QA. Building on this dataset, we propose a progressive training pipeline together with a decoupled multi-agent workflow that decomposes complex visual reasoning into Perception, Reasoning, and Knowledge stages. On the DiagramNet benchmark, integrating our 3B-parameter model with the proposed workflow surpasses the 2025 EDA Elite Challenge winner and outperforms GPT-5, Claude-Sonnet-4, and Gemini-2.5-Pro by over 2x in end-to-end evaluation. Notably, the workflow generalizes beyond our model, boosting Task 1 performance by 128.7x for Gemini-2.5-Pro and 12.4x for GPT-5. Furthermore, with only 60 images for detector adaptation, the method transfers effectively to AMSBench, achieving zero-shot connectivity reasoning on par with GPT-5 and Claude-Sonnet-4 while surpassing the AMS state-of-the-art method Netlistify.

ai.tldr: 提出首个系统级图表多模态数据集DiagramNet和渐进训练流程，结合解耦多智能体工作流，在非标准图表识别上超越GPT-5等模型。
ai.motivation: 现有MLLM难以识别非标准化的系统级图表，缺乏结构化训练数据。
ai.method: 构建DiagramNet数据集（含10977条连接标注和15515条思维链QA），设计渐进训练流程和解耦多智能体工作流（感知-推理-知识三阶段）。
ai.result: 3B参数模型配合工作流在DiagramNet基准上超越EDA竞赛冠军和GPT-5等模型2倍以上；工作流可泛化，使Gemini-2.5-Pro任务1性能提升128.7倍；仅用60张图像即可迁移至AMSBench，零样本连接推理与GPT-5持平。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动识别和推理芯片设计中的非标准系统级图表，提升EDA流程效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂视觉推理分解为感知、推理、知识三个阶段，每个阶段由独立智能体处理。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量思维链标注，泛化至全新图表类型时可能需重新训练。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴人类专家逐步推理过程，将端到端任务拆解为可解释的子任务。

zh.tldr: 提出首个系统级图表多模态数据集DiagramNet和渐进训练流程，结合解耦多智能体工作流，在非标准图表识别上超越GPT-5等模型。

zh.method: 构建DiagramNet数据集（含10977条连接标注和15515条思维链QA），设计渐进训练流程和解耦多智能体工作流（感知-推理-知识三阶段）。
zh.result: 3B参数模型配合工作流在DiagramNet基准上超越EDA竞赛冠军和GPT-5等模型2倍以上；工作流可泛化，使Gemini-2.5-Pro任务1性能提升128.7倍；仅用60张图像即可迁移至AMSBench，零样本连接推理与GPT-5持平。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动识别和推理芯片设计中的非标准系统级图表，提升EDA流程效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂视觉推理分解为感知、推理、知识三个阶段，每个阶段由独立智能体处理。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量思维链标注，泛化至全新图表类型时可能需重新训练。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴人类专家逐步推理过程，将端到端任务拆解为可解释的子任务。

en.tldr: Proposes the first multimodal dataset DiagramNet for system-level diagrams and a progressive training pipeline with a decoupled multi-agent workflow, outperforming GPT-5 and other models on non-standard diagram recognition.
en.motivation: Existing MLLMs struggle with non-standardized system-level diagrams due to lack of structured training data.
en.method: Constructs DiagramNet dataset (10,977 connection annotations and 15,515 chain-of-thought QA pairs), designs a progressive training pipeline and a decoupled multi-agent workflow (Perception, Reasoning, Knowledge stages).
en.result: A 3B-parameter model with the workflow surpasses the EDA competition winner and GPT-5 by over 2x on DiagramNet; the workflow generalizes, boosting Gemini-2.5-Pro Task 1 performance by 128.7x; transfers to AMSBench with only 60 images, achieving zero-shot connectivity reasoning on par with GPT-5.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically recognize and reason about non-standard system-level diagrams in chip design, improving EDA workflow efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose complex visual reasoning into Perception, Reasoning, and Knowledge stages, each handled by an independent agent.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality chain-of-thought annotations; may require retraining for novel diagram types.
idea_spark.inspiration: Mimics human expert step-by-step reasoning by breaking down end-to-end tasks into interpretable sub-tasks.

papers.title: Leveraging Imperfect Medical Data: A Manifold-Consistent Spatio-Temporal Network for Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition (HAR) has attracted increasing attention in medical and healthcare monitoring, particularly with the growth of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). However, in real-world wearable sensing scenarios, IoMT signals are often corrupted by missing measurements, sensor failures, and environmental noise, which significantly degrade the performance of conventional deep learning models that assume clean and complete inputs. To address this challenge, we propose a Manifold-Consistent Spatio-Temporal Network (MCSTN) for robust HAR under imperfect sensing conditions. The proposed framework introduces a dual-level corruption modeling mechanism that simulates realistic sensor imperfections through both physical-level corruption and diffusion-driven continuous corruption. By enforcing representation consistency across multiple corrupted views, the model learns stable and corruption-invariant semantic representations. Furthermore, we design a dual-stream spatio-temporal architecture that explicitly decouples temporal dynamics modeling and spatial correlation learning. The temporal stream captures long-term activity dynamics, while the spatial stream models inter-sensor relationships, enabling more effective spatio-temporal representation learning. Extensive experiments on three widely used HAR benchmark datasets, PAMAP2, Opportunity, and WISDM, demonstrate that the proposed MCSTN achieves competitive performance compared with existing state-of-the-art methods, particularly under imperfect sensing conditions. These results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed framework for real-world wearable IoMT sensing applications.

ai.tldr: 提出一种流形一致时空网络（MCSTN），在传感器数据不完美（缺失、故障、噪声）条件下实现鲁棒的人体活动识别。
ai.motivation: 真实可穿戴传感中IoMT信号常受缺失、故障和噪声污染，传统深度学习模型性能严重下降。
ai.method: 设计双层损坏建模（物理级+扩散驱动连续损坏），通过多视角表示一致性学习不变语义，并采用双流时空架构解耦时序动态与空间相关性。
ai.result: 在PAMAP2、Opportunity、WISDM三个基准数据集上，MCSTN在非完美传感条件下达到与现有最优方法相当或更优的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师构建对传感器噪声鲁棒的活动识别系统，适用于医疗健康监测等实际场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将扩散模型引入传感器数据增强，生成连续损坏视图以学习不变特征。
idea_spark.risk: 扩散过程计算开销较大，可能影响实时性；双流架构在资源受限设备上部署有挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用扩散模型模拟真实传感器退化过程，为鲁棒表示学习提供新思路。

zh.motivation: 真实可穿戴传感中IoMT信号常受缺失、故障和噪声污染，传统深度学习模型性能严重下降。

idea_spark.idea: 将扩散模型引入传感器数据增强，生成连续损坏视图以学习不变特征。
idea_spark.risk: 扩散过程计算开销较大，可能影响实时性；双流架构在资源受限设备上部署有挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用扩散模型模拟真实传感器退化过程，为鲁棒表示学习提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes a Manifold-Consistent Spatio-Temporal Network (MCSTN) for robust human activity recognition under imperfect sensor data (missing, faulty, noisy).
en.motivation: Real-world wearable IoMT signals are often corrupted by missing measurements, sensor failures, and noise, severely degrading conventional deep learning models that assume clean inputs.
en.method: Introduces dual-level corruption modeling (physical-level + diffusion-driven continuous corruption) and enforces representation consistency across multiple corrupted views; uses a dual-stream spatio-temporal architecture to decouple temporal dynamics and spatial correlations.
en.result: On PAMAP2, Opportunity, and WISDM benchmarks, MCSTN achieves competitive or superior performance under imperfect sensing conditions compared to state-of-the-art methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and practitioners build activity recognition systems robust to sensor noise, suitable for real-world healthcare monitoring.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce diffusion models into sensor data augmentation to generate continuous corrupted views for learning invariant features.
idea_spark.risk: Diffusion process may incur high computational cost, affecting real-time performance; dual-stream architecture may be challenging to deploy on resource-constrained devices.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using diffusion models to simulate realistic sensor degradation offers a new perspective for robust representation learning.

papers.title: Distilling Long-CoT Reasoning through Collaborative Step-wise Multi-Teacher Decoding
papers.summary: Distilling large reasoning models is essential for making Long-CoT reasoning practical, as full-scale inference remains computationally prohibitive. Existing curation-based approaches select complete reasoning traces post-hoc, overlooking collaboration among heterogeneous teachers and lacking dynamic exploration, which leads to redundant sampling and missed complementary reasoning. We introduce CoRD, a collaborative multi-teacher decoding framework that performs step-wise reasoning synthesis guided by predictive perplexity-based scoring and beam search. This enables heterogeneous LRMs to jointly construct coherent reasoning trajectories while efficiently preserving diverse, high-potential hypotheses. Experiments show that CoRD produces higher-quality reasoning data and achieves near teacher-level student performance with fewer, structured supervision signals, without substantial efficiency overhead. CoRD further generalizes well to out-of-domain and open-ended settings. The dataset and model are available at \href{https://github.com/DISL-Lab/CoRD}{https://github.com/DISL-Lab/CoRD}.

ai.tldr: 提出CoRD框架，通过多教师协作逐步解码和困惑度评分，高效蒸馏长链推理数据，降低计算成本。
ai.motivation: 现有蒸馏方法依赖事后筛选完整推理链，忽视异构教师协作和动态探索，导致采样冗余且互补推理缺失。
ai.method: 采用基于预测困惑度评分和束搜索的逐步推理合成，让异构大推理模型联合构建连贯推理轨迹，同时保留多样高潜力假设。
ai.result: 生成更高质量的推理数据，用更少的结构化监督信号达到接近教师水平的学生性能，且泛化到域外和开放场景。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效蒸馏长链推理能力的方法，减少计算开销，适合资源受限场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将困惑度评分替换为其他质量指标（如一致性、多样性），或引入自适应束宽。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预定义评分函数可能偏向特定推理模式，需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多智能体协作和束搜索启发，将推理链构建视为搜索问题，结合教师多样性。

zh.tldr: 提出CoRD框架，通过多教师协作逐步解码和困惑度评分，高效蒸馏长链推理数据，降低计算成本。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种高效蒸馏长链推理能力的方法，减少计算开销，适合资源受限场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将困惑度评分替换为其他质量指标（如一致性、多样性），或引入自适应束宽。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预定义评分函数可能偏向特定推理模式，需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多智能体协作和束搜索启发，将推理链构建视为搜索问题，结合教师多样性。

en.tldr: Proposes CoRD, a collaborative multi-teacher decoding framework that uses step-wise reasoning synthesis with perplexity-based scoring and beam search to efficiently distill long chain-of-thought reasoning data.
en.motivation: Existing distillation methods select complete reasoning traces post-hoc, ignoring collaboration among heterogeneous teachers and dynamic exploration, leading to redundant sampling and missed complementary reasoning.
en.method: Employs predictive perplexity-based scoring and beam search for step-wise reasoning synthesis, enabling heterogeneous large reasoning models to jointly construct coherent trajectories while preserving diverse, high-potential hypotheses.
en.result: Produces higher-quality reasoning data, achieves near teacher-level student performance with fewer structured supervision signals, and generalizes well to out-of-domain and open-ended settings.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient method to distill long chain-of-thought reasoning capabilities with reduced computational cost, suitable for resource-constrained scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Explore replacing perplexity scoring with other quality metrics (e.g., consistency, diversity) or introducing adaptive beam width.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on a predefined scoring function may bias toward specific reasoning patterns; robustness needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-agent collaboration and beam search, framing reasoning chain construction as a search problem leveraging teacher diversity.

papers.title: Seeing Realism from Simulation: Efficient Video Transfer for Vision-Language-Action Data Augmentation
papers.summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models typically rely on large-scale real-world videos, whereas simulated data, despite being inexpensive and highly parallelizable to collect, often suffers from a substantial visual domain gap and limited environmental diversity, resulting in weak real-world generalization. We present an efficient video augmentation framework that converts simulated VLA videos into realistic training videos while preserving task semantics and action trajectories. Our pipeline extracts structured conditions from simulation via video semantic segmentation and video captioning, rewrites captions to diversify environments, and uses a conditional video transfer model to synthesize realistic videos. To make augmentation practical at scale, we introduce a diffusion feature-reuse mechanism that reuses video tokens across adjacent timesteps to accelerate generation, and a coreset sampling strategy that identifies a compact, non-redundant subset for augmentation under limited computation. Extensive experiments on Robotwin 2.0, LIBERO, LIBERO-Plus, and a real robotic platform demonstrate consistent improvements. For example, our method improves RDT-1B by 8% on Robotwin 2.0, and boosts $π_0$ by 5.1% on the more challenging LIBERO-Plus benchmark. Code is available at: https://github.com/nanfangxiansheng/Seeing-Realism-from-Simulation.

ai.tldr: 提出一个高效视频增强框架，将仿真VLA视频转换为逼真训练视频，保留任务语义和动作轨迹，通过扩散特征复用和核心集采样实现大规模实用化。
ai.motivation: VLA模型依赖大规模真实视频，仿真数据虽廉价易收集但存在视觉域差和环境多样性不足，导致真实泛化弱。
ai.method: 从仿真中提取结构化条件（视频语义分割、视频描述），重写描述以多样化环境，用条件视频迁移模型合成真实视频；引入扩散特征复用机制加速生成，以及核心集采样策略选择紧凑子集。
ai.result: 在Robotwin 2.0、LIBERO、LIBERO-Plus和真实机器人平台上取得一致提升，例如RDT-1B提升8%，π0提升5.1%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户低成本地将仿真数据转化为高质量真实训练数据，提升VLA模型在真实场景中的表现。

idea_spark.idea: 结合扩散特征复用和核心集采样，在保持生成质量的同时大幅降低计算成本。
idea_spark.risk: 条件视频迁移模型可能对复杂动态场景生成不够稳定，核心集采样可能遗漏关键边缘案例。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用仿真结构化信息（语义分割、描述）作为条件，结合扩散模型高效生成，为数据增强提供新思路。

zh.tldr: 提出一个高效视频增强框架，将仿真VLA视频转换为逼真训练视频，保留任务语义和动作轨迹，通过扩散特征复用和核心集采样实现大规模实用化。
zh.motivation: VLA模型依赖大规模真实视频，仿真数据虽廉价易收集但存在视觉域差和环境多样性不足，导致真实泛化弱。

zh.result: 在Robotwin 2.0、LIBERO、LIBERO-Plus和真实机器人平台上取得一致提升，例如RDT-1B提升8%，π0提升5.1%。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户低成本地将仿真数据转化为高质量真实训练数据，提升VLA模型在真实场景中的表现。

idea_spark.idea: 结合扩散特征复用和核心集采样，在保持生成质量的同时大幅降低计算成本。
idea_spark.risk: 条件视频迁移模型可能对复杂动态场景生成不够稳定，核心集采样可能遗漏关键边缘案例。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用仿真结构化信息（语义分割、描述）作为条件，结合扩散模型高效生成，为数据增强提供新思路。

en.tldr: An efficient video augmentation framework that converts simulated VLA videos into realistic training videos while preserving task semantics and action trajectories, using diffusion feature-reuse and coreset sampling for scalable deployment.
en.motivation: VLA models rely on large-scale real-world videos; simulated data is cheap but suffers from visual domain gap and limited diversity, leading to poor real-world generalization.
en.method: Extract structured conditions from simulation via video semantic segmentation and captioning, rewrite captions to diversify environments, and use a conditional video transfer model to synthesize realistic videos; introduce diffusion feature-reuse to accelerate generation and coreset sampling to select a compact subset.
en.result: Consistent improvements on Robotwin 2.0, LIBERO, LIBERO-Plus, and a real robotic platform, e.g., 8% improvement on RDT-1B and 5.1% on π0.
en.help_to_user: Helps users convert simulated data into high-quality realistic training data at low cost, improving VLA model performance in real-world scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combine diffusion feature-reuse and coreset sampling to significantly reduce computational cost while maintaining generation quality.
idea_spark.risk: The conditional video transfer model may be unstable for complex dynamic scenes; coreset sampling might miss critical edge cases.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using structured simulation information (semantic segmentation, captions) as conditions with efficient diffusion generation offers a new perspective for data augmentation.

papers.title: Controlled Paraphrase Geometry in Sentence Embedding Space: Local Manifold Modeling and Latent Probing
papers.summary: The paper studies the local geometry of embedding clouds induced by \emph{controlled local classes of semantically close sentences}. The central question is how controlled paraphrase-like semantic variation is organized in sentence embedding space and whether this local structure can be explicitly modeled by low-degree fitted carriers.   We introduce a local geometric modeling scheme based on affine, quadratic, and cubic fitted models. We also use a surface-based latent probing procedure that constructs synthetic latent points in a reduced local PCA space with respect to the fitted carrier. The procedure is intended as an offline method for representation-space analysis, local manifold modeling, and geometry-aware latent probing.   Generated latent points are evaluated using criteria that measure consistency with the fitted surface, preservation of neighborhood structure, agreement with the empirical distribution, stability of Hessian-based second-order shape descriptors, and stability of fitted-model coefficients. Experiments on controlled sets of semantically close sentences show that nonlinear local models describe embedding clouds more accurately than affine models. Surface-based generation provides strong fitted-geometry fidelity, including surface consistency, Hessian-based shape consistency, and coefficient consistency.   Downstream experiments show that geometric validity of synthetic latent points does not automatically translate into improved classification performance. The results support explicit local geometric modeling of sentence embedding space and highlight the need to distinguish geometric validity from discriminative utility. As a resource contribution, we introduce \textbf{CoPaGE-300K}, a controlled template-based dataset of semantically close sentence variants with slot-level annotations and precomputed sentence embeddings.

ai.tldr: 研究句子嵌入空间中受控释义的局部几何结构，提出基于低阶拟合模型的局部几何建模和潜在探测方法，并构建了CoPaGE-300K数据集。
ai.motivation: 理解句子嵌入空间中语义相近句子的局部几何组织方式，并探索能否显式建模这种局部结构。
ai.method: 使用仿射、二次和三次拟合模型进行局部几何建模，并通过表面潜在探测在降维PCA空间中生成合成潜在点。
ai.result: 非线性模型比仿射模型更准确描述嵌入云；表面生成保持了拟合几何保真度，但几何有效性不直接提升分类性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了句子嵌入空间局部几何建模的框架和工具，以及一个受控释义数据集，可用于表示空间分析和几何感知探测。

idea_spark.idea: 利用拟合模型的Hessian形状描述符作为正则化项，引导生成更具判别性的潜在表示。
idea_spark.risk: 几何保真度与下游任务性能之间的脱节可能导致生成点虽几何合理但实际无用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 区分几何有效性与判别效用，启发设计同时优化几何和任务目标的联合训练目标。

zh.tldr: 研究句子嵌入空间中受控释义的局部几何结构，提出基于低阶拟合模型的局部几何建模和潜在探测方法，并构建了CoPaGE-300K数据集。
zh.motivation: 理解句子嵌入空间中语义相近句子的局部几何组织方式，并探索能否显式建模这种局部结构。
zh.method: 使用仿射、二次和三次拟合模型进行局部几何建模，并通过表面潜在探测在降维PCA空间中生成合成潜在点。
zh.result: 非线性模型比仿射模型更准确描述嵌入云；表面生成保持了拟合几何保真度，但几何有效性不直接提升分类性能。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了句子嵌入空间局部几何建模的框架和工具，以及一个受控释义数据集，可用于表示空间分析和几何感知探测。

idea_spark.idea: 利用拟合模型的Hessian形状描述符作为正则化项，引导生成更具判别性的潜在表示。
idea_spark.risk: 几何保真度与下游任务性能之间的脱节可能导致生成点虽几何合理但实际无用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 区分几何有效性与判别效用，启发设计同时优化几何和任务目标的联合训练目标。

en.tldr: Studies the local geometry of controlled paraphrases in sentence embedding space, proposes local manifold modeling with low-degree fitted models and latent probing, and introduces the CoPaGE-300K dataset.
en.motivation: To understand how semantically close sentences are organized locally in embedding space and whether this structure can be explicitly modeled.
en.method: Uses affine, quadratic, and cubic fitted models for local geometric modeling, and a surface-based latent probing procedure to generate synthetic points in reduced PCA space.
en.result: Nonlinear models describe embedding clouds more accurately than affine models; surface-based generation achieves high geometric fidelity, but geometric validity does not automatically improve classification performance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework for local geometric modeling and latent probing of sentence embedding spaces, along with a controlled paraphrase dataset for analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Use Hessian-based shape descriptors from fitted models as regularization to guide generation of more discriminative latent points.
idea_spark.risk: The disconnect between geometric fidelity and downstream task performance may yield geometrically valid but practically useless generated points.
idea_spark.inspiration: Distinguishing geometric validity from discriminative utility inspires designing joint objectives that optimize both geometry and task performance.

papers.title: Visual Implicit Autoregressive Modeling
papers.summary: Visual Autoregressive Modeling (VAR) based on next-scale prediction achieves strong generation quality, but their explicit deep stacks fix the amount of computation per scale and inflate memory at high resolutions. We introduce Visual Implicit Autoregressive Modeling (VIAR), a next-scale autoregressive generator that embeds an implicit equilibrium layer between shallow pre/post blocks. The implicit layer is trained with Jacobian-Free Backpropagation, yielding constant training memory, while inference exposes a per-scale iteration knob that enables compute control. On ImageNet 256x256 benchmark, VIAR attains FID 2.16, and sFID 8.07 with only 38.4% parameters of VAR, matching or surpassing strong AR baselines and remaining competitive with large diffusion models. By controlling the per-scale knob, VIAR can reduce peak memory from 19.24 GB to 8.53 GB and doubles throughput from 15.16 to 32.08 images/s on a single RTX 4090, without retraining. Ablations show that fewer steps are sufficient for fixed-point iterations to converge and that VIAR consistently dominates VAR across quality efficiency operating points. In zero shot in-painting and class-conditional editing, VIAR produces sharper details and smoother boundaries while preserving global structure, validating the benefits of implicit equilibria and per-scale compute control for practical, deployable visual generation.

ai.tldr: 提出视觉隐式自回归建模（VIAR），通过隐式平衡层实现每尺度计算可控，在ImageNet 256×256上以38.4%参数达到FID 2.16，并支持推理时灵活调整内存与吞吐量。
ai.motivation: 显式自回归模型（如VAR）每尺度计算固定且高分辨率下内存膨胀，需要一种计算可动态调整且内存高效的生成方法。
ai.method: 在浅层前后处理块之间嵌入隐式平衡层，采用无雅可比反向传播训练，推理时通过每尺度迭代旋钮控制计算量。
ai.result: ImageNet 256×256上FID 2.16、sFID 8.07，参数仅为VAR的38.4%；峰值内存从19.24GB降至8.53GB，吞吐量从15.46提升至32.08 images/s；零样本修复和编辑中细节更清晰、边界更平滑。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种计算可控、内存高效的视觉生成方案，适合资源受限或需要灵活部署的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将隐式深度学习方法引入视觉自回归生成，用迭代求解替代显式堆叠，实现计算与质量的灵活权衡。
idea_spark.risk: 隐式层收敛性依赖固定点迭代，可能在某些复杂分布下需要更多迭代步数或导致不稳定；训练时无雅可比反向传播的近似可能影响梯度质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受隐式神经网络（如DEQ）和计算感知模型启发，将“深度”转化为“迭代”，使模型在推理时自适应调整计算资源。

en.tldr: Proposes Visual Implicit Autoregressive Modeling (VIAR), which uses an implicit equilibrium layer for per-scale compute control, achieving FID 2.16 on ImageNet 256×256 with 38.4% parameters of VAR, and enabling flexible memory-throughput trade-offs at inference.
en.motivation: Explicit autoregressive models like VAR fix computation per scale and inflate memory at high resolutions, necessitating a method with dynamic compute allocation and memory efficiency.
en.method: Embeds an implicit equilibrium layer between shallow pre/post blocks, trained with Jacobian-Free Backpropagation, and exposes a per-scale iteration knob for compute control during inference.
en.result: FID 2.16 and sFID 8.07 on ImageNet 256×256 with only 38.4% parameters of VAR; peak memory reduced from 19.24 GB to 8.53 GB, throughput doubled from 15.46 to 32.08 images/s; sharper details and smoother boundaries in zero-shot inpainting and editing.
en.help_to_user: Provides a compute-controllable and memory-efficient visual generation method suitable for resource-constrained or flexible deployment scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Introduces implicit deep learning into visual autoregressive generation, replacing explicit stacking with iterative solving for flexible compute-quality trade-offs.
idea_spark.risk: Convergence of the implicit layer depends on fixed-point iterations, which may require more steps or become unstable for complex distributions; Jacobian-Free Backpropagation approximation may affect gradient quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by implicit neural networks (e.g., DEQ) and compute-aware models, turning 'depth' into 'iteration' to adaptively allocate compute resources at inference.

papers.title: SIAM: Head and Brain MRI Segmentation from Few High-Quality Templates via Synthetic Training
papers.summary: Synthetic training has recently advanced brain MRI segmentation by enabling contrast-agnostic models trained entirely on generated data. However, most existing approaches rely on hundreds of automatically labeled templates, introducing systematic biases and limiting their flexibility to incorporate new anatomical structures. We present the Segment It All Model (SIAM), a 3D whole-head segmentation framework for 16 anatomical structures, trained using only six high-quality, manually annotated templates. SIAM extends domain randomization to both intensity and shape domains: synthetic image generation ensures contrast variability, while high-resolution spatial transformations model anatomical differences in cortical thickness and deep nuclei morphology. Unlike prior synthetic models, SIAM simultaneously segments brain as well as extra-cerebral tissues, including cerebrospinal fluid, vessels, dura mater, skull, and skin, enabling fully automated, preprocessing-free analysis. Evaluation across eight heterogeneous datasets (N=301), that include multiple contrasts (T1-weighted, T2-weighted, CT) and span a wide range of ages, demonstrates that SIAM matches or outperforms state-of-the-art methods for brain structures, in addition to extending automated segmentation to non-brain structures. The model also exhibits superior consistency across contrasts and repeated acquisitions, together with improved sensitivity to subtle gray matter atrophy. We openly release the model and the label templates at https://github.com/romainVala/SIAM.

ai.tldr: 提出SIAM模型，仅用6个高质量手动标注模板，通过合成训练实现16种解剖结构的全头MRI分割，包括脑组织和颅外组织。
ai.motivation: 现有合成训练方法依赖数百个自动标注模板，引入系统偏差且难以添加新结构。
ai.method: 扩展域随机化到强度和形状域：合成图像生成实现对比度变化，高分辨率空间变换模拟解剖差异。
ai.result: 在8个数据集（N=301）上，SIAM在脑结构分割上匹配或超越现有方法，并扩展到非脑结构，跨对比度和重复扫描一致性更优。
ai.help_to_user: 提供无需预处理的自动化全头分割工具，适用于多种MRI和CT对比度。

idea_spark.idea: 利用少量高质量模板结合合成数据，可降低标注成本并灵活扩展新结构。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据可能无法完全模拟真实病理变异，影响泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合形状域随机化与强度域随机化，提升模型对解剖变异的鲁棒性。

en.tldr: SIAM is a 3D whole-head segmentation framework trained on only six high-quality templates via synthetic training, segmenting 16 structures including brain and extra-cerebral tissues.
en.motivation: Existing synthetic training methods rely on hundreds of automatically labeled templates, introducing biases and limiting flexibility for new structures.
en.method: Extends domain randomization to intensity and shape domains: synthetic image generation for contrast variability and high-resolution spatial transformations for anatomical differences.
en.result: On eight datasets (N=301), SIAM matches or surpasses state-of-the-art for brain structures, extends to non-brain structures, and shows superior cross-contrast consistency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a fully automated, preprocessing-free whole-head segmentation tool applicable to various MRI and CT contrasts.

idea_spark.idea: Using few high-quality templates with synthetic data reduces annotation cost and allows flexible addition of new structures.
idea_spark.risk: Synthetic data may not fully capture real pathological variations, affecting generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining shape domain randomization with intensity domain randomization enhances robustness to anatomical variability.

papers.title: MTA: Multi-Granular Trajectory Alignment for Large Language Model Distillation
papers.summary: Knowledge distillation is a key technique for compressing large language models (LLMs), but most existing methods align representations at fixed layers or token-level outputs, ignoring how representations evolve across depth. As a result, the student is only weakly guided to capture the teacher's internal relational structure during distillation, which limits knowledge transfer. To address this limitation, we propose Multi-Granular Trajectory Alignment (MTA), a framework that aligns teacher and student representations along their layer-wise transformation trajectory. MTA adopts a layer-adaptive strategy: lower layers are aligned at the word level to preserve lexical information, while higher layers operate on phrase-level spans (e.g., noun and verb phrases) to capture compositional semantics. We instantiate this idea through a Dynamic Structural Alignment loss that matches the relative geometry among semantic units within each layer. This design is motivated by empirical findings that Transformer representations become increasingly abstract with depth, and is also consistent with linguistic views in which higher-level meaning emerges through the composition of lower-level lexical units. We further incorporate a Hidden Representation Alignment loss to directly align selected teacher-student layers. Experiments show that MTA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on standard benchmarks, with ablations confirming the contribution of each component.

ai.tldr: 提出多粒度轨迹对齐框架MTA，通过层自适应策略对齐教师和学生模型的表示演化轨迹，提升知识蒸馏效果。
ai.motivation: 现有蒸馏方法仅在固定层或token级别对齐，忽略了表示随深度演化的结构关系，导致学生模型难以捕捉教师内部关系。
ai.method: MTA采用层自适应对齐：低层进行词级对齐保留词汇信息，高层进行短语级对齐捕获组合语义，并引入动态结构对齐损失匹配语义单元相对几何关系，同时结合隐藏表示对齐损失直接对齐选定层。
ai.result: 在标准基准上持续优于现有方法，消融实验验证了各组件的贡献。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种更有效的LLM蒸馏方案，可帮助用户压缩模型同时保持教师的知识结构。

idea_spark.idea: 可探索将轨迹对齐扩展到跨模态（如文本-图像）表示演化对齐。
idea_spark.risk: 短语级对齐依赖句法解析，可能引入噪声或增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Transformer表示随层抽象化及语言学组合性启发，将层次化语义结构融入对齐目标。

zh.tldr: 提出多粒度轨迹对齐框架MTA，通过层自适应策略对齐教师和学生模型的表示演化轨迹，提升知识蒸馏效果。
zh.motivation: 现有蒸馏方法仅在固定层或token级别对齐，忽略了表示随深度演化的结构关系，导致学生模型难以捕捉教师内部关系。

zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种更有效的LLM蒸馏方案，可帮助用户压缩模型同时保持教师的知识结构。

idea_spark.idea: 可探索将轨迹对齐扩展到跨模态（如文本-图像）表示演化对齐。
idea_spark.risk: 短语级对齐依赖句法解析，可能引入噪声或增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Transformer表示随层抽象化及语言学组合性启发，将层次化语义结构融入对齐目标。

en.tldr: Proposes Multi-Granular Trajectory Alignment (MTA), a framework that aligns teacher and student representation evolution across layers via a layer-adaptive strategy for improved knowledge distillation.
en.motivation: Existing distillation methods align at fixed layers or token-level outputs, ignoring how representations evolve with depth, limiting the student's ability to capture the teacher's internal relational structure.
en.method: MTA adopts layer-adaptive alignment: lower layers align at word level to preserve lexical information, higher layers align at phrase level to capture compositional semantics, with a Dynamic Structural Alignment loss matching relative geometry of semantic units, plus a Hidden Representation Alignment loss.
en.result: Consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on standard benchmarks; ablations confirm each component's contribution.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more effective LLM distillation approach that helps users compress models while preserving the teacher's knowledge structure.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending trajectory alignment to cross-modal (e.g., text-image) representation evolution alignment.
idea_spark.risk: Phrase-level alignment relies on syntactic parsing, which may introduce noise or increase computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the abstraction of Transformer representations with depth and linguistic compositionality, incorporating hierarchical semantic structure into alignment objectives.

papers.title: DriftDecode: One-Step Wireless Image Decoding via Drifting-Inspired Detail Recovery
papers.summary: Generative receivers for wireless image transmission can improve reconstruction quality, but diffusion-based and flow-based decoding relies on iterative inference and therefore incurs substantial latency. In wireless image transmission, however, the received signal already preserves the coarse structure of the source image. Wireless decoding is therefore better viewed as a recovery task than as image generation from scratch, and the main challenge lies in restoring channel-impaired details. Motivated by this recovery-oriented perspective, this paper proposes DriftDecode, a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR)-conditioned one-step decoder for wireless image reconstruction. DriftDecode couples a one-step U-Net decoder with a drift-inspired instance-level texture loss. The loss reformulates the drifting-field mechanism from generative drifting models in perceptual feature space, guiding each reconstructed local feature toward its spatially aligned ground-truth counterpart while suppressing mismatched textures. Experiments on DIV2K and MNIST under additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) and Rayleigh fading channels show a favorable quality-latency tradeoff. DriftDecode achieves 30~ms decoding latency, providing a 4.8$\times$ speedup over a 10-step flow-matching decoder, while consistently outperforming MSE-only training and yielding up to 1.13~dB PSNR gain on MNIST under Rayleigh fading. These results support recovery-oriented one-step decoding as an effective alternative to iterative generative decoding for low-latency wireless image transmission.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于漂移启发细节恢复的单步无线图像解码方法，在保持低延迟的同时提升重建质量。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散/流模型解码器迭代推理延迟高，而无线接收信号已保留粗结构，解码本质是恢复而非生成。
ai.method: 设计SNR条件单步U-Net解码器，并引入漂移启发的实例级纹理损失，在感知特征空间引导局部特征对齐。
ai.result: 在DIV2K和MNIST上，30ms解码延迟，比10步流匹配解码器快4.8倍，PSNR最高提升1.13dB。
ai.help_to_user: 为低延迟无线图像传输提供一种高效的单步解码方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将漂移场机制从生成模型改造为判别式纹理损失，实现单步高质量恢复。
idea_spark.risk: 在极端信道条件下，单步解码可能无法恢复严重缺失的细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 无线传输中信号已含粗结构，解码应聚焦细节恢复而非从头生成。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于漂移启发细节恢复的单步无线图像解码方法，在保持低延迟的同时提升重建质量。
zh.motivation: 现有扩散/流模型解码器迭代推理延迟高，而无线接收信号已保留粗结构，解码本质是恢复而非生成。

zh.result: 在DIV2K和MNIST上，30ms解码延迟，比10步流匹配解码器快4.8倍，PSNR最高提升1.13dB。
zh.help_to_user: 为低延迟无线图像传输提供一种高效的单步解码方案。

idea_spark.risk: 在极端信道条件下，单步解码可能无法恢复严重缺失的细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 无线传输中信号已含粗结构，解码应聚焦细节恢复而非从头生成。

en.tldr: Proposes a one-step wireless image decoder with drift-inspired detail recovery, achieving low latency and high quality.
en.motivation: Diffusion/flow decoders suffer from iterative inference latency; received signals already preserve coarse structure, so decoding is recovery rather than generation.
en.method: An SNR-conditioned one-step U-Net decoder with a drift-inspired instance-level texture loss that aligns local features in perceptual space.
en.result: 30ms decoding latency on DIV2K and MNIST, 4.8× speedup over 10-step flow matching, up to 1.13dB PSNR gain.
en.help_to_user: Offers an efficient one-step decoding solution for low-latency wireless image transmission.

idea_spark.idea: Reformulate the drift field mechanism from generative models into a discriminative texture loss for one-step high-quality recovery.
idea_spark.risk: Under extreme channel conditions, one-step decoding may fail to recover severely missing details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Wireless signals already contain coarse structure, so decoding should focus on detail recovery rather than generation from scratch.

papers.title: Multi-Dataset Cross-Domain Knowledge Distillation for Unified Medical Image Segmentation, Classification, and Detection
papers.summary: We propose a unified cross-domain transfer learning framework that leverages knowledge from multiple heterogeneous medical imaging datasets to improve performance across segmentation, classification, and object detection tasks. Our approach employs a teacher-student paradigm in which a joint teacher model aggregates domain-invariant representations learned from diverse source datasets, while a task-specific student model is trained via multi-level knowledge distillation. Originally developed for medical image segmentation, the framework is extended to support image-level classification and object-level detection, enabling a general multi-task formulation for medical image analysis. We evaluate our method on a broad suite of datasets, including six segmentation benchmarks, BrainMetShare, ISLES, BraTS (MRI) and Lung MSD, LiTS, KiTS (CT), as well as multiple classification datasets for pulmonary disease and dementia, and detection datasets with native bounding-box annotations. Across all tasks and modalities, the proposed approach yields consistent improvements over strong dataset-specific and multi-head baselines, demonstrating enhanced robustness to distributional shifts and superior generalization. These findings highlight the potential of multi-dataset knowledge distillation as a scalable and task-agnostic approach for enhancing segmentation, classification, and object detection performance across heterogeneous medical imaging domains.

ai.tldr: 提出一个统一的多数据集跨域知识蒸馏框架，同时提升医学图像分割、分类和检测任务的性能。
ai.motivation: 现有方法通常针对单一任务或数据集，难以处理异构医学影像的分布差异，缺乏统一的多任务框架。
ai.method: 采用教师-学生范式：教师模型从多个源数据集聚合域不变表示，学生模型通过多层次知识蒸馏进行任务特定训练，支持分割、分类和检测。
ai.result: 在多个分割、分类和检测数据集上，该方法一致优于强基线，对分布偏移更鲁棒，泛化能力更强。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展、任务无关的知识蒸馏方法，帮助用户利用多个数据集提升医学影像分析模型的性能。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将域不变表示学习与对比学习结合，进一步强化教师模型的特征泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 多数据集联合训练可能引入标签噪声或任务冲突，需设计更精细的蒸馏权重分配策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多任务学习和知识蒸馏启发，将异构数据统一建模，实现任务间知识共享。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将域不变表示学习与对比学习结合，进一步强化教师模型的特征泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 多数据集联合训练可能引入标签噪声或任务冲突，需设计更精细的蒸馏权重分配策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多任务学习和知识蒸馏启发，将异构数据统一建模，实现任务间知识共享。

en.tldr: Proposes a unified multi-dataset cross-domain knowledge distillation framework that simultaneously improves medical image segmentation, classification, and detection.
en.motivation: Existing methods typically target single tasks or datasets, struggling with distribution shifts across heterogeneous medical images and lacking a unified multi-task framework.
en.method: Employs a teacher-student paradigm: the teacher aggregates domain-invariant representations from multiple source datasets, and the student is trained via multi-level knowledge distillation for task-specific segmentation, classification, and detection.
en.result: Consistent improvements over strong baselines across multiple segmentation, classification, and detection datasets, with enhanced robustness to distribution shifts and superior generalization.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable, task-agnostic knowledge distillation approach to leverage multiple datasets for improving medical image analysis models.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining domain-invariant representation learning with contrastive learning to further enhance feature generalization of the teacher model.
idea_spark.risk: Joint training on multiple datasets may introduce label noise or task conflicts, requiring more refined distillation weight assignment strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-task learning and knowledge distillation, unifying heterogeneous data for cross-task knowledge sharing.

papers.title: Decompose and Recompose: Reasoning New Skills from Existing Abilities for Cross-Task Robotic Manipulation
papers.summary: Cross-task generalization is a core challenge in open-world robotic manipulation, and the key lies in extracting transferable manipulation knowledge from seen tasks. Recent in-context learning approaches leverage seen task demonstrations to generate actions for unseen tasks without parameter updates. However, existing methods provide only low-level continuous action sequences as context, failing to capture composable skill knowledge and causing models to degenerate into superficial trajectory imitation. We propose Decompose and Recompose, a skill reasoning framework using atomic skill-action pairs as intermediate representations. Our approach decomposes seen demonstrations into interpretable skill--action alignments, enabling the model to recompose these skills for unseen tasks through compositional reasoning. Specifically, we construct a task-adaptive dynamic demonstration library via visual-semantic retrieval combined with skill sequences from a planning agent, complemented by a coverage-aware static library to fill missing skill patterns. Together, these yield skill-comprehensive demonstrations that explicitly elicit compositional reasoning for skill composition and execution ordering. Experiments on the AGNOSTOS benchmark and real-world environments validate our method's zero-shot cross-task generalization capability.

ai.result: 在AGNOSTOS基准和真实环境中验证了零样本跨任务泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过技能分解与重组实现机器人对新任务的零样本适应。

papers.title: RADMI: Latent Information Aggregation as a Proxy for Model Uncertainty
papers.summary: Epistemic uncertainty estimation is essential for identifying regions where deep learning system outputs may be unreliable. However, existing approaches require computationally expensive ensemble methods or multiple stochastic forward passes, limiting their scalability to dense prediction tasks like segmentation. We propose Resolution-Aggregated Decoder Mutual Information (RADMI), a single-pass method that estimates prediction uncertainty by measuring mutual information (MI) between consecutive decoder layers in segmentation networks. We observe that elevated inter-layer MI correlates with prediction uncertainty, as the network must integrate conflicting contextual information at ambiguous regions such as class boundaries. Evaluating on a seismic facies segmentation benchmark, RADMI achieves the highest correlation with deep ensemble uncertainty among all single-pass methods, outperforming the next-best baselines by 5.5% in Pearson and 10.7% in Spearman correlation coefficients. Compared to baselines that either lack spatial precision or demand significant computational overhead, RADMI yields sharp, boundary-localized uncertainty maps without architectural modifications. Our results suggest that linear aggregation of normalized information flow provides a principled and efficient proxy for prediction uncertainty in encoder-decoder architectures.

ai.tldr: 提出RADMI，一种单次前向传播的模型不确定性估计方法，通过测量分割网络解码器层间的互信息来预测不确定性，在 seismic facies 分割基准上达到单次方法中最高的与深度集成不确定性的相关性。
ai.motivation: 现有不确定性估计方法需要昂贵的集成或多随机前向传播，难以扩展到密集预测任务如分割。
ai.method: 计算分割网络连续解码器层之间的互信息作为不确定性的代理，利用高互信息对应预测不确定性（如类别边界处）。
ai.result: 在 seismic facies 分割基准上，Pearson 相关系数比次优基线高5.5%，Spearman 高10.7%，生成边界清晰的不确定性图。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、单次前向传播的不确定性估计方法，适用于分割等密集预测任务，无需修改网络架构。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将互信息聚合应用于其他网络层（如编码器层）或不同模态数据的不确定性估计。
idea_spark.risk: 互信息作为不确定性代理的泛化性可能受限于特定网络结构和任务，需验证在不同数据集上的表现。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用网络内部信息流（层间互信息）作为不确定性信号，启发设计更轻量级的不确定性估计方法。

en.tldr: Proposes RADMI, a single-pass method for epistemic uncertainty estimation by measuring mutual information between consecutive decoder layers in segmentation networks, achieving the highest correlation with deep ensemble uncertainty among single-pass methods on a seismic facies segmentation benchmark.
en.motivation: Existing uncertainty estimation methods require expensive ensembles or multiple stochastic forward passes, limiting scalability to dense prediction tasks like segmentation.
en.method: Measures mutual information between consecutive decoder layers of a segmentation network as a proxy for prediction uncertainty, leveraging the observation that high inter-layer MI correlates with uncertainty at ambiguous regions such as class boundaries.

idea_spark.risk: The generalizability of mutual information as an uncertainty proxy may be limited to specific network structures and tasks, requiring validation on diverse datasets.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging internal network information flow (inter-layer MI) as an uncertainty signal inspires the design of more lightweight uncertainty estimation methods.

papers.title: Robustness of Transformer-Based Fluence Map Prediction Under Clinically Realistic Perturbations
papers.summary: Learning-based fluence map prediction offers a fast alternative to iterative inverse planning in intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT), but its robustness under realistic distribution shifts remains unclear. We study a two-stage transformer pipeline that maps anatomy (CT and contours) to dose and then to beamlet fluence maps. We compare fluence-stage transformer backbones with hierarchical, global, and hybrid attention, trained with a physics-informed loss enforcing energy consistency. Robustness is evaluated under geometric perturbations, radiometric noise, reduced training data, and domain shifts using a prostate IMRT dataset, with additional evaluation of the dose stage on public datasets. Results show smooth degradation under moderate perturbations but sharp failures under severe rotations and noise. Hierarchical transformers (e.g., SwinUNETR) exhibit slower growth in upper-quartile energy error, indicating improved robustness. We further show that SSIM alone fails to capture clinically relevant errors, highlighting the need for physics-informed evaluation.

ai.tldr: 研究基于Transformer的通量图预测在临床现实扰动下的鲁棒性，发现分层注意力模型更稳健，且SSIM不足以评估临床相关误差。
ai.motivation: 学习型通量图预测虽快，但在临床分布偏移下的鲁棒性未知，需系统评估。
ai.method: 采用两阶段Transformer流水线（解剖→剂量→通量），比较不同注意力机制骨干，并用物理信息损失训练，在前列腺IMRT数据集上测试几何、噪声、数据量及域偏移。
ai.result: 中等扰动下性能平滑下降，严重旋转和噪声时急剧失败；分层Transformer（如SwinUNETR）能量误差上四分位数增长更慢，鲁棒性更好；SSIM无法捕捉临床相关误差。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解Transformer在放疗规划中的鲁棒性边界，指导模型选择与评估指标设计。

idea_spark.idea: 结合对抗训练或数据增强提升对严重几何扰动的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 严重扰动下模型可能完全失效，需设计安全阈值。
idea_spark.inspiration: SSIM的局限性提示需开发临床相关的物理感知评估指标。

en.tldr: Evaluates robustness of transformer-based fluence map prediction under clinical perturbations, finding hierarchical attention more robust and SSIM insufficient for clinical error detection.
en.motivation: Learning-based fluence map prediction is fast but its robustness under clinical distribution shifts is unknown, requiring systematic evaluation.
en.method: Uses a two-stage transformer pipeline (anatomy to dose to fluence), compares hierarchical, global, and hybrid attention backbones trained with physics-informed loss, tested on prostate IMRT dataset with geometric, noise, data size, and domain shifts.
en.result: Smooth degradation under moderate perturbations, sharp failures under severe rotation and noise; hierarchical transformers (e.g., SwinUNETR) show slower growth in upper-quartile energy error; SSIM fails to capture clinically relevant errors.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into robustness boundaries of transformers in radiotherapy planning, guiding model selection and evaluation metrics.

idea_spark.idea: Combine adversarial training or data augmentation to improve robustness against severe geometric perturbations.
idea_spark.risk: Model may completely fail under severe perturbations, requiring safety thresholds.
idea_spark.inspiration: SSIM's limitation motivates development of clinically relevant physics-aware evaluation metrics.

papers.title: (POSTER) From Sensors to Insight: Rapid, Edge-to-Core Application Development for Sensor-Driven Applications
papers.summary: Scientists increasingly rely on sensor-based data; however transforming raw streams into insights across the edge-to-cloud continuum remains difficult due to the breadth of expertise required to coordinate the necessary data and computation flow. This paper introduces a pattern-based, AI-assisted methodology for rapid development of sensor-driven applications. Using Pegasus workflows executing on the FABRIC testbed, we demonstrate a 5-step development loop that shifts workflow construction and deployment from code-first to intent-first design. Starting from an existing Orcasound hydrophone workflow as a reusable template, we generate and refine workflows for air quality, earthquake, and soil moisture monitoring applications. We further show how these workflows extend to edge resources-including BlueField-3 DPUs and Raspberry Pis-through configuration and placement rather than workflow redesign. Our evaluation, from the perspective of a novice Pegasus user, shows that AI-assisted pattern reuse compresses multi-stage workflow development to 1-1.5 days per workflow while preserving the rigor and portability of workflow-based execution.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于模式、AI辅助的快速开发方法，利用Pegasus工作流和FABRIC测试台，将传感器数据从边缘到云的分析流程开发时间压缩至1-1.5天。
ai.motivation: 科学家依赖传感器数据，但将原始流转化为洞察需要跨边缘到云的广泛专业知识，现有开发流程复杂耗时。
ai.method: 采用5步开发循环，从代码优先转向意图优先设计；以现有Orcasound水听器工作流为模板，通过AI辅助模式复用生成空气质量、地震、土壤湿度监测工作流，并通过配置而非重新设计扩展到边缘资源（BlueField-3 DPU、树莓派）。
ai.result: 从新手Pegasus用户角度评估，AI辅助模式复用将多阶段工作流开发压缩至每个工作流1-1.5天，同时保持工作流执行的严谨性和可移植性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助传感器应用开发者快速构建从边缘到云的分析管道，降低多领域专业知识门槛，加速原型开发。

idea_spark.idea: 结合大型语言模型自动生成工作流模板，并基于传感器类型和部署环境智能推荐最优边缘计算资源配置。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖AI模板可能导致对特定场景的优化不足；边缘设备异构性可能使配置自动化复杂化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件工程中的设计模式复用和低代码平台获得启发，将类似思想应用于科学工作流开发。
