papers.title: Automated Channel Fault Analysis with Tofu
papers.summary: Distributed protocols are the linchpin of the modern internet, underpinning every internet service. This has in turn motivated a massive body of research ensuring the security, reliability, and performance of distributed protocols. In these works, a wide-ranging assumption is that distributed protocols operate over faulty or attacker-controlled channels, where messages can be arbitrarily inserted, dropped, replayed, or reordered. Formal verification work targeting distributed protocols typically defines its own notion of faulty or malicious channels, then constructively proves their protocol is correct with respect to it. In this work we take a fundamentally different approach: we develop a rigorous methodology for automatically conducting channel fault analysis on distributed protocols, and we introduce Tofu, a generalizable tool that implements our methodology. Tofu provides sound, complete analysis, synthesizing channel fault-based attack traces on arbitrary linear temporal logic (LTL) protocol specifications or proving the absence of such through an exhaustive state-space search. We demonstrate the applicability of Tofu by employing it to study TCP.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种自动分析分布式协议信道故障的方法，并实现了工具Tofu，能够合成攻击轨迹或证明无此类攻击。
ai.motivation: 现有分布式协议的形式化验证通常自行定义故障信道模型，缺乏通用的自动分析方法。
ai.method: 基于线性时序逻辑（LTL）规范，通过穷举状态空间搜索自动合成信道故障攻击轨迹或证明其不存在。
ai.result: Tofu提供完备的分析，并在TCP协议上展示了其适用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动发现分布式协议中因信道故障导致的安全漏洞，或验证协议在信道故障下的正确性。

idea_spark.idea: 将信道故障模型形式化并集成到LTL规范中，实现自动化攻击轨迹合成。
idea_spark.risk: 状态空间爆炸可能限制对复杂协议的分析规模。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴模型检验技术，将协议安全属性转化为可自动验证的LTL公式。

en.tldr: Proposes an automated methodology for channel fault analysis in distributed protocols, implemented in Tofu, which synthesizes attack traces or proves their absence.
en.motivation: Existing formal verification of distributed protocols often defines ad hoc faulty channel models, lacking a general automated analysis approach.
en.method: Based on linear temporal logic (LTL) specifications, performs exhaustive state-space search to synthesize channel fault attack traces or prove their absence.
en.result: Tofu provides sound and complete analysis, demonstrated on TCP.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically discover security vulnerabilities due to channel faults in distributed protocols, or verify correctness under such faults.

idea_spark.idea: Formalize channel fault models and integrate them into LTL specifications for automated attack trace synthesis.
idea_spark.risk: State-space explosion may limit scalability to complex protocols.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by model checking techniques, translating protocol security properties into automatically verifiable LTL formulas.

papers.title: The Productivity-Reliability Paradox: Specification-Driven Governance for AI-Augmented Software Development
papers.summary: Since 2022, AI-powered coding assistants have produced contradictory evidence: controlled studies report 20-56% productivity gains on well-scoped tasks, while the most rigorous RCT documents a 19% slowdown for experienced developers, and telemetry across 10,000+ developers shows 98% more pull requests but 91% longer review times with flat delivery metrics. This paper argues these findings constitute the Productivity-Reliability Paradox (PRP): a systematic phenomenon emerging from non-deterministic code generators and insufficient specification discipline. Through a multivocal literature review of 67 sources (2022-2026), this paper: (1) formally defines the PRP with three moderating variables (task abstraction, codebase maturity, developer experience) and two amplifying mechanisms (code review bottleneck, context window constraint); (2) proposes the AI-Augmented Methodology Taxonomy (AAMT), classifying six methodologies under three AI integration tiers; (3) introduces the Specification Governance Model (SGM), grounded in Transaction Cost Economics, with a practical governance decision guide; and (4) evaluates Spec Kit and TDAD as SGM instantiations via a four-month pilot study. Specification discipline, not model capability, is the binding constraint on AI-assisted software dependability.

ai.tldr: 本文提出生产力-可靠性悖论（PRP），指出规范纪律而非模型能力是AI辅助软件开发可靠性的关键约束，并通过多声部文献综述、分类法和治理模型进行论证。
ai.motivation: 现有研究显示AI编码助手在受控实验中提升生产力，但在实际开发中导致效率下降和审查时间延长，存在矛盾证据。
ai.method: 采用多声部文献综述（67篇文献，2022-2026），提出PRP定义、AAMT分类法、基于交易成本经济学的SGM模型，并通过四个月试点研究评估Spec Kit和TDAD。
ai.result: 规范纪律是AI辅助软件开发可靠性的关键约束，而非模型能力；SGM模型可有效缓解PRP。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解AI辅助开发中的生产力与可靠性矛盾，并提供规范治理的实用决策指南。

idea_spark.idea: 开发基于规范纪律的自动化代码审查工具，结合交易成本经济学优化开发流程。
idea_spark.risk: 过度规范可能抑制开发灵活性，需平衡规范与效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将经济学理论（交易成本）应用于软件工程治理，为AI辅助开发提供新视角。

en.tldr: This paper proposes the Productivity-Reliability Paradox (PRP), arguing that specification discipline, not model capability, is the binding constraint on AI-assisted software dependability, supported by a multivocal literature review, taxonomy, and governance model.
en.motivation: Contradictory evidence shows AI coding assistants boost productivity in controlled studies but cause slowdowns and longer review times in real-world settings.
en.method: Multivocal literature review of 67 sources (2022-2026), formal definition of PRP, AI-Augmented Methodology Taxonomy (AAMT), Specification Governance Model (SGM) grounded in Transaction Cost Economics, and a four-month pilot study evaluating Spec Kit and TDAD.

en.help_to_user: Helps understand the productivity-reliability paradox in AI-assisted development and provides a practical governance decision guide.

idea_spark.idea: Develop an automated code review tool based on specification discipline, integrating transaction cost economics to optimize development workflows.
idea_spark.risk: Over-specification may reduce development flexibility; a balance between specification and efficiency is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Applying economic theory (transaction cost) to software engineering governance offers a novel perspective for AI-assisted development.

papers.title: MORPH: Multi-Environment Orchestrated Reinforcement Learning for PRB Handling in O-RAN
papers.summary: Reinforcement-learning (RL) solutions for dynamic spectrum access and radio resource management in Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) depend critically on the fidelity of the throughput signal used for training. Analytical or physical-layer (PHY)-only simulators scale well but often miss protocol-stack effects such as signaling overhead and retransmissions, whereas exhaustive throughput profiling on a standards-compliant 5G stack is slow and can be unstable under software execution constraints. This paper presents MORPH, a measurement-grounded multi-environment RL pipeline {for slice-aware PRB-level spectrum allocation (spectrum sharing and slice isolation within a single gNB)} built on OpenAirInterface (OAI) 5G-NR RF-simulator mode. MORPH leverages three complementary throughput sources: (i) application-layer throughput measured via \texttt{iPerf} on the OAI stack under controlled AWGN pathloss settings, (ii) empirical MCS-selection distributions conditioned on path loss, enabling a distribution-aware theoretical throughput estimator that reflects standards-compliant link adaptation, and (iii) scalable throughput estimates from a 3GPP-parameterized PHY-fidelity OFDM simulator. Using these components, we train and compare agents that differ only in the origin of their throughput feedback: an OAI-grounded practical agent, a simulator-driven agent, and MORPH, which fuses real and synthetic throughput signals for policy optimization. Evaluation on the OAI execution harness across heterogeneous slicing scenarios shows that MORPH yields more robust slice-wise performance and improved SLA compliance than single-source training, providing a practical foundation for PRB-level spectrum sharing and slice isolation within a single-cell stack and a stepping stone toward multi-cell spectrum coordination and interference management.

ai.tldr: 提出MORPH，一种多环境强化学习流水线，融合实测与仿真吞吐信号，用于O-RAN中PRB级频谱分配与切片隔离。
ai.motivation: 现有RL方案依赖的吞吐信号要么来自简化仿真（忽略协议栈效应），要么来自完整5G协议栈（慢且不稳定），缺乏兼顾保真度与可扩展性的训练信号。
ai.method: 构建三源吞吐信号：OAI协议栈iPerf实测、基于MCS分布的理论估计、3GPP参数化OFDM仿真；设计多环境RL流水线融合这些信号进行策略优化。
ai.result: 在OAI执行框架上的异构切片场景评估表明，MORPH比单源训练获得更鲁棒的切片性能与更好的SLA合规性。
ai.help_to_user: 为O-RAN中基于强化学习的PRB级频谱共享与切片隔离提供了实用且可扩展的训练框架。

idea_spark.idea: 利用MCS分布构建分布感知的理论吞吐估计器，替代传统单一链路自适应模型。
idea_spark.risk: 多环境信号融合可能引入不一致性，需仔细设计融合权重或自适应机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将协议栈实测与物理层仿真互补，类似数字孪生思想，可推广至其他通信系统优化。

en.tldr: Proposes MORPH, a multi-environment RL pipeline that fuses real and simulated throughput signals for PRB-level spectrum allocation and slice isolation in O-RAN.
en.motivation: Existing RL solutions rely on throughput signals that are either from simplified simulators (missing protocol-stack effects) or from full 5G stacks (slow and unstable), lacking both fidelity and scalability.
en.method: Constructs three complementary throughput sources: iPerf measurements on OAI stack, distribution-aware theoretical estimator based on empirical MCS selection, and scalable OFDM simulator; designs a multi-environment RL pipeline to fuse these signals for policy optimization.
en.result: Evaluation on OAI execution harness across heterogeneous slicing scenarios shows MORPH yields more robust slice-wise performance and improved SLA compliance than single-source training.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical and scalable training framework for RL-based PRB-level spectrum sharing and slice isolation in O-RAN.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage MCS distribution to build a distribution-aware theoretical throughput estimator, replacing traditional single link adaptation model.
idea_spark.risk: Fusing signals from multiple environments may introduce inconsistencies, requiring careful design of fusion weights or adaptive mechanisms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Complementing protocol-stack measurements with PHY simulations resembles digital twin concepts and can be extended to other communication system optimizations.

papers.title: Needle-in-RAG: Prompt-Conditioned Character-Level Traceback of Poisoned Spans in Retrieved Evidence
papers.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) improves factual grounding by conditioning large language models on retrieved evidence, but it also opens a data-layer attack surface: poisoned corpus entries can steer outputs without changing model parameters. Existing defenses and traceback methods are largely passage-level, which is too coarse for modern attacks whose effective payload may be a short fabricated claim, trigger phrase, or hidden instruction embedded inside an otherwise benign chunk. We study black-box character-level poison traceback in RAG and present RAGCharacter, a two-pass forensic framework that localizes the responsible retrieved span for a concrete misgeneration event. Pass-0 runs standard RAG while logging a prompt-anchored execution trace. Pass-1 re-enters a triggered trace and performs event-conditioned traceback over prompt-used evidence via budgeted counterfactual masking and replay, yielding an attribution span for forensic reporting and a causal span under the logged trace. We further introduce an evaluation protocol that measures both event-level chunk traceback and character-level localization fidelity. Across two QA corpora, five poisoning attack families, six target LLMs, and multiple passage- and character-level baselines, RAGCharacter achieves the best overall trade-off within our benchmark between localization accuracy and low over-attribution. These results suggest that prompt-conditioned, black-box character-level traceback can be feasible, moving RAG forensics from document-level suspicion toward finer-grained evidence auditing and potential remediation.

ai.tldr: 提出RAGCharacter框架，在检索增强生成中实现字符级别的毒化跨度回溯，从文档级怀疑推进到细粒度证据审计。
ai.motivation: 现有防御和回溯方法多为段落级别，无法定位现代攻击中嵌入在良性文本内的短小伪造声明或触发短语。
ai.method: 两遍取证框架：Pass-0记录提示锚定的执行轨迹，Pass-1通过预算约束的反事实掩码和重放进行事件条件回溯，输出归因跨度和因果跨度。
ai.result: 在多个数据集、攻击族和目标LLM上，RAGCharacter在定位准确性和低过度归因之间取得最佳平衡。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员和系统管理员在RAG系统中精确追溯恶意注入的文本片段，提升数据层攻击的检测与修复能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将提示条件与反事实推理结合，实现无需模型内部信息的黑盒字符级回溯。
idea_spark.risk: 反事实掩码可能因预算限制遗漏关键跨度，或在高噪声环境下产生误报。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受因果推断中反事实推理启发，将事件回溯转化为条件掩码下的生成差异分析。

zh.tldr: 提出RAGCharacter框架，在检索增强生成中实现字符级别的毒化跨度回溯，从文档级怀疑推进到细粒度证据审计。
zh.motivation: 现有防御和回溯方法多为段落级别，无法定位现代攻击中嵌入在良性文本内的短小伪造声明或触发短语。

idea_spark.risk: 反事实掩码可能因预算限制遗漏关键跨度，或在高噪声环境下产生误报。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受因果推断中反事实推理启发，将事件回溯转化为条件掩码下的生成差异分析。

en.tldr: Proposes RAGCharacter, a framework for character-level traceback of poisoned spans in retrieval-augmented generation, moving from document-level suspicion to fine-grained evidence auditing.
en.motivation: Existing defenses and traceback methods are mostly passage-level, too coarse to locate short fabricated claims or trigger phrases embedded in benign text by modern attacks.
en.method: Two-pass forensic framework: Pass-0 logs a prompt-anchored execution trace; Pass-1 performs event-conditioned traceback via budgeted counterfactual masking and replay, outputting attribution and causal spans.
en.result: Across multiple datasets, attack families, and target LLMs, RAGCharacter achieves the best trade-off between localization accuracy and low over-attribution.
en.help_to_user: Helps security researchers and system administrators precisely trace maliciously injected text spans in RAG systems, enhancing detection and remediation of data-layer attacks.

idea_spark.idea: Combines prompt conditioning with counterfactual reasoning to achieve black-box character-level traceback without internal model access.
idea_spark.risk: Counterfactual masking may miss critical spans due to budget constraints or produce false positives in high-noise settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by counterfactual reasoning in causal inference, reformulating event traceback as generation difference analysis under conditional masking.

papers.title: FIRCE: A Framework for Intrusion Response and Conformal Evaluation
papers.summary: Machine learning-based intrusion detection systems deployed in real-world environments frequently suffer from model degradation due to concept drift, where changes in traffic patterns invalidate training assumptions. To address this, we present FIRCE, a Framework for Intrusion Response and Conformal Evaluation that augments supervised IDS classifiers with conformal evaluation-based uncertainty quantification and drift detection. FIRCE supports four conformal evaluation strategies: Inductive, Cross, Approximate Transductive, and our proposed Approximate Cross-Conformal Evaluator, which achieves robust performance with minimal calibration overhead. FIRCE also introduces an adaptive chunking mechanism that dynamically adjusts evaluation granularity in response to stream volatility, improving drift responsiveness while preserving computational efficiency. Using a custom IoT testbed of 10 commercial devices and time-series network captures under simulated attack and drift conditions, we demonstrate FIRCE's ability to detect distributional shifts and trigger model retraining. We additionally benchmark FIRCE on the CICIDS2018 and UNSW-NB15 datasets to validate its generalizability. Experimental results show that conformal evaluation-based drift detection, combined with adaptive chunking, enables an efficient and robust response to evolving threats.

ai.tldr: 提出FIRCE框架，通过保形评估量化不确定性和检测概念漂移，结合自适应分块机制，提升入侵检测系统在动态环境中的鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 真实环境中基于机器学习的入侵检测系统常因概念漂移导致模型退化，现有方法缺乏有效的漂移检测与响应机制。
ai.method: 在监督式IDS分类器上集成保形评估（包括归纳、交叉、近似传导和提出的近似交叉保形评估器），并引入自适应分块机制动态调整评估粒度。
ai.result: 在自建IoT测试台和CICIDS2018、UNSW-NB15数据集上，保形评估驱动的漂移检测结合自适应分块能高效鲁棒地响应演化威胁。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全运维人员理解如何利用保形评估和自适应分块来增强IDS对数据漂移的适应能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将自适应分块与在线学习结合，实现更细粒度的模型更新策略。
idea_spark.risk: 保形评估的计算开销在高速网络环境中可能成为瓶颈，需优化实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从保形预测的统计保证出发，探索其在对抗性漂移下的鲁棒性边界。

idea_spark.risk: 保形评估的计算开销在高速网络环境中可能成为瓶颈，需优化实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从保形预测的统计保证出发，探索其在对抗性漂移下的鲁棒性边界。

en.tldr: Proposes FIRCE, a framework that augments supervised IDS with conformal evaluation for uncertainty quantification and drift detection, plus adaptive chunking for efficient response to evolving threats.
en.motivation: ML-based IDS in real-world environments suffer from model degradation due to concept drift, lacking effective drift detection and response mechanisms.
en.method: Integrates conformal evaluation strategies (Inductive, Cross, Approximate Transductive, and proposed Approximate Cross-Conformal Evaluator) into supervised IDS classifiers, with an adaptive chunking mechanism that dynamically adjusts evaluation granularity.
en.result: On a custom IoT testbed and CICIDS2018/UNSW-NB15 datasets, conformal evaluation-based drift detection combined with adaptive chunking enables efficient and robust response to evolving threats.
en.help_to_user: Helps security practitioners understand how to leverage conformal evaluation and adaptive chunking to enhance IDS resilience against data drift.

idea_spark.idea: Combine adaptive chunking with online learning for finer-grained model update strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Computational overhead of conformal evaluation may become a bottleneck in high-speed network environments; real-time optimization is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore the robustness boundaries of conformal prediction under adversarial drift, leveraging its statistical guarantees.

papers.title: Cross-Layer Energy Analysis of Multimodal Training on Grace Hopper Superchips
papers.summary: Multimodal deep learning models enable joint learning across heterogeneous data sources, including text, images, and video, but their rapid scaling introduces significant memory and communication bottlenecks. As model sizes and sequence lengths increase, training performance becomes increasingly impacted by data movement rather than computation. Frameworks such as DeepSpeed mitigate these challenges through CPU offloading, activation checkpointing, and communication optimizations. However, these techniques introduce additional system activity, which may affect energy efficiency. Meanwhile, tightly integrated heterogeneous architectures, such as the NVIDIA Grace Hopper (GH200) superchip, provide high-bandwidth CPU-GPU interconnects and unified memory, thereby reducing data transfer overhead. In this work, we present a cross-layer analysis of energy and performance trade-offs in multimodal training on GH200 systems, explicitly characterizing the interactions between application, runtime, and hardware layers. Leveraging high-bandwidth CPU-GPU interconnects, our results show that energy efficiency is primarily governed by data movement and overlap rather than raw compute utilization, and that configurations optimized for runtime are not necessarily optimal for energy. Based on these findings, we distill a set of actionable guidelines for practitioners that demonstrate how to balance offloading strategies, sequence parallelism, and hardware-aware scheduling to achieve energy-efficient training. Our results demonstrate that leveraging high-bandwidth CPU-GPU interconnects enables offloading strategies and sequence parallelism, achieving a strong balance among energy efficiency, runtime performance, and computational throughput, providing practical guidelines for efficient multimodal training on modern heterogeneous systems.

ai.tldr: 本文在NVIDIA Grace Hopper超算芯片上对多模态训练进行了跨层能量分析，发现能量效率主要由数据移动和重叠决定，而非计算利用率，并提出了兼顾能效与性能的实用指南。
ai.motivation: 多模态模型规模快速增长导致内存和通信瓶颈，现有优化技术可能影响能效，而Grace Hopper架构提供了高带宽CPU-GPU互连，需要系统分析其能量与性能权衡。
ai.method: 通过跨层（应用、运行时、硬件）分析，在GH200系统上表征数据移动、卸载策略、序列并行与硬件感知调度对能量效率的影响。
ai.result: 能量效率主要受数据移动和重叠控制，运行时最优配置不一定能量最优；利用高带宽互连可实现卸载与序列并行，在能效、运行时间和吞吐量间取得良好平衡。
ai.help_to_user: 为在多模态训练中平衡卸载策略、序列并行和硬件感知调度以优化能效提供了可操作指南。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应调度器，根据实时数据移动开销动态调整卸载和并行策略，以最小化能量延迟积。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应调度可能引入额外运行时开销，且对硬件计数器精度敏感，需验证其在不同工作负载下的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受“数据移动而非计算主导能效”启发，可探索利用近内存计算或存内计算进一步减少数据搬运。

zh.tldr: 本文在NVIDIA Grace Hopper超算芯片上对多模态训练进行了跨层能量分析，发现能量效率主要由数据移动和重叠决定，而非计算利用率，并提出了兼顾能效与性能的实用指南。
zh.motivation: 多模态模型规模快速增长导致内存和通信瓶颈，现有优化技术可能影响能效，而Grace Hopper架构提供了高带宽CPU-GPU互连，需要系统分析其能量与性能权衡。
zh.method: 通过跨层（应用、运行时、硬件）分析，在GH200系统上表征数据移动、卸载策略、序列并行与硬件感知调度对能量效率的影响。
zh.result: 能量效率主要受数据移动和重叠控制，运行时最优配置不一定能量最优；利用高带宽互连可实现卸载与序列并行，在能效、运行时间和吞吐量间取得良好平衡。
zh.help_to_user: 为在多模态训练中平衡卸载策略、序列并行和硬件感知调度以优化能效提供了可操作指南。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应调度器，根据实时数据移动开销动态调整卸载和并行策略，以最小化能量延迟积。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应调度可能引入额外运行时开销，且对硬件计数器精度敏感，需验证其在不同工作负载下的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受“数据移动而非计算主导能效”启发，可探索利用近内存计算或存内计算进一步减少数据搬运。

en.tldr: This paper presents a cross-layer energy analysis of multimodal training on NVIDIA Grace Hopper superchips, revealing that energy efficiency is governed by data movement and overlap rather than compute utilization, and provides actionable guidelines for balancing offloading, sequence parallelism, and hardware-aware scheduling.
en.motivation: Rapid scaling of multimodal models introduces memory and communication bottlenecks; existing optimizations may affect energy efficiency, and the Grace Hopper architecture with high-bandwidth CPU-GPU interconnects requires systematic analysis of energy-performance trade-offs.
en.method: Cross-layer analysis (application, runtime, hardware) on GH200 systems characterizing the impact of data movement, offloading strategies, sequence parallelism, and hardware-aware scheduling on energy efficiency.
en.result: Energy efficiency is primarily governed by data movement and overlap; runtime-optimal configurations are not necessarily energy-optimal. Leveraging high-bandwidth interconnects enables offloading and sequence parallelism, achieving a strong balance among energy efficiency, runtime, and throughput.
en.help_to_user: Provides actionable guidelines for practitioners to balance offloading strategies, sequence parallelism, and hardware-aware scheduling for energy-efficient multimodal training.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive scheduler that dynamically adjusts offloading and parallelism strategies based on real-time data movement overhead to minimize energy-delay product.
idea_spark.risk: Adaptive scheduling may introduce runtime overhead and is sensitive to hardware counter accuracy; robustness across workloads needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the finding that data movement dominates energy efficiency, explore near-memory or in-memory computing to further reduce data movement.

papers.title: CvxCluster: Solving Large, Complex, Granular Resource Allocation Problems 100-1000x Faster
papers.summary: Cluster resource allocation is a multidimensional search problem that finds the best allocation of tasks to servers. Because the search space grows exponentially, modern approaches frame it as a mixed integer program (MIP) or a complex set of search heuristics. This paper proposes using a different approach: convex optimization, which has extremely fast solution methods. The research challenge is devising how to transform cluster resource allocation into a convex problem that generates good placements. We describe CvxCluster, which allocates cluster resources with a two-stage algorithm. The first stage solves a convex relaxation of the placement problem to yield a principled set of per-machine resource prices. The second stage uses these prices to drive a lightweight greedy procedure to place tasks. Experimental results with Azure traces find that CvxCluster scales to 100,480 servers under proportional workload growth and sustains arrival rates up to 500,000x the baseline trace. CvxCluster runs 100 to 2,500x faster than a state-of-the-art MIP solver while remaining within 3% of the optimal objective. CvxCluster can support complex constraints such as job anti-affinity, machine types, and GPU servers. The key insight behind CvxCluster is that reformulating placement as a continuous rather than discrete problem enables much faster methods that find solutions just as good or better than prior heuristics.

ai.tldr: 提出CvxCluster，通过凸优化将集群资源分配问题转化为连续优化，实现比MIP快100-2500倍且目标值差距在3%以内。
ai.motivation: 传统集群资源分配使用混合整数规划或启发式搜索，求解速度慢且难以扩展。
ai.method: 两阶段算法：第一阶段求解凸松弛得到每台机器的资源价格，第二阶段用价格驱动轻量级贪心放置任务。
ai.result: 在Azure轨迹上扩展到100,480台服务器，支持复杂约束，速度提升100-2500倍，目标值接近最优。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速高效地解决大规模、细粒度的集群资源分配问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将离散放置问题连续化，利用凸优化快速求解，再通过价格信号引导贪心决策。
idea_spark.risk: 凸松弛可能无法保证所有约束的精确满足，需要验证贪心阶段对复杂约束的处理能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续优化中提取价格信息来指导离散决策，类似经济学中的市场机制，可启发其他资源分配场景。

zh.motivation: 传统集群资源分配使用混合整数规划或启发式搜索，求解速度慢且难以扩展。
zh.method: 两阶段算法：第一阶段求解凸松弛得到每台机器的资源价格，第二阶段用价格驱动轻量级贪心放置任务。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速高效地解决大规模、细粒度的集群资源分配问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将离散放置问题连续化，利用凸优化快速求解，再通过价格信号引导贪心决策。
idea_spark.risk: 凸松弛可能无法保证所有约束的精确满足，需要验证贪心阶段对复杂约束的处理能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续优化中提取价格信息来指导离散决策，类似经济学中的市场机制，可启发其他资源分配场景。

en.tldr: Proposes CvxCluster, which reformulates cluster resource allocation as a convex optimization problem, achieving 100-2500x speedup over MIP with objective within 3% of optimal.
en.motivation: Traditional cluster resource allocation uses mixed integer programming or heuristics, which are slow and hard to scale.
en.method: Two-stage algorithm: first stage solves a convex relaxation to obtain per-machine resource prices; second stage uses prices to drive a lightweight greedy placement.
en.result: Scales to 100,480 servers on Azure traces, supports complex constraints, runs 100-2500x faster than MIP with near-optimal objective.
en.help_to_user: Helps users solve large-scale, granular cluster resource allocation problems quickly and efficiently.

idea_spark.idea: Convert discrete placement into a continuous problem, leverage fast convex solvers, and use price signals to guide greedy decisions.
idea_spark.risk: Convex relaxation may not guarantee exact satisfaction of all constraints; need to verify greedy stage's handling of complex constraints.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extracting price information from continuous optimization to guide discrete decisions, similar to market mechanisms in economics, can inspire other resource allocation scenarios.

papers.title: Understanding Simulated Architecture via gem5 Call-Stack Profiling
papers.summary: Understanding the behavior of simulated architectures in gem5 is critical for studying complex, deeply integrated computing systems. However, conventional analysis methods provide only an indirect view of the simulated system internals. In this work, we show that call-stack profiling of gem5 itself offers a powerful yet underutilized perspective: the simulator's own call-stack directly reflects the activity of the simulated system, exposing insights that conventional statistics may overlook.   Profiling gem5's call-stacks is challenging due to its highly layered and complex software design patterns. To address this, we introduce a specialized, lightweight profiling framework built on Linux's perf_event interface which samples gem5's runtime call-stacks throughout the simulation, resolves symbols on the fly, and merges samples into a hierarchical call-tree representation supporting both high-level structural views and focused, user-defined, component-specific analysis. Moreover, all profiling is performed in a separate process running alongside the main gem5 process, avoiding intrusive changes and overheads to the simulation itself.   We apply our framework to gem5's three major CPU models -- AtomicSimpleCPU, TimingSimpleCPU, and O3CPU -- together with the Ruby memory system, and uncover behaviors that are not easily observable in conventional gem5 statistics. Our case studies reveal, for example, that TimingSimpleCPU is inefficient due to its use of a lockup-cache model and, despite its conceptual simplicity, does not simulate faster than a full out-of-order core. In addition, our tool makes it straightforward to detect cache coherence protocol deadlock and livelock -- issues that are otherwise difficult to identify, since the simulation either appears to run normally or terminates abruptly, making it hard to pinpoint when these conditions occur.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于gem5调用栈的轻量级剖析框架，通过perf_event采样、实时符号解析和分层调用树表示，揭示模拟架构的内部行为。
ai.motivation: 传统分析方法只能间接观察模拟系统内部，而gem5自身的调用栈直接反映模拟系统的活动，但现有工具难以处理gem5高度分层和复杂的软件设计。
ai.method: 基于Linux perf_event接口，在独立进程中采样gem5运行时调用栈，实时解析符号，构建分层调用树，支持全局视图和组件级分析，且不侵入模拟主进程。
ai.result: 应用于AtomicSimpleCPU、TimingSimpleCPU、O3CPU及Ruby内存系统，发现TimingSimpleCPU因锁缓存模型导致效率低下，且能轻松检测缓存一致性协议死锁和活锁。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助架构研究人员深入理解gem5模拟器的内部行为，发现传统统计指标难以察觉的性能瓶颈和协议问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将调用栈剖析与硬件性能计数器结合，实现更细粒度的模拟行为诊断。
idea_spark.risk: 调用栈采样可能引入少量开销，且符号解析依赖调试信息，可能影响可移植性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模拟器自身调用栈作为“探针”来理解模拟系统，为系统软件性能分析提供新视角。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于gem5调用栈的轻量级剖析框架，通过perf_event采样、实时符号解析和分层调用树表示，揭示模拟架构的内部行为。
zh.motivation: 传统分析方法只能间接观察模拟系统内部，而gem5自身的调用栈直接反映模拟系统的活动，但现有工具难以处理gem5高度分层和复杂的软件设计。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助架构研究人员深入理解gem5模拟器的内部行为，发现传统统计指标难以察觉的性能瓶颈和协议问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将调用栈剖析与硬件性能计数器结合，实现更细粒度的模拟行为诊断。
idea_spark.risk: 调用栈采样可能引入少量开销，且符号解析依赖调试信息，可能影响可移植性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模拟器自身调用栈作为“探针”来理解模拟系统，为系统软件性能分析提供新视角。

en.tldr: A lightweight call-stack profiling framework for gem5 using perf_event, on-the-fly symbol resolution, and hierarchical call-tree representation to reveal internal behaviors of simulated architectures.
en.motivation: Conventional analysis provides only indirect views of simulated system internals; gem5's own call-stack directly reflects simulated activity, but existing tools struggle with gem5's layered and complex design.
en.method: A separate profiling process samples gem5's runtime call-stacks via Linux perf_event, resolves symbols on the fly, and builds a hierarchical call-tree supporting both high-level and component-specific analysis without intrusive changes.
en.result: Applied to AtomicSimpleCPU, TimingSimpleCPU, O3CPU, and Ruby memory system, revealing TimingSimpleCPU's inefficiency due to lockup-cache model and enabling straightforward detection of cache coherence deadlocks and livelocks.
en.help_to_user: Helps architecture researchers deeply understand gem5 simulator internals and uncover performance bottlenecks and protocol issues invisible to conventional statistics.

idea_spark.idea: Combine call-stack profiling with hardware performance counters for finer-grained diagnosis of simulation behavior.
idea_spark.risk: Call-stack sampling may introduce slight overhead, and symbol resolution relies on debug information, potentially affecting portability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using the simulator's own call-stack as a probe to understand the simulated system offers a new perspective for system software performance analysis.

papers.title: Tool Use as Action: Towards Agentic Control in Mobile Core Networks
papers.summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) will play an essential role in 6G. It will fundamentally reshape the network architecture itself and drive major changes in the design of network entities, interfaces, and procedures. The adoption of agentic AI in next-generation networks is expected to enhance network intelligence and autonomy through agents capable of planning, reasoning, and acting, while also opening up new business opportunities. Under this vision, existing network functions are expected to evolve into AI-enabled agents and tools that deliver both connectivity and beyond-connectivity services. As an initial attempt to move toward this vision, this paper presents a tool-based interface design and an experimental prototype that are based on agentic AI for the mobile core network, with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol as foundational protocols. MCP is selected to design the interface between the agent and network tools, and the A2A protocol is used for message exchange between AI agents. In such an experimental setup, we analyze packet-level message flows between the agents, tools, and network functions and break down the latency of end-to-end operations, starting from the prompt injection until the completion of the input task. This work demonstrates how an AI agent-based core network combined with network-specific tools can be utilized in next generation mobile systems to execute intent-based tasks.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于工具接口和智能体AI的移动核心网络设计，利用MCP和A2A协议实现智能体与网络工具的交互，并通过实验原型分析了端到端操作的延迟。
ai.motivation: 6G网络需要更高层次的智能和自主性，现有网络功能需演变为AI智能体和工具，以支持意图驱动的任务。
ai.method: 设计了基于MCP的智能体-工具接口和基于A2A协议的智能体间通信，构建了实验原型，并分析了数据包级消息流和延迟。
ai.result: 实验展示了AI智能体核心网络结合网络特定工具可执行意图任务，并量化了从提示注入到任务完成的延迟组成。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何将智能体AI集成到移动核心网络中，以及工具接口和协议选择对系统性能的影响。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将MCP和A2A协议用于跨域智能体协作，实现更复杂的网络自动化任务。
idea_spark.risk: 协议标准化和互操作性可能成为挑战，且延迟分析未覆盖大规模部署场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于智能体使用工具执行任务，可进一步研究工具库的动态扩展和智能体学习机制。

zh.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于工具接口和智能体AI的移动核心网络设计，利用MCP和A2A协议实现智能体与网络工具的交互，并通过实验原型分析了端到端操作的延迟。
zh.motivation: 6G网络需要更高层次的智能和自主性，现有网络功能需演变为AI智能体和工具，以支持意图驱动的任务。

idea_spark.risk: 协议标准化和互操作性可能成为挑战，且延迟分析未覆盖大规模部署场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于智能体使用工具执行任务，可进一步研究工具库的动态扩展和智能体学习机制。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a tool-based interface and agentic AI design for mobile core networks, using MCP and A2A protocols, and experimentally analyzes end-to-end latency from prompt injection to task completion.
en.motivation: 6G networks require higher intelligence and autonomy, necessitating the evolution of network functions into AI agents and tools for intent-driven tasks.
en.method: Designed an agent-tool interface based on MCP and inter-agent communication via A2A protocol, built an experimental prototype, and analyzed packet-level message flows and latency.
en.result: Demonstrated that an AI agent-based core network with network-specific tools can execute intent-based tasks, and quantified latency breakdown from prompt injection to task completion.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to integrate agentic AI into mobile core networks and the impact of tool interface and protocol choices on system performance.

idea_spark.idea: Explore using MCP and A2A protocols for cross-domain agent collaboration to achieve more complex network automation tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Protocol standardization and interoperability may pose challenges, and latency analysis does not cover large-scale deployment scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by agents using tools to perform tasks, further research could investigate dynamic expansion of tool libraries and agent learning mechanisms.

papers.title: AgenticVM: Agentic AI for Adaptive Software Vulnerability Management
papers.summary: As software systems grow in scale and complexity, vulnerability management is increasingly strained by high alert volumes, fragmented toolchains, and manual triage processes. We introduce AgenticVM, a multi-agent framework that integrates large language models with security tools to automate vulnerability detection, assessment, prioritization, and reporting. AgenticVM combines rule-based processing, a BERT-based CVSS prediction module, and specialised LLM-driven agents, leveraging data from sources such as the National Vulnerability Database and the European Union Vulnerability Database. Across multiple evaluation scenarios, AgenticVM reduces raw scanner outputs into compact, actionable queues, achieving up to 98% alert reduction (e.g., from 3,983 findings to 82 high-priority items), while predicting missing CVSS attributes with 89.3% accuracy. These results demonstrate improved prioritisation efficiency and reduced analyst workload without compromising risk visibility. Beyond performance, the framework provides practical design insights into agent decomposition, tool-LLM integration, and human-in-the-loop governance for real-world deployment.

ai.tldr: 提出AgenticVM多智能体框架，集成大语言模型与安全工具，自动完成漏洞检测、评估、优先级排序和报告，实现高达98%的告警缩减和89.3%的CVSS属性预测准确率。
ai.motivation: 软件系统规模与复杂度增长导致漏洞管理面临告警量大、工具链碎片化、人工分诊流程繁琐等挑战。
ai.method: 结合规则处理、基于BERT的CVSS预测模块和专用LLM驱动智能体，利用NVD与欧盟漏洞数据库等多源数据。
ai.result: 将原始扫描输出压缩为可操作队列，告警缩减率达98%（如从3983项降至82项高优先级），缺失CVSS属性预测准确率89.3%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全分析师减少手动分诊工作量，提升漏洞优先级排序效率，同时保持风险可见性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将AgenticVM框架应用于开源软件供应链漏洞的实时监控与自动修复建议生成。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖外部漏洞数据库的时效性与完整性；LLM可能产生误判或遗漏关键漏洞。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴agent分解与工具-LLM集成设计，构建面向DevSecOps的自动化安全编排平台。

zh.tldr: 提出AgenticVM多智能体框架，集成大语言模型与安全工具，自动完成漏洞检测、评估、优先级排序和报告，实现高达98%的告警缩减和89.3%的CVSS属性预测准确率。
zh.motivation: 软件系统规模与复杂度增长导致漏洞管理面临告警量大、工具链碎片化、人工分诊流程繁琐等挑战。
zh.method: 结合规则处理、基于BERT的CVSS预测模块和专用LLM驱动智能体，利用NVD与欧盟漏洞数据库等多源数据。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将AgenticVM框架应用于开源软件供应链漏洞的实时监控与自动修复建议生成。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖外部漏洞数据库的时效性与完整性；LLM可能产生误判或遗漏关键漏洞。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴agent分解与工具-LLM集成设计，构建面向DevSecOps的自动化安全编排平台。

en.tldr: Proposes AgenticVM, a multi-agent framework integrating LLMs with security tools to automate vulnerability detection, assessment, prioritization, and reporting, achieving up to 98% alert reduction and 89.3% accuracy in predicting missing CVSS attributes.
en.motivation: Growing scale and complexity of software systems strain vulnerability management with high alert volumes, fragmented toolchains, and manual triage.
en.method: Combines rule-based processing, a BERT-based CVSS prediction module, and specialized LLM-driven agents, leveraging data from NVD and EU Vulnerability Database.
en.result: Reduces raw scanner outputs to actionable queues with up to 98% alert reduction (e.g., from 3,983 findings to 82 high-priority items) and predicts missing CVSS attributes with 89.3% accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Helps security analysts reduce manual triage workload, improve prioritization efficiency, while maintaining risk visibility.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applying AgenticVM to real-time monitoring and automated fix suggestion for open-source software supply chain vulnerabilities.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on timeliness and completeness of external vulnerability databases; LLMs may produce false positives or miss critical vulnerabilities.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage agent decomposition and tool-LLM integration design to build an automated security orchestration platform for DevSecOps.

papers.title: EvoPoC: Automated Exploit Synthesis for DeFi Smart Contracts via Hierarchical Knowledge Graphs
papers.summary: Smart contract vulnerabilities in Decentralized Finance caused over billions of dollars losses every year, yet the security community faces a critical bottleneck: identifying a vulnerability is not the same as proving it is exploitable. Manual PoC construction is prohibitively labor-intensive, leaving most disclosed vulnerabilities unverified and protocols exposed long before mitigation is applied. In this paper, we propose \sys, a knowledge-driven agentic system for end-to-end contract vulnerability detection and exploit synthesis. Our core insight is that exploit synthesis is not a code generation task but a \emph{structured reasoning problem} that requires grounded knowledge of protocol semantics, failure root cause, and exploit primitives. \sys organizes this knowledge into a \emph{Hierarchical Knowledge Graph} (HKG) that serves as structured memory for LLM-guided multi-hop reasoning. To validate exploit feasibility beyond code synthesis, \sys employs a two-stage validation framework that checks exploit-path reachability via SMT solving and profit realizability via asset-level state simulation, ensuring generated PoCs satisfy both logical and economic viability constraints. Evaluated on 88 real-world DeFi attacks and 72 audited projects (2,573 contracts), \sys achieves 98\% recall and 0.9 F1-score in detection, and a 96.6\% exploit success rate (ESR), reproducing 85 historical exploits and recovering over \$116.2M revenue. \sys outperforms SOTA fuzzers (\textsc{Verite}, \textsc{ItyFuzz}) by up to $5\times$ in ESR and $300\times$ in recoverable value, and the LLM-based exploit generator \textsc{A1} by $2\times$ and $8.5\times$ respectively. In bug bounty evaluation, \sys identified 16 confirmed 0-day vulnerabilities, helping secure over \$70.6M and earning \$2,900 in bounties.

ai.tldr: 提出EvoPoC系统，利用分层知识图谱和LLM推理，自动合成DeFi智能合约漏洞利用代码，在88个真实攻击和2573个合约上达到96.6%的利用成功率，恢复超1.16亿美元。
ai.motivation: 手动构建PoC（概念验证）极其耗时，导致大多数已披露漏洞无法被验证，协议长期暴露在风险中。
ai.method: 构建分层知识图谱（HKG）作为结构化记忆，引导LLM进行多跳推理；采用两阶段验证框架：SMT求解检查路径可达性，资产级状态模拟检查利润可实现性。
ai.result: 检测召回率98%，F1分数0.9；利用成功率96.6%，复现85个历史漏洞，恢复超1.16亿美元；在漏洞赏金评估中发现16个零日漏洞，保护超7060万美元。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员自动验证DeFi合约漏洞的可利用性，生成可运行的PoC，加速漏洞修复和协议安全加固。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量知识图谱构建，对新型漏洞模式可能覆盖不足；SMT求解和模拟可能面临状态爆炸。

papers.title: Autonomous LLM Agent Worms: Cross-Platform Propagation, Automated Discovery and Temporal Re-Entry Defense
papers.summary: Autonomous LLM agents operate as long-running processes with persistent workspaces, memory files, scheduled task state, and messaging integrations. These features create a new propagation risk: attacker-influenced content can be written into persistent agent state, re-enter the LLM decision context through scheduled autoloading, and drive high-risk actions including configuration changes and cross-agent transmission. We present the first systematic framework for automated analysis of persistent worm propagation in file-backed multi-agent LLM ecosystems. SSCGV, our automated source-code graph analyzer, traces data flow from file I/O to LLM context injection points and ranks carriers by context injection position without manual analysis. SRPO, our summary-resilient payload optimizer, generates worm payloads robust to LLM-mediated summarization and paraphrasing across multi-hop communication. Evaluated on three production agent frameworks, we demonstrate zero-click autonomous propagation, 3-hop cross-platform transmission without platform-specific adaptation, inter-agent privilege escalation, and data exfiltration. We identify two empirical insights: user prompt carriers achieve higher attack compliance than system prompt carriers, and read operations represent the primary integrity threat in LLM-mediated systems. To defend against this class of attacks, we develop RTW-A, proven under a formal No Persistent Worm Propagation theorem. RTW blocks write-before-exposed-read re-entry; sealed configuration protects static files; typed memory promotion prevents untrusted summaries from entering trusted memory; and capability attenuation limits high-risk actions after external reads. These mechanisms eliminate the persistence, re-entry, action chain while preserving ordinary workflows. Affected systems are anonymized pending coordinated disclosure.

ai.tldr: 提出首个针对多智能体LLM生态系统中持久性蠕虫传播的自动化分析框架，包括源代码图分析器SSCGV和摘要鲁棒载荷优化器SRPO，并设计RTW-A防御机制。
ai.motivation: 自主LLM代理的持久化工作区、内存文件和定时任务状态等特性，使攻击者注入的内容可重新进入LLM决策上下文，驱动高风险操作，但缺乏系统性的自动化分析框架。
ai.method: SSCGV自动追踪文件I/O到LLM上下文注入点的数据流并排序载体；SRPO生成对LLM摘要和改写鲁棒的蠕虫载荷；RTW-A通过阻断写前读回、密封配置、类型化内存提升和能力衰减消除持久化-重入-动作链。
ai.result: 在三个生产级代理框架上实现零点击自主传播、3跳跨平台传输、权限提升和数据窃取；用户提示载体比系统提示载体攻击合规性更高；读操作是主要完整性威胁。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员理解并防御针对自主LLM代理的持久性蠕虫攻击，提供自动化分析工具和形式化验证的防御机制。

idea_spark.idea: 利用SSCGV和SRPO构建自动化红队工具，持续评估新代理框架的蠕虫传播风险。
idea_spark.risk: 防御机制可能影响正常代理工作流，需平衡安全性与可用性；攻击者可能绕过类型化内存提升或能力衰减。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物蠕虫传播启发，将文件I/O视为感染载体，LLM上下文注入视为感染点，设计自动化分析。

en.tldr: First systematic framework for automated analysis of persistent worm propagation in multi-agent LLM ecosystems, including SSCGV source-code graph analyzer and SRPO summary-resilient payload optimizer, with RTW-A defense.
en.motivation: Persistent workspaces, memory files, and scheduled task states in autonomous LLM agents allow attacker-injected content to re-enter LLM decision context, driving high-risk actions, yet no automated analysis framework exists.

idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological worm propagation, treating file I/O as infection vector and LLM context injection as infection point for automated analysis.

papers.title: APIOT: Autonomous Vulnerability Management Across Bare-Metal Industrial OT Networks
papers.summary: Bare-metal operational technology (OT) devices -- especially the microcontrollers running Modbus/TCP and CoAP at the base of industrial control systems -- have remained outside the reach of autonomous security attacks. Prior autonomous pentesting studies target Linux and web systems, whose shells and filesystems are familiar to LLM agents. Bare-metal OT has neither, so agents must reason directly over protocol fields and parser semantics. This requires new action-space designs and runtime controls, and opens new research questions about protocol-level exploit reasoning and its deployment envelope. We present APIOT (Autonomous Purple-teaming for Industrial OT), the first large language model (LLM) framework demonstrating an autonomous attack and remediation of bare-metal OT devices, achieving the full discovery -> exploitation -> patching -> verification cycle without step-by-step human intervention. We implemented and evaluated this framework on Zephyr RTOS firmware across heterogeneous industrial IoT (IIoT) topologies. Through 290 experiment runs spanning five frontier LLMs, three network topologies, two impairment levels, and guided versus unguided conditions, APIOT achieved a mission success rate of 90.0% on the full attack-remediation cycle. We found that the runtime governance layer (which we call an overseer) is a critical engineering variable: without it, agents exhibit systematic degenerate patterns, including repetition loops, missing crash verification, and reconnaissance deadlocks. Together, these findings carry two implications beyond our testbed. Attacker expertise is no longer the binding constraint on bare-metal OT exploitation, and defender threat models must now assume LLM-augmented adversaries capable of executing autonomous discovery-through-remediation cycles against industrial firmware.

ai.tldr: 提出首个针对裸机工业OT设备的自主攻击与修复框架APIOT，实现从发现到验证的全自动流程。
ai.motivation: 现有自主渗透测试仅适用于Linux和Web系统，裸机OT设备缺乏shell和文件系统，LLM代理需直接推理协议字段和解析器语义。
ai.method: 设计新的动作空间和运行时控制，引入监管层（overseer）防止代理退化模式，在Zephyr RTOS固件上跨异构IIoT拓扑进行290次实验。
ai.result: 在完整攻击-修复周期上达到90.0%任务成功率，发现无监管时代理出现重复循环、崩溃验证缺失和侦察死锁等退化模式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员理解LLM代理在裸机OT环境中的攻击与防御能力，以及运行时治理的重要性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将监管层设计为可学习的自适应策略，动态调整代理行为以应对未知协议异常。
idea_spark.risk: 若监管层被绕过或存在漏洞，可能导致代理失控或产生不可预测的攻击行为。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从生物免疫系统的分层防御机制中获取灵感，设计多级监管与自愈策略。

en.motivation: Existing autonomous pentesting targets Linux and web systems; bare-metal OT lacks shells and filesystems, requiring agents to reason directly over protocol fields and parser semantics.

idea_spark.idea: Explore designing the overseer as a learnable adaptive policy that dynamically adjusts agent behavior to handle unknown protocol anomalies.
idea_spark.risk: If the overseer is bypassed or has vulnerabilities, it may lead to uncontrolled or unpredictable agent actions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from the layered defense mechanisms of biological immune systems to design multi-level governance and self-healing strategies.

papers.title: CommitSuite: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Commit Classification and Message Generation
papers.summary: High-quality commit messages are critical for maintaining software projects, yet ensuring their consistency and informativeness remains a practical challenge. While the Conventional Commits Specification (CCS) provides a structured format for commit messages, research on CCS-based commit classification and commit message generation (CMG) is limited by the absence of large-scale benchmarks, semantic annotations, and reliable evaluation methods. In this paper, we introduce CommitSuite, a benchmark comprising 63,533 CCS-compliant commits from 243 open-source repositories across seven programming languages. Each commit is labeled with its CCS type and enriched with AST-level code changes, along with LLM-assisted semantic annotations that capture the "what" and "why" behind the change. To evaluate CMG systems, we propose a reference-free framework based on five binary metrics: rationality, comprehensiveness, non-redundancy, authenticity, and logicality, enabling semantic-level assessment without relying on human-written references. Our experiments show that LLMs can effectively support both generation and evaluation, with evaluation achieving 0.849 Cohen's Kappa agreement against human judgments. CommitSuite offers a unified resource for structured commit understanding and facilitates reproducible research on commit classification and generation.

ai.tldr: 提出了CommitSuite基准，包含63,533个符合CCS规范的提交，支持提交分类与消息生成，并设计了无参考评估框架。
ai.motivation: 现有研究缺乏大规模、语义标注的CCS提交基准和可靠的评估方法。
ai.method: 收集243个开源仓库的CCS提交，标注类型和AST变更，利用LLM生成语义注释；提出基于五个二元指标的无参考评估框架。
ai.result: LLM在生成和评估上均有效，评估与人工判断的Cohen's Kappa达0.849。
ai.help_to_user: 提供统一的结构化提交理解资源，便于复现提交分类与生成研究。

idea_spark.idea: 利用AST变更和LLM语义注释，可进一步探索代码变更与自然语言描述的细粒度对齐。
idea_spark.risk: 评估指标可能过度依赖LLM，需验证其在不同场景下的泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合结构化格式与语义理解，提升软件工程中自动化工具的可信度。

en.tldr: Proposes CommitSuite, a benchmark of 63,533 CCS-compliant commits for commit classification and message generation, with a reference-free evaluation framework.
en.motivation: Lack of large-scale, semantically annotated CCS commit benchmarks and reliable evaluation methods.
en.method: Collected CCS commits from 243 open-source repos, labeled types and AST changes, used LLM for semantic annotations; proposed a reference-free evaluation framework with five binary metrics.
en.result: LLMs effectively support both generation and evaluation, achieving 0.849 Cohen's Kappa agreement with human judgments.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified resource for structured commit understanding and facilitates reproducible research.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage AST changes and LLM semantic annotations to explore fine-grained alignment between code changes and natural language descriptions.
idea_spark.risk: Evaluation metrics may overly rely on LLMs; generalization across different scenarios needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine structured formats with semantic understanding to enhance the trustworthiness of automation tools in software engineering.

papers.title: InsureConnect: Blockchain and Digital Identity for the Property Insurance Market
papers.summary: This paper presents InsureConnect, a blockchain-based system for improving transparency, authentication, and auditability in property-insurance workflows after natural disasters. The system combines Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI), Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs), Verifiable Credentials (VCs), satellite imagery, Hyperledger Fabric, and IPFS to register identities, insurance contracts, and damage claims. Property images are stored off-chain in IPFS, while content hashes and signed records are maintained on a permissioned blockchain. Users interact with the system through a desktop application, while chaincode enforces role-based access control and validates digital signatures. The prototype was evaluated under concurrent request loads from 50 to 3000 requests, measuring latency, throughput, and dropped connections. The results indicate that the system sustains increasing throughput under load, although latency rises and dropped connections appear at higher concurrency levels.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于区块链和数字身份（SSI、DID、VC）的财产保险系统InsureConnect，结合卫星图像、Hyperledger Fabric和IPFS，实现灾后保险工作流的透明、认证和可审计性。
ai.motivation: 传统财产保险在灾后理赔中缺乏透明度、身份认证和审计追踪，导致欺诈和效率低下。
ai.method: 采用自主权身份（SSI）、去中心化标识符（DID）和可验证凭证（VC）注册身份与合同；财产图像存于IPFS，哈希和签名记录上链；链码实施基于角色的访问控制并验证数字签名。
ai.result: 在50至3000并发请求下测试，系统吞吐量随负载增加而提升，但延迟上升，高并发时出现连接丢失。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解如何利用区块链和数字身份技术构建可信、可审计的保险理赔系统。

idea_spark.idea: 结合卫星图像自动评估灾损，并利用智能合约自动触发理赔支付，减少人工干预。
idea_spark.risk: 卫星图像可能受天气或分辨率影响导致误判；高并发下系统性能瓶颈需进一步优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将去中心化身份与物联网传感器数据结合，实现实时灾损监测与自动理赔。

en.tldr: Presents InsureConnect, a blockchain-based system using SSI, DIDs, VCs, satellite imagery, Hyperledger Fabric, and IPFS to improve transparency, authentication, and auditability in property insurance workflows after natural disasters.
en.motivation: Traditional property insurance lacks transparency, identity authentication, and audit trails in post-disaster claims, leading to fraud and inefficiency.
en.method: Uses Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI), Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs), and Verifiable Credentials (VCs) to register identities and contracts; stores property images off-chain in IPFS with hashes and signed records on a permissioned blockchain; chaincode enforces role-based access control and validates digital signatures.
en.result: Tested under concurrent loads from 50 to 3000 requests, throughput increases with load, but latency rises and dropped connections occur at higher concurrency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to build a trusted, auditable insurance claims system using blockchain and digital identity.

idea_spark.idea: Combine satellite imagery for automatic damage assessment and use smart contracts to trigger claim payments automatically, reducing manual intervention.
idea_spark.risk: Satellite imagery may be affected by weather or resolution, leading to misjudgment; system performance bottlenecks under high concurrency need further optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrate decentralized identity with IoT sensor data for real-time damage monitoring and automated claims.

papers.title: From Stealthy Data Fabrication to Unsafe Driving: Realistic Scenario Attacks on Collaborative Perception
papers.summary: Collaborative perception allows connected and autonomous vehicles (CAVs) to improve perception by sharing sensory data, but it also introduces security risks from manipulated inputs. Prior work shows that attackers can spoof or remove objects by fabricating shared data, yet the practicality of such attacks in real-world driving remains unclear. Existing attacks are often detectable or evaluated in manually constructed scenarios, leaving open whether they can induce safety-critical outcomes in dynamic environments. To bridge this gap, we present a stealthy, scenario-realistic data fabrication attack that induces unsafe driving behaviors through end-to-end system effects. Instead of creating large, easily detectable anomalies, our attack subtly manipulates the poses of existing objects in shared perception results, keeping perturbations below detection thresholds. These small errors are then propagated through downstream modules, including object tracking and trajectory prediction, leading to significant deviations in predicted behaviors and ultimately unsafe driving decisions. We further design an online, scenario-aware attack framework that adapts to dynamic traffic conditions and optimizes attack strategies at runtime. Experiments on OPV2V and V2X-Real demonstrate that the attack achieves over 90% success in inducing detection errors and triggers safety-critical behaviors, such as unnecessary hard braking, in up to 50% of scenarios, while largely evading state-of-the-art defenses. We also propose a mitigation that focuses on detecting anomalies in localized, safety-critical regions, achieving an 80% detection rate on the small pose perturbation compared to 11% for the best existing methods.

ai.tldr: 提出一种隐蔽且场景逼真的数据篡改攻击，通过微小姿态扰动诱导不安全驾驶行为，并设计在线攻击框架，实验显示高成功率且能绕过现有防御。
ai.motivation: 现有协同感知攻击在真实驾驶场景中的实用性不明，且易被检测或仅在人工场景中评估。
ai.method: 通过篡改共享感知结果中已有物体的姿态，使扰动低于检测阈值，并利用下游模块传播误差，结合在线场景感知框架动态优化攻击策略。
ai.result: 在OPV2V和V2X-Real数据集上，攻击诱导检测错误成功率超90%，引发安全关键行为（如急刹车）达50%，同时绕过最先进防御；提出的局部安全区域异常检测方法将检测率从11%提升至80%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解协同感知系统面临的新型隐蔽攻击及其对驾驶安全的影响，并提供防御思路。

idea_spark.idea: 利用对抗性姿态扰动结合在线优化，实现低检测率下的安全关键行为诱导。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击可能被用于真实交通场景，造成事故；防御方法需平衡检测率与误报率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从端到端系统效应角度设计攻击，启发未来安全研究关注模块间误差传播。

en.tldr: Proposes a stealthy, scenario-realistic data fabrication attack that induces unsafe driving via subtle pose perturbations, with an online attack framework; experiments show high success rates and evasion of defenses.
en.motivation: Existing collaborative perception attacks lack practicality in real-world driving and are often detectable or evaluated only in manual scenarios.
en.method: Manipulates poses of existing objects in shared perception results below detection thresholds, propagates errors through downstream modules, and uses an online scenario-aware framework to optimize attack strategies dynamically.
en.result: On OPV2V and V2X-Real, the attack achieves over 90% success in inducing detection errors and triggers safety-critical behaviors (e.g., hard braking) in up to 50% of scenarios, while evading state-of-the-art defenses; a proposed mitigation detects anomalies in localized safety-critical regions with 80% detection rate vs. 11% for existing methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand novel stealthy attacks on collaborative perception and their safety implications, along with potential defense directions.

idea_spark.idea: Use adversarial pose perturbations with online optimization to induce safety-critical behaviors while evading detection.
idea_spark.risk: Attack could be deployed in real traffic, causing accidents; defenses must balance detection rate and false positives.
idea_spark.inspiration: Designing attacks from an end-to-end system effect perspective inspires future security research on inter-module error propagation.

papers.title: Architectural Obsolescence of Unhardened Agentic-AI Runtimes
papers.summary: An agentic-AI runtime issues tool calls, sends messages, and actuates devices on behalf of an LLM. Catching the four ways an action can diverge from its audit record -- F1 gate-bypass, F2 audit-forgery, silent host failure, F4 wrong-target, -- is a load-bearing safety property of any such runtime. We show that upstream OpenClaw, the most engineered single-user agentic-AI gateway in public release, catches none of them: recall is 0.000 on every cell of every confusion matrix, on a 1600-sample template baseline through OpenClaw's actual production command-line interface (CLI) and on a ten-LLM cross-model generalisation run. Detecting F1--F4 requires seven specific runtime structures absent from OpenClaw's source tree: a biconditional checker, a hash-chained audit log, an extension admission gate, a two-layer egress guard, a Bell-LaPadula classification policy, a module-signing trust root, and a bootstrap seal. enclawed-oss -- an MIT-licensed drop-in fork that ships all seven -- reaches $P = R = F_1 =$ accuracy $= 1.000$ on the same input. The gap is structural, not parametric: a six-line append-only widening of enclawed-oss's data-loss-prevention (DLP) regex catalog raises per-channel F3 detection by 14.6\% net at unchanged precision; the same edit on OpenClaw has nowhere to land. The harness deliberately exercises real Discord and Telegram channels -- plugin categories the first enclawed release deleted as unsafe -- to show F1--F4 detection extends to those previously-unsafe extensions. With architectural superiority for security and feature parity for extensions, we argue that unhardened agentic-AI runtimes are architecturally obsolete: a strictly better alternative exists, is adoptable today, and the gap requires re-architecture rather than configuration. We invite reviewers to apply the harness to any candidate runtime.

ai.tldr: 本文证明当前最成熟的单用户智能体AI网关OpenClaw在检测四种动作偏离审计记录（F1-F4）时召回率为0，而通过添加七种必要运行时结构的分支enclawed-oss实现了完美检测，表明未加固的智能体AI运行时在架构上已经过时。
ai.motivation: 智能体AI运行时代表LLM执行工具调用、发送消息和驱动设备，确保动作与审计记录一致是安全关键属性，但现有系统OpenClaw无法检测任何偏离模式。
ai.method: 设计1600样本模板基线测试，通过OpenClaw实际生产CLI和十种LLM跨模型泛化运行，评估F1-F4检测能力；然后实现包含七种结构（双条件检查器、哈希链审计日志等）的分支enclawed-oss并对比。
ai.result: OpenClaw在所有混淆矩阵单元上召回率为0.000，而enclawed-oss达到精确率、召回率、F1和准确率均为1.000；对enclawed-oss的DLP正则表达式进行六行扩展，使每通道F3检测净提升14.6%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解智能体AI运行时安全审计的架构缺陷，以及如何通过重新设计而非配置来彻底解决。

idea_spark.idea: 将enclawed-oss的架构模式抽象为安全运行时模板，用于自动加固现有智能体AI网关。
idea_spark.risk: 新结构可能引入性能开销，且需要与现有扩展生态兼容。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从操作系统安全（如Bell-LaPadula模型）和区块链（哈希链）中借鉴成熟机制，应用于AI运行时审计。

idea_spark.risk: 新结构可能引入性能开销，且需要与现有扩展生态兼容。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从操作系统安全（如Bell-LaPadula模型）和区块链（哈希链）中借鉴成熟机制，应用于AI运行时审计。

en.tldr: This paper demonstrates that OpenClaw, the most engineered single-user agentic-AI gateway, has 0% recall in detecting four action-audit divergence modes (F1-F4), while a fork enclawed-oss with seven required runtime structures achieves perfect detection, proving unhardened agentic-AI runtimes are architecturally obsolete.
en.motivation: Agentic-AI runtimes execute tool calls, messages, and device actuations on behalf of LLMs; ensuring actions match audit records is a critical safety property, yet current system OpenClaw fails to detect any divergence pattern.
en.method: A 1600-sample template baseline is tested through OpenClaw's production CLI and a ten-LLM cross-model generalization run to evaluate F1-F4 detection; then a fork enclawed-oss implementing seven structures (biconditional checker, hash-chained audit log, etc.) is compared.
en.result: OpenClaw achieves 0.000 recall on all confusion matrix cells, while enclawed-oss reaches precision, recall, F1, and accuracy all 1.000; a six-line DLP regex expansion in enclawed-oss improves per-channel F3 detection by 14.6% net.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand architectural deficiencies in agentic-AI runtime security auditing and how re-architecture rather than configuration can fully address them.

idea_spark.idea: Abstract enclawed-oss's architectural patterns into a security runtime template for automatically hardening existing agentic-AI gateways.
idea_spark.risk: New structures may introduce performance overhead and require compatibility with existing extension ecosystems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow mature mechanisms from OS security (e.g., Bell-LaPadula model) and blockchain (hash chains) for AI runtime auditing.

papers.title: MANOJAVAM: A Scalable, Unified FPGA Accelerator for Matrix Multiplication and Singular Value Decomposition in Principal Component Analysis
papers.summary: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is widely used for dimensionality reduction in hyperspectral imaging, genomics, and neurosciences. However, it suffers from computational bottlenecks in matrix multiplication and singular value decomposition (SVD). Prior PCA hardware accelerators either target only one of these stages, rely on High Level Synthesis (HLS) that limits microarchitectural optimizations or use fixed point datapaths with limited dataset scalability. There is a need for a unified PCA accelerator that is suitable for datasets of any input dimension. Hence, the proposed work presents MANOJAVAM, a scalable PCA accelerator fabric, unifying matrix multiplication and SVD in a single architecture. MANOJAVAM(T,S) comprises an S number of TxT TPU-style systolic arrays employing block streaming for high-throughput matrix multiplication. It further integrates a highly parallel Jacobian unit implementing the Jacobi method for SVD with pipelined CORDIC based rotations. A two tier cache hierarchy and mode-aware memory policies adapts to the distinct memory access patterns of covariance matrix and rotation computation. For demonstration, MANOJAVAM(4,8) is realized on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, achieving a frequency of 200 MHz at 1.271W. MANOJAVAM(16,32) is realized on Xilinx Virtex-Ultrascale+ FPGA, achieving a frequency of 434 MHz at 16.957W. Benchmarking on real-world datasets reveals that MANOJAVAM(16,32) achieves up to a 22.75x speedup in SVD latency and a 42.14x reduction in total energy consumption compared to a high-performance NVIDIA A6000 GPU. The architecture offers a unified, scalable, and energy-efficient platform for large-scale data analytics in both high-performance and edge-computing environments.

ai.tldr: 提出MANOJAVAM，一种统一且可扩展的FPGA加速器，同时支持PCA中的矩阵乘法和奇异值分解。
ai.motivation: 现有PCA硬件加速器仅针对单一阶段，或使用高层次综合限制微架构优化，或采用定点数据路径导致数据集可扩展性差。
ai.method: 采用TPU风格的可扩展脉动阵列进行矩阵乘法，集成基于CORDIC流水线旋转的雅可比单元实现SVD，并设计两级缓存层次和模式感知内存策略。
ai.result: 在Xilinx Artix-7和Virtex-Ultrascale+ FPGA上实现，相比NVIDIA A6000 GPU，SVD延迟降低22.75倍，总能耗降低42.14倍。
ai.help_to_user: 为高维数据分析提供统一、可扩展、低功耗的硬件加速方案，适用于高计算和边缘计算场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将雅可比方法与脉动阵列结合，实现更高效的在线PCA更新。
idea_spark.risk: 雅可比方法的并行度受限于矩阵大小，大规模矩阵时可能成为瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴TPU的脉动阵列和CORDIC算法，启发设计面向数据科学的专用加速器。

idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴TPU的脉动阵列和CORDIC算法，启发设计面向数据科学的专用加速器。

en.tldr: Proposes MANOJAVAM, a scalable unified FPGA accelerator for both matrix multiplication and SVD in PCA.
en.motivation: Existing PCA hardware accelerators target only one stage, rely on HLS limiting microarchitectural optimizations, or use fixed-point datapaths with poor dataset scalability.
en.method: Employs scalable TPU-style systolic arrays for matrix multiplication, integrates a highly parallel Jacobian unit with pipelined CORDIC rotations for SVD, and uses a two-tier cache hierarchy with mode-aware memory policies.
en.result: Implemented on Xilinx Artix-7 and Virtex-Ultrascale+ FPGAs; achieves up to 22.75x speedup in SVD latency and 42.14x energy reduction vs. NVIDIA A6000 GPU.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified, scalable, energy-efficient hardware acceleration platform for large-scale data analytics in both HPC and edge environments.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining Jacobi method with systolic arrays for efficient online PCA updates.
idea_spark.risk: Parallelism of Jacobi method is limited by matrix size, potentially becoming a bottleneck for large matrices.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by TPU systolic arrays and CORDIC algorithm, motivating design of domain-specific accelerators for data science.

papers.title: Analyzing Unsolicited Internet Traffic: Measuring IoT Security Threats via Network Telescopes
papers.summary: Network telescopes serve as a critical passive monitoring tool for capturing unsolicited Internet traffic, providing insights into global scanning and reconnaissance behavior. This study analyzes a 10-day dataset during January 2025 consisting of approximately 22 million packets collected by the ORION network telescope at Merit Network. By employing privacy-preserving metadata analysis and lightweight behavioral heuristics, we identify scanning and backscatter patterns without payload inspection. Our results reveal a highly structured and centralized ecosystem, where the top 1% of source IP addresses generate over 81% of total traffic. A significant finding is the dominance of Port 23 (Telnet) and Port 2323 (Telnet Alt), which highlights the persistent nature of IoT security threats and widespread attempts to exploit weak credentials in legacy IoT devices. Furthermore, synchronized surges in packet volume and Shannon entropy indicate coordinated, multi-vector reconnaissance campaigns. These findings offer a practical framework for identifying large-scale threat activity and support cybersecurity research and education.

ai.tldr: 通过分析网络望远镜捕获的未请求互联网流量，揭示IoT安全威胁的结构化特征。
ai.motivation: 被动监测未请求流量以理解全球扫描和侦察行为，特别是针对IoT设备的威胁。
ai.method: 使用隐私保护的元数据分析和轻量级行为启发式方法，识别扫描和反向散射模式，无需检查数据包负载。
ai.result: 流量高度集中，前1%源IP产生81%以上流量；Telnet端口（23和2323）占主导，表明IoT设备弱凭证攻击持续存在；同步流量和熵值激增显示协调的多向量侦察活动。
ai.help_to_user: 提供识别大规模威胁活动的实用框架，支持网络安全研究和教育。

idea_spark.idea: 结合熵值变化与源IP聚类，可开发实时预警系统。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖元数据可能遗漏加密流量中的威胁；启发式方法可能产生误报。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用网络望远镜数据训练轻量级ML模型，自动识别新型IoT攻击模式。

en.tldr: Analyzing unsolicited Internet traffic captured by network telescopes reveals structured IoT security threats.
en.motivation: Passively monitor unsolicited traffic to understand global scanning and reconnaissance behavior, especially threats targeting IoT devices.
en.method: Privacy-preserving metadata analysis and lightweight behavioral heuristics identify scanning and backscatter patterns without payload inspection.
en.result: Traffic is highly centralized: top 1% source IPs generate over 81% of traffic; Telnet ports (23 and 2323) dominate, indicating persistent exploitation of weak IoT credentials; synchronized surges in packet volume and entropy suggest coordinated multi-vector reconnaissance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical framework for identifying large-scale threat activity, supporting cybersecurity research and education.

idea_spark.idea: Combine entropy changes with source IP clustering to develop a real-time early warning system.
idea_spark.risk: Metadata-only analysis may miss threats in encrypted traffic; heuristics could produce false positives.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use network telescope data to train lightweight ML models for automatic detection of novel IoT attack patterns.

papers.title: Conventional Commit Classification using Large Language Models and Prompt Engineering
papers.summary: Conventional commits provide a structured format for writing commit messages, which improves readability, software maintenance, and enables automation tools such as changelog generators and semantic versioning systems. Existing approaches to conventional commit classification typically rely on ML/DL models trained on large labeled datasets. In this paper, we investigated a training-free alternative by leveraging large language models (LLMs) through prompt engineering. Rather than building a task-specific classifier, we evaluate three prompting strategies, such as zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought, across three open-source LLMs of varying scale: Mistral-7B-Instruct, LLaMA-3-8B, and DeepSeek-R1-32B. Classification is performed directly on code diffs extracted from a balanced dataset of 3,200 commits mined from the InfluxDB repository, without any model fine-tuning. Our results show that few-shot prompting consistently achieves the highest accuracy, while chain-of-thought prompting does not yield additional gains for this classification task. Among the evaluated models, DeepSeek-R1-32B achieves the strongest overall performance, suggesting that model scale plays a meaningful role in conventional commit classification. These findings provide practical guidance for researchers and practitioners seeking to automate commit classification without the overhead of curating and maintaining labeled training data.

ai.tldr: 本文研究利用大语言模型和提示工程对常规提交进行分类，无需训练数据，在InfluxDB数据集上评估了三种提示策略。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖大量标注数据训练模型，成本高且维护困难，本文探索无需训练的替代方案。

ai.result: 少样本提示准确率最高，思维链无额外收益；DeepSeek-R1-32B表现最佳，模型规模影响显著。
ai.help_to_user: 为开发者提供无需标注数据即可自动分类提交消息的实用指导。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在软件工程中利用大语言模型减少人工标注，推动自动化工具链发展。

en.tldr: This paper investigates training-free conventional commit classification using LLMs and prompt engineering, evaluating three strategies on InfluxDB commits.
en.motivation: Existing ML/DL approaches require large labeled datasets, which are costly and hard to maintain; this work explores a training-free alternative.
en.method: Three open-source LLMs (Mistral-7B-Instruct, LLaMA-3-8B, DeepSeek-R1-32B) are used with zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompting to classify code diffs directly.

en.help_to_user: Provides practical guidance for automating commit classification without labeled training data.

idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires leveraging LLMs in software engineering to reduce manual labeling and advance automation toolchains.

papers.title: CyberAId: AI-Driven Cybersecurity for Financial Service Providers
papers.summary: European financial institutions face mounting regulatory pressure while their security operations centres remain constrained not by data or staffing but by reasoning capacity: enterprise SIEMs cover only a fraction of MITRE ATT&CK techniques, two thirds of SOC teams cannot keep pace with alert volumes, and the majority of breaches are preceded by alerts that are generated but never investigated. Frontier large language models now achieve state-of-the-art results on isolated cybersecurity tasks (one-day vulnerability exploitation, code-level patching, intrusion detection) yet no narrow win constitutes a platform that can compose across functions, persist multi-tenant state, map findings to regulatory regimes and survive an audit. This position paper argues that the right unit of construction is a hybrid multi-agent system in which specialised LLM subagents reason over classical SIEM/XDR telemetry rather than replacing it, share accumulated agent state across institutions through privacy-preserving federation, and can connect to complementary capability packs such as quantum-based authentication, digital twins for adversarial validation, and eBPF-based kernel telemetry. We present CyberAId, a model-agnostic, on-premise-deployable platform in which a Main Agent coordination layer, a Reporting capability, and specialist subagents operate within a shared runtime under bounded human-in-the-loop autonomy, organised around four falsifiable design principles, and aligned with relevant regulations. CyberAId will be validated at four representative financial use cases (client impersonation, anti-money-laundering for payment service providers, retail-banking incident response, and high-frequency-trading resilience) and propose skill-based agent adaptation as the most promising research direction for turning each deployment into a contribution to a continuously refined collective defence.

ai.tldr: 提出一个名为CyberAId的混合多智能体系统，利用专用LLM子代理增强传统SIEM/XDR，通过隐私保护联邦共享状态，并符合金融监管要求。
ai.motivation: 欧洲金融机构面临监管压力，安全运营中心受限于推理能力而非数据或人员，现有SIEM覆盖不足，大量告警未被调查。
ai.method: 构建模型无关、可本地部署的平台，包含主代理协调层、报告能力和专用子代理，在有限人类监督下运行，基于四个可证伪设计原则。
ai.result: 将在四个金融用例（客户冒充、反洗钱、零售银行事件响应、高频交易韧性）中验证，并提出基于技能的代理适应作为持续集体防御的研究方向。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助金融安全团队整合AI能力，提升告警调查效率，满足监管审计要求。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将量子认证、数字孪生和eBPF内核遥测作为可插拔能力包，增强系统韧性。
idea_spark.risk: 多代理协调复杂性可能导致状态不一致；联邦学习可能面临数据异构和通信开销挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受金融行业高监管要求和告警疲劳现状启发，将AI作为推理增强层而非替代现有系统。

idea_spark.risk: Multi-agent coordination complexity may cause state inconsistency; federated learning may face data heterogeneity and communication overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by high regulatory demands and alert fatigue in finance, using AI as a reasoning augmentation layer rather than replacing existing systems.

papers.title: A Language for Describing Agentic LLM Contexts
papers.summary: Large language models are increasingly used within larger systems ("LLM agents"). These make a sequence of LLM calls, each call providing the LLM with a combination of instructions, observations, and interaction history. The design of the encoded information and its structure play a central role in the quality of the resulting system, leading to efforts spent on context engineering. It is therefore critical to communicate the composition of the LLM context in a system, and how it evolves over time. Yet, no standard exists for doing so: context construction is typically conveyed through informal prose, ad hoc diagrams, or direct inspection of code, none of which precisely capture how a prompt evolves across interaction steps or how two context representation strategies differ. To remedy this, we introduce the Agentic Context Description Language (ACDL), a language for specifying the structure and dynamics of LLM input contexts in a precise, readable, and standard manner, along with visualizations. ACDL provides constructs for specifying context aspects such as role message sequences, dynamic content, time-indexed references, and conditional or iterative structure, capturing the full architecture of a prompt independently of any particular implementation. ACDL diagrams can be hand drawn on a whiteboard, or written in formal language which can then be rendered. We describe the language, demonstrate it by documenting several existing systems and their variants, and encourage the community to adopt it for describing LLM systems context, both in day-to-day communication and in papers. Tooling, examples and documentation are available at www.acdlang.org.

ai.tldr: 提出一种用于精确描述LLM代理上下文的语言ACDL，支持结构化和动态表示。
ai.motivation: 当前缺乏标准方式描述LLM上下文的组成和演化，导致沟通和比较困难。
ai.method: 设计ACDL语言，包含角色消息序列、动态内容、时间索引引用、条件/迭代结构等构造，并配套可视化。
ai.result: ACDL可精确、可读、标准化地描述多种现有系统的上下文架构，支持白板手绘和正式渲染。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师清晰记录、比较和复用LLM代理系统的上下文设计。

idea_spark.idea: 开发自动从代码或日志生成ACDL描述的工具，降低使用门槛。
idea_spark.risk: 社区采纳需要时间，可能面临现有非正式描述习惯的惯性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴了软件工程中UML等标准描述语言的成功经验。

en.tldr: Proposes ACDL, a language for precisely describing the structure and dynamics of LLM agent contexts.
en.motivation: No standard exists for communicating LLM context composition and evolution, hindering clarity and comparison.
en.method: Designs ACDL with constructs for role message sequences, dynamic content, time-indexed references, conditional/iterative structures, and visualizations.
en.result: ACDL enables precise, readable, standardized documentation of context architectures across multiple existing systems, supporting both whiteboard sketches and formal rendering.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and engineers clearly document, compare, and reuse context designs in LLM agent systems.

idea_spark.idea: Develop tools to automatically generate ACDL descriptions from code or logs to lower adoption barriers.
idea_spark.risk: Community adoption may be slow due to inertia of informal description habits.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from successful standard description languages like UML in software engineering.

papers.title: In-Orbit Optical SSA Using Proliferated LEO Satellites for Space Traffic Monitoring: An Analytical Framework
papers.summary: The increase in space activities has increased the risks of space debris generation, affecting space safety and sustainability. Traditional space situational awareness (SSA) relies on single star trackers and ground-based tracking facilities. There is limited discussion on the use of in-orbit optical sensors on low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations for SSA, despite their importance for efficient space traffic management systems. In this paper, we aim to address this important challenge. We first present a new analytical system model for utilizing LEO satellite constellations for in-orbit SSA. We then develop a method to evaluate and analyze such a system. We also propose a Poisson expected revisit period algorithm and introduce the period of equivalent orbital distributions to reveal the relationship between revisit period and geometric variables, with insightful results based on real-world and custom satellite constellations. Experiments on real-world constellation show that the representative Poisson expected revisit period ranges from 0.4 days to 5.7 days for targets whose apogee altitude ranges from 552 km to 650 km, while requiring a per-case computation time of 0.4 s to 4.8 s. Our work can inform the future design of in-orbit and onboard computing systems for SSA, such as space object detection and space traffic monitoring systems.

ai.tldr: 提出利用低轨卫星星座进行在轨光学空间态势感知的解析框架，并开发了评估方法。
ai.motivation: 传统空间态势感知依赖单星跟踪器和地面设施，缺乏对低轨星座在轨光学传感器的系统讨论。
ai.method: 建立解析系统模型，提出泊松期望重访周期算法和等效轨道分布周期概念。
ai.result: 真实星座实验中，目标重访周期为0.4至5.7天，单次计算时间0.4至4.8秒。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解低轨星座在轨光学传感器的性能边界，指导空间目标检测与交通监测系统设计。

idea_spark.idea: 结合边缘计算与星间链路，实现分布式在轨数据处理，降低地面依赖。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用等效轨道分布简化几何变量关系，启发其他星座性能建模方法。
