en.tldr: Proposes an analytical framework for in-orbit optical SSA using LEO satellite constellations and develops evaluation methods.
en.motivation: Traditional SSA relies on single star trackers and ground facilities, lacking systematic discussion on in-orbit optical sensors on LEO constellations.
en.method: Establishes an analytical system model, proposes a Poisson expected revisit period algorithm and the concept of period of equivalent orbital distributions.
en.result: In real-world constellation experiments, target revisit periods range from 0.4 to 5.7 days, with per-case computation time of 0.4 to 4.8 seconds.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the performance bounds of in-orbit optical sensors on LEO constellations, guiding the design of space object detection and traffic monitoring systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine edge computing with inter-satellite links to enable distributed on-orbit data processing, reducing ground dependency.
idea_spark.risk: Larger constellations may increase computational complexity, requiring real-time algorithm optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using equivalent orbital distributions to simplify geometric variable relationships inspires modeling approaches for other constellation performance.

papers.title: 6G Needs Agents: Toward Agentic AI-Native Networks for Autonomous Intelligence
papers.summary: Sixth-generation (6G) networks are increasingly envisioned as AI-native infrastructures integrating communication, sensing, and computing into a unified fabric. However, existing approaches remain largely optimization-centric, relying on closed-loop control with limited reasoning capability. In this paper, we argue for a paradigm shift toward Agentic AI-Native 6G, in which Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents operate as bounded, policy-governed reasoning entities within a semantic control plane layered above deterministic 3GPP infrastructure. We propose a four-layer architecture that integrates deterministic network infrastructure, semantic abstraction of intent and context, hierarchical reasoning, and a distributed multi-agent fabric spanning device, edge, and core domains. To assess feasibility, we develop a proof-of-concept agentic reasoning and orchestration framework and conduct an extensive empirical study using a domain-specific 6G benchmark under realistic deployment constraints. Our results reveal a fundamental tradeoff between reasoning capability and system efficiency, showing that no single model simultaneously satisfies latency, throughput, and accuracy requirements. Instead, heterogeneous deployment of LLM agents across the device--edge--core continuum is necessary to balance these constraints. We further demonstrate that quantization introduces non-uniform effects across models, reinforcing the need for system-level optimization rather than model-level compression alone. These findings establish agentic intelligence as a viable architectural direction for 6G and highlight key challenges in achieving scalable, trustworthy, and self-reasoning networks. All experimental results and evaluation scripts are publicly available to support reproducibility.

ai.tldr: 提出面向6G的智能体AI原生网络架构，通过LLM代理实现自主推理，并验证了异构部署的必要性。
ai.motivation: 现有6G网络依赖优化闭环控制，推理能力有限，无法满足自主智能需求。
ai.method: 设计四层架构（确定性基础设施、语义抽象、分层推理、分布式多智能体），并构建概念验证框架进行实证研究。
ai.result: 发现推理能力与系统效率存在根本权衡，单一模型无法同时满足延迟、吞吐量和精度要求；量化对不同模型影响不均。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解6G网络中引入LLM代理的架构设计思路与关键权衡。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在设备-边缘-核心连续体上动态调度不同量化级别的LLM代理以优化整体性能。
idea_spark.risk: 多代理协调可能引入额外通信开销和信任问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于网络领域从优化驱动向智能体驱动的范式转变，可应用于物联网自治系统。

zh.tldr: 提出面向6G的智能体AI原生网络架构，通过LLM代理实现自主推理，并验证了异构部署的必要性。
zh.motivation: 现有6G网络依赖优化闭环控制，推理能力有限，无法满足自主智能需求。
zh.method: 设计四层架构（确定性基础设施、语义抽象、分层推理、分布式多智能体），并构建概念验证框架进行实证研究。
zh.result: 发现推理能力与系统效率存在根本权衡，单一模型无法同时满足延迟、吞吐量和精度要求；量化对不同模型影响不均。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解6G网络中引入LLM代理的架构设计思路与关键权衡。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在设备-边缘-核心连续体上动态调度不同量化级别的LLM代理以优化整体性能。
idea_spark.risk: 多代理协调可能引入额外通信开销和信任问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于网络领域从优化驱动向智能体驱动的范式转变，可应用于物联网自治系统。

en.tldr: Proposes an agentic AI-native architecture for 6G using LLM-based agents for autonomous reasoning, and empirically demonstrates the need for heterogeneous deployment.
en.motivation: Existing 6G approaches rely on optimization-centric closed-loop control with limited reasoning, falling short of autonomous intelligence requirements.
en.method: Designs a four-layer architecture (deterministic infrastructure, semantic abstraction, hierarchical reasoning, distributed multi-agent fabric) and builds a proof-of-concept framework for empirical study.
en.result: Reveals a fundamental tradeoff between reasoning capability and system efficiency; no single model meets latency, throughput, and accuracy simultaneously; quantization effects are non-uniform across models.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the architectural design and key tradeoffs of integrating LLM agents into 6G networks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore dynamic scheduling of LLM agents with different quantization levels across the device-edge-core continuum to optimize overall performance.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-agent coordination may introduce additional communication overhead and trust issues.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the paradigm shift from optimization-driven to agent-driven networks, applicable to IoT autonomous systems.

papers.title: Throughput Analysis and On-Board Buffer Sizing for Hybrid RF and Optical LEO Satellites
papers.summary: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks are increasingly adopting laser (Free Space Optics, FSO) links to provide high-capacity communications. Although laser inter-satellite links offer high throughput and low latency, RF up- and downlinks remain necessary to maintain connectivity during optical outages caused by adverse atmospheric conditions. In such hybrid link scenarios, satellite buffer design remains a key challenge, since up- and downlink traffic must be buffered and forwarded among satellite nodes. The hybrid RF/FSO scenario requires careful transmission scheduling, especially at envisioned optical transmission rates of 100Gb/s and beyond, making buffer sizing critical under strict onboard energy and weight constraints. Thus, this paper analyzes throughput performance and buffer sizing in hybrid RF/laser satellite networks with finite buffer capacity, interference-aware scheduling, and weather-dependent laser link outage probabilities. Numerical results indicate that laser communications bring significant performance gains. Instead of increasing the transmission power of the satellite to maximize the throughput, we can select a suitable transmission scheduling priority to achieve a maximum throughput, while minimizing the buffer requirement, and lowering packet loss probability under realistic operational conditions and constraints.

ai.tldr: 分析了混合射频/激光低轨卫星网络中的吞吐量和星上缓存大小，提出通过选择合适调度优先级来最大化吞吐量并最小化缓存需求。
ai.motivation: 混合RF/FSO链路中，星上缓存设计面临能量和重量约束，需要平衡吞吐量、缓存大小和丢包率。
ai.method: 建立有限缓存、干扰感知调度和天气相关激光链路中断概率的模型，进行数值分析。
ai.result: 激光通信带来显著性能提升；选择合适调度优先级可在不增加发射功率下最大化吞吐量，同时降低缓存需求和丢包率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解混合卫星网络中缓存与调度策略的权衡，指导实际星上缓存设计。

idea_spark.idea: 利用机器学习预测天气导致的激光链路中断，动态调整调度优先级和缓存分配。
idea_spark.risk: 预测误差可能导致缓存溢出或链路利用率下降；实时计算开销需考虑星上资源限制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将卫星网络中的缓存调度问题类比于数据中心网络中的流量工程，借鉴其自适应算法。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解混合卫星网络中缓存与调度策略的权衡，指导实际星上缓存设计。

idea_spark.risk: 预测误差可能导致缓存溢出或链路利用率下降；实时计算开销需考虑星上资源限制。

en.tldr: Analyzes throughput and on-board buffer sizing in hybrid RF/laser LEO satellite networks, showing that selecting appropriate scheduling priority maximizes throughput while minimizing buffer requirements.
en.motivation: On-board buffer design faces energy and weight constraints in hybrid RF/FSO links, requiring trade-offs among throughput, buffer size, and packet loss.
en.method: Models finite buffer capacity, interference-aware scheduling, and weather-dependent laser link outage probabilities, then performs numerical analysis.
en.result: Laser communications yield significant performance gains; proper scheduling priority maximizes throughput without increasing transmit power, while reducing buffer needs and packet loss.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into buffer and scheduling trade-offs in hybrid satellite networks, guiding practical on-board buffer design.

idea_spark.idea: Use machine learning to predict weather-induced laser link outages and dynamically adjust scheduling priority and buffer allocation.
idea_spark.risk: Prediction errors may cause buffer overflow or underutilization; real-time computation overhead must respect on-board resource constraints.
idea_spark.inspiration: Analogize satellite buffer scheduling to traffic engineering in data center networks and borrow adaptive algorithms from that domain.

papers.title: When Alignment Isn't Enough: Response-Path Attacks on LLM Agents
papers.summary: Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) agent architectures let users route LLM traffic through third-party relays, creating a critical integrity gap: a malicious relay can modify an aligned LLM response after generation but before agent execution. We formalize this post-alignment tampering threat and show that, without end-to-end integrity, the relay can observe, suppress, or replace downstream messages, making even perfectly aligned LLMs ineffective against such attacks. We instantiate this threat as the Relay Tampering Attack (RTA), which performs multi-round strategic rewriting, minimal security-critical edits, and stealth restoration by resubmitting tampered outputs to the upstream LLM. Across AgentDojo and ASB with six LLMs, RTA achieves up to 99.1% attack success, outperforming prompt-injection baselines with modest overhead. Case studies on OpenClaw and Claude Code demonstrate real-world feasibility, and evaluations of four defenses show that none fully prevent RTA. Finally, we propose a time-based detection defense that mitigates RTA while preserving agent utility.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种针对LLM代理的响应路径攻击（RTA），在BYOK架构中，恶意中继可在对齐后篡改响应，绕过对齐机制。
ai.motivation: BYOK架构中，用户通过第三方中继路由LLM流量，但中继可能篡改已对齐的响应，导致对齐失效。现有研究未充分关注此完整性缺口。
ai.method: 形式化后对齐篡改威胁，设计RTA攻击：多轮策略重写、最小安全关键编辑、通过重新提交篡改输出至上游LLM实现隐蔽恢复。在AgentDojo和ASB上测试六种LLM。
ai.result: RTA攻击成功率最高达99.1%，优于提示注入基线，且开销适中。真实案例（OpenClaw、Claude Code）验证可行性。四种防御均无法完全阻止RTA。提出基于时间的检测防御可缓解攻击。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解BYOK架构中LLM代理面临的新型安全威胁，并提供一种基于时间的检测防御思路。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一种轻量级、端到端的完整性验证机制，例如基于区块链的响应哈希链，确保从生成到执行的每一步不可篡改。
idea_spark.risk: 时间检测防御可能被更高级的时序攻击绕过，且增加延迟可能影响用户体验。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“对齐后篡改”角度出发，启发思考AI安全中“完整性”与“对齐”的互补关系，以及防御设计需覆盖全链路。

en.tldr: Proposes Response-Path Attacks (RTA) on LLM agents, where a malicious relay in BYOK architecture can tamper with aligned responses post-generation, bypassing alignment.
en.motivation: In BYOK architecture, users route LLM traffic through third-party relays, but relays can tamper with aligned responses, creating an integrity gap not fully addressed by prior work.
en.method: Formalizes post-alignment tampering threat and designs RTA: multi-round strategic rewriting, minimal security-critical edits, and stealth restoration via resubmission to upstream LLM. Tested on AgentDojo and ASB with six LLMs.
en.result: RTA achieves up to 99.1% attack success, outperforming prompt-injection baselines with modest overhead. Real-world feasibility shown via OpenClaw and Claude Code. None of four defenses fully prevent RTA. Proposes time-based detection defense.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a novel security threat to LLM agents in BYOK architectures and offers a time-based detection defense approach.

idea_spark.idea: Design a lightweight end-to-end integrity verification mechanism, e.g., a blockchain-based response hash chain, ensuring tamper-proof from generation to execution.
idea_spark.risk: Time-based detection may be bypassed by advanced timing attacks, and added latency could degrade user experience.
idea_spark.inspiration: The 'post-alignment tampering' perspective inspires thinking about the complementary roles of integrity and alignment in AI security, and the need for full-chain defense.

papers.title: Write-Domain Separation and Non-Custodial Enforcement: A Structural Impossibility in Account-Based Ledgers, with a Commitment-Based Construction
papers.summary: Account-based ledgers -- standard externally-owned accounts (EOAs), ERC-4337 smart accounts, post-Pectra EIP-7702 delegated EOAs -- place the holder of the controlling key at the apex of asset authorization. We ask a structural question about ledger access control: under this authorization model, can a protocol enforce the future disposition of an asset without taking custody and without requiring the owner's cooperation at enforcement time? We formalize the target as Non-Custodial Enforced Encumbrance (NCEE), a four-property specification covering self-custody, transition restriction, irrevocability, and permissionless enforcement.   We define the Key Sovereignty Axiom (KS) and prove that any ledger satisfying KS cannot realize NCEE; standard EOAs, ERC-4337 smart accounts, and EIP-7702 delegated EOAs satisfy KS for their standard asset paths. We define Asset-Authorization Coupling (AAC) and prove it necessary for NCEE in the transfer-dichotomous asset setting. To witness the positive side, we introduce the envelope, a primitive for commitment-based private-state ledgers that binds a note, a condition tree, and a redistribution intent to protocol-maintained marker sets, separating ordinary spend nullifiers from a new encumbrance-namespace nullifier derived from note randomness rather than the owner key. We prove the envelope realizes NCEE under stated cryptographic assumptions and a deployment assumption that the marker-set registry is immutable; three concrete deployment templates are given.   We define games for encumbrance integrity, settlement security, key-compromise resilience, and encumbrance indistinguishability. A reference implementation in Noir and UltraHonk supports the empirical claims, with gas measurements, recursive aggregation benchmarks, and a practical-economics analysis.

ai.tldr: 本文证明在账户式账本中，由于密钥主权公理的存在，无法同时实现非托管强制执行，并提出了一种基于承诺的“信封”原语来绕过这一不可能性。
ai.motivation: 现有账户式账本（如EOA、ERC-4337智能账户、EIP-7702委托EOA）中，资产授权完全依赖于控制密钥持有者，导致无法在不托管资产且不要求所有者配合的情况下强制执行资产的未来处置。
ai.method: 形式化定义了非托管强制执行封装（NCEE）的四属性规范，提出密钥主权公理（KS）并证明其与NCEE不相容；引入资产-授权耦合（AAC）作为NCEE的必要条件；设计“信封”原语，通过承诺、条件树和再分配意图绑定到协议维护的标记集，并利用新的加密命名空间无效器实现NCEE。
ai.result: 证明了标准账户模型无法实现NCEE；给出了基于承诺的私有状态账本构造，并在Noir和UltraHonk中实现，提供了Gas测量、递归聚合基准和实用经济学分析。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解账户式账本中资产强制执行的固有局限性，并提供一种可行的非托管解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 利用承诺和条件树分离资产授权与执行，使得第三方可以在不持有密钥的情况下触发资产转移，同时保持用户自托管。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖标记集注册表不可变假设，若注册表被篡改或升级，可能破坏安全性；此外，零知识证明的实现复杂度可能带来性能瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受密码学中承诺方案和匿名凭证系统的启发，将资产授权与执行解耦，类似于盲签名或环签名中的匿名性设计。

zh.motivation: 现有账户式账本（如EOA、ERC-4337智能账户、EIP-7702委托EOA）中，资产授权完全依赖于控制密钥持有者，导致无法在不托管资产且不要求所有者配合的情况下强制执行资产的未来处置。

en.tldr: This paper proves a structural impossibility of non-custodial enforced encumbrance in account-based ledgers due to the Key Sovereignty Axiom, and proposes a commitment-based 'envelope' primitive to circumvent it.
en.motivation: Existing account-based ledgers (EOAs, ERC-4337 smart accounts, EIP-7702 delegated EOAs) place asset authorization solely under the control of the key holder, making it impossible to enforce future asset disposition without custody or owner cooperation.
en.method: Formalizes Non-Custodial Enforced Encumbrance (NCEE) with four properties, defines the Key Sovereignty Axiom (KS) and proves its incompatibility with NCEE; introduces Asset-Authorization Coupling (AAC) as necessary for NCEE; designs the 'envelope' primitive that binds a note, condition tree, and redistribution intent to protocol-maintained marker sets, using a new encumbrance-namespace nullifier derived from note randomness.
en.result: Proves that standard account models cannot realize NCEE; provides a commitment-based private-state ledger construction that realizes NCEE under stated assumptions, with a reference implementation in Noir and UltraHonk including gas measurements and recursive aggregation benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the inherent limitations of asset enforcement in account-based ledgers and offers a feasible non-custodial solution.

idea_spark.idea: Use commitments and condition trees to decouple asset authorization from execution, enabling third parties to trigger asset transfers without holding the key, while preserving user self-custody.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the immutability of the marker-set registry; tampering or upgrades could break security. Additionally, the complexity of zero-knowledge proofs may introduce performance bottlenecks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by cryptographic commitment schemes and anonymous credential systems, decoupling asset authorization from execution, similar to blind signatures or ring signatures in anonymity design.

papers.title: Canonical LST: A Protocol-Native Liquid Staking Solution for Tezos
papers.summary: Canonical LST (sTEZ) is an enshrined, protocol-native mechanism designed to mitigate the centralization risks associated with liquid staking intermediaries. Intended to complement direct staking rather than replace it, Canonical LST provides a neutral, public alternative managed directly by the Tezos protocol. It allows any tez holder to participate in aggregated staking without reliance on third-party operators. sTEZ follows an accrual-based design: all slashing events and rewards are reflected in the token's exchange rate to tez, keeping balances fungible while exposing holders to the precise economics of staking. This approach ensures that liquid staking functions as fundamental network infrastructure--with deterministic lifecycle rules, transparent on-chain data, and governance anchored in the amendment process--rather than as a discretionary commercial product. This white paper summarises the motivation for enshrining liquid staking, the core mechanics, exchange-rate model, regulatory touchpoints, risk posture, and forward-looking roadmap for Canonical LST.

ai.tldr: 提出一种协议原生的流动性质押方案sTEZ，旨在减少第三方中介的中心化风险，作为直接质押的补充。
ai.motivation: 现有流动性质押依赖第三方运营商，带来中心化风险；需要一种协议内建、中立且公开的替代方案。
ai.method: 采用应计制设计，所有罚没和奖励反映在sTEZ对tez的汇率中，保持代币可互换性并暴露精确的质押经济。
ai.result: sTEZ作为基础网络基础设施运行，具有确定性生命周期规则、透明链上数据和基于修正过程的治理。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解Tezos上协议原生流动性质押的设计原理、风险特征和治理方式。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将协议原生质押机制与自动做市商（AMM）集成，实现sTEZ的即时流动性。
idea_spark.risk: 协议原生机制可能限制创新灵活性，且汇率模型在极端市场条件下可能面临预言机依赖风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将关键金融基础设施内建到协议层，而非依赖第三方，可降低系统性风险并增强网络韧性。

idea_spark.risk: 协议原生机制可能限制创新灵活性，且汇率模型在极端市场条件下可能面临预言机依赖风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将关键金融基础设施内建到协议层，而非依赖第三方，可降低系统性风险并增强网络韧性。

en.tldr: Proposes a protocol-native liquid staking solution sTEZ to mitigate centralization risks from third-party intermediaries, complementing direct staking.
en.motivation: Existing liquid staking relies on third-party operators, introducing centralization risks; a protocol-enshrined, neutral, and public alternative is needed.
en.method: Uses an accrual-based design where all slashing events and rewards are reflected in sTEZ's exchange rate to tez, maintaining fungibility while exposing holders to staking economics.
en.result: sTEZ operates as fundamental network infrastructure with deterministic lifecycle rules, transparent on-chain data, and governance via the amendment process.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the design rationale, risk posture, and governance of protocol-native liquid staking on Tezos.

idea_spark.idea: Explore integrating protocol-native staking with automated market makers (AMMs) to provide instant liquidity for sTEZ.
idea_spark.risk: Protocol-native mechanisms may limit innovation flexibility, and the exchange rate model could face oracle dependency risks under extreme market conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embedding critical financial infrastructure at the protocol layer, rather than relying on third parties, can reduce systemic risk and enhance network resilience.
